title: 21 Together Regional Hubs
address: All around Kent What we do We know the vital support that can come from a local friend who you can just catch up with for a coffee and be on call in an emergency to help support you. That’s why we have split Kent and Medway into 5 Regional Hubs, each hub will be supported by volunteers from that area and provide a mixture of organised social gatherings, formal advisory support sessions, and access to a regional WhatsApp group of other parents and carers of children and young people with Down’s syndrome in the area. For more information and to join a hub visit our website here
price: Often free or highly subsidised events Organisation 21 Together Telephone 01622 230720 Email info@21together.org.uk Organisation 21 Together

title: 24/7 Older Person’s Abuse Helpline
address: Virtual What we do The Hourglass helpline is the only service in the UK dedicated to helping those effected by the abuse of older people. Whether that’s an older person who feels threatened or unsafe at home, or a friend of family member concerned about the wellbeing of a loved one, our helpline services team are there to support and offer advice.

title: 6-12 Months Mentoring for Women Affected by Domestic Abuse
address: Empowering Women Hub, The Mall, Adjacent Chequers Bus Lane ME15 6AT What we do A safe, supportive mentoring programme designed to help women rebuild confidence, rediscover their voice, and regain direction in life after experiencing abuse. The programme is overseen by a qualified Mental Health Specialist with recognised mentoring credentials, ensuring that support is both compassionate and professionally informed. All mentors receive structured in-house training and are DBS certified to maintain safeguarding and trust. Mentees are carefully matched with mentors based on shared values, personal needs, and goals, creating a supportive relationship that encourages healing, growth, and the rebuilding of self-belief.
price: Free Organisation Unleash Yourself with Gladness CIC Telephone 01622 943929 Organisation Unleash Yourself with Gladness CIC

title: 6 and 12 Week Football Programmes
address: Kent Wide What we do Our 6 and 12-week programs are typically funded and supported by local councils, organisations, or community projects. We facilitate mental health-focused sports training sessions with a unique approach each week, all aimed at alleviating life pressures on mental health and addressing the stigma around open and honest conversations. Supported by experienced sports and talking therapy coaches, each session focuses on a different mental health topic each week, while still providing mental health and wellbeing support in a secure and trusted environment. Taking place in Gravesend, New Ash Green, Tonbridge, Swanley, Dover, Tunbridge Wells, Herne Bay FC, Charlton Athletic, Dartford FC and Chelsea FC. To find our more or to register your place please visit https://headinthegame.co.uk/upcoming-sessions/
price: Free Organisation Head In The Game Telephone 07507 268045 Email info@headinthegame.co.uk Organisation Head In The Game

title: A listening ear for all SEND needs
address: C/O Snapdragons childrens centre, Cliffe Road, Rochester, Kent, ME2 3FF What we do We are an independent, pan disability, parent led charity who work with (not for) the local authority, health and local services to improve services for children and young people with special educational needs and/or disabilities (SEND) and their families. To ensure all our children have the best chance in life and receive support and services needed to reach their full potential, we work alongside Medway Council and Kent and Medway Integrated Care Board. We attend meetings, run training sessions and workshops to make sure parent carers’ voices and children’s needs are being heard, helping plan and develop the quality, range, and accessibility of services, creating better outcomes for families through participation and co-production. This service is available for people living in Medway. You can find more information on our website here: https://medwaypcf.org.uk/about/
price: Free Organisation Medway Parents & Carers Forum Telephone 07813123984 Email mpcf2@medwaypcf.org.uk Organisation Medway Parents & Carers Forum

title: A Touch of Gentleness
address: Many different locations throughout East Sussex and further. What we do Bringing connection, joy and peace back to the heart of society through a touch of gentleness. Offering 20 minutes therapeutic hand massages whilst listening with full presence and gentleness. We also run free training days for people who wish to volunteer with us. A Touch of  Gentleness  is a growing community of volunteers who are committed to bringing joy, connection, and peace back to  the heart of society. Volunteers are trained to give a beautiful hand massage whilst listening with deep gentleness. This service is then offered to the local community, organisations and charities. Through this service we are shaping a more compassionate, cohesive culture grounded in generosity, gentleness and human connection. To understand more about our mission , our vision and how gentleness , touch and a listening ear can reshape society, please click the links. Please visit our website to find out more. You can also find out about our public events on our website here.

title: Access to Work
address: Tonbridge Jobcentre Plus, Crown Building, Bradford Street, Tonbridge, TN9 1DU What we do Access to Work is something that someone who is about to enter work can apply for if they need extra help or support in order to start work. Support is available if they have a health condition or disability that will impact on the ability to do their job. Someone who is claiming either JSA, IS, or UC only need to be starting work of at least 1 hour per week and if you are claiming ESA you need to be starting approved Permitted Work or work over 16 hours per week. You will have to be at least 16 years of age to qualify and you have to be living with the UK (The Channel islands and the Isle of Man are excluded from this) (To access this provision you will need to be in receipt of either Jobseekers Allowance (JSA), Employment Support Allowance (ESA), Income Support (IS), or Universal Credit (UC) and attend an appointment with your local JobCentre Plus to check your eligibility.)
price: Free Organisation Tonbridge JobCentre Plus Telephone 0800 169 0190 Email tonbridge.chat@dwp.gov.uk Organisation Tonbridge JobCentre Plus

title: AccessAble
address: High Street, 18-20, Stevenage, Hertfordshire, SG1 3EJ What we do AccessAble gives disabled people, carers, and anyone who needs to know about accessibility the detailed information they need to work out if a place is going to be accessible to them. AccessAble’s Detailed Access Guides tell you all about a venue’s access - hotels and guest houses, places to eat and drink, places to visit and much more. They are 100% facts, figures and photographs. We know everyone’s accessibility needs are different, which is why having detailed, accurate information is so important. It’s why AccessAble send trained surveyors to check out every single place in person and why the information collected has all been decided by the people who use it. We have worked with Tunbridge Wells Borough Council since 2009 and we have over 80 Detailed Access Guides for the area, including Scotney Castle Garden and the Spa Valley Railway. You can view the Accessibility Guide to Tunbridge Wells here and a Detailed Access Guides to various Council buildings due to our partnership with Kent County Council here. Through our partnership with Visit Kent, we provide Detailed Access Guides to various tourist attractions across the county, such as The Historic Dockyard Chatham, Port Lympne, and Hever Castle just to name a few. Check out the Guides here .
price: Free Organisation AccessAble Telephone 01438 842710 Email hello@accessable.co.uk Organisation AccessAble

title: Active Bodies Active Minds
address: Various What we do A dementia diagnosis shouldn’t mean giving up the activities you enjoy or missing out on new experiences. Our Active Bodies, Active Minds programme helps people living with dementia stay active, connected and engaged through a range of enjoyable group activities designed to support both physical and emotional wellbeing. Whether it’s rediscovering an old hobby, trying something completely new or simply spending time with others in a supportive environment, these sessions are all about building confidence, maintaining independence and having fun. Just as importantly, they provide opportunities to make friendships, share experiences and continue living well with dementia. Activities vary throughout the year, with some groups running seasonally, ensuring there is always something new to enjoy. Current and recent activities include: Indoor Pub Activities Tea Dance Ten Pin Bowling Walking Tennis Medway Movers Daddlems, Darts & Cards Indoor Boules at Stone Lodge Farm Horse Therapy Dementia Cinema Chair Fit (Low Impact Exercise) As some activities are seasonal and availability varies by location, please contact us to find out what is currently running in your area and how to get involved. Call 01474 533990, email info@adss.org.uk or visit our website www.adss.org.uk to explore the latest activities near you.
price: Various Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: Active Communities
address: Various What we do Our new Active Communities programme is here to support your wellbeing – physically, mentally and socially. Whether you’re just getting started, returning after time away, or managing a health condition, there’s something here for you. Running weekdays during term time, the programme offers a wide variety of sessions – from energising dance and calming yoga to inclusive walking sports and more. Each activity is designed to help you move in a way that feels right for you. We know that not everyone can get to a leisure centre – so we’re bringing activities closer to home. Sessions take place in a range of welcoming local venues, including village halls, community hubs and other accessible spaces. This outreach approach helps us reach those in more rural or isolated areas, making it easier for everyone to get involved. You also have the opportunity to connect with our Referrals Specialists, who can guide you through personalised options to support your wellbeing. Explore the Programme Check out our weekly timetable to discover a range of sessions tailored to your interests . Click on the activities that spark your curiosity to find out more. Ready to get started? If you would like to attend a session, simply complete the Active Communities Enquiry Form
price: Various - some free and some have a small fee Organisation TMActive Email natalie.williams@tmactive.co.uk Organisation TMActive

title: Activities and Support Centre (Tunbridge Wells)
address: Munro House, 3, Culverden Park, Tunbridge Wells, TN4 9QT What we do Our centre offers a safe and friendly environment for those living with the effects of brain injury to spend time in a relaxed, informal setting. We pride ourselves on creating an inclusive, non-judgemental atmosphere where each person is respected as an individual and encouraged to come to terms with the changes in their life. The centre is a place to socialise with others who may share similar experiences and to participate in stimulating and varied activities. Our activities are designed to be enjoyable as well as promoting cognitive and physical development e.g. creative writing, pottery and word games. Our centre has a beautiful garden where people can relax or join in some gardening. The centre and garden are specifically designed to accommodate all physical needs; there is a lift to the first floor, accessible toilets and ramps in the garden. The centre is open Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday 10.00am – 3.00pm.
price: Free Organisation Brains Matter Telephone 01892 619001 Email info@brainsmatter.org.uk Organisation Brains Matter

title: Activities at Paddock Wood Community Centre
address: Paddock Wood Community Centre, Maidstone Rd, Paddock Wood, Tonbridge TN12 6EB What we do Paddock Wood Community Centre aims to provide groups, clubs and events for all ages. We offer so many different activities including wellbeing classes, mobility classes, support groups, disability sports, friendly coffee mornings and children’s play group as well as crafts and other activities. We also host many local small businesses offering classes and clubs although please note that these activities are all paid for and are not run by community organisations. You can find the up to date list of all of our activities on our website: https://www.paddockwoodcc.co.uk/what-s-on/ Our centre is fully wheelchair accessible.

title: Activity Groups
address: Dartford, Gravesend, Swanley What we do In Dartford (Tuesday’s), Gravesend (Monday’s) and Swanley (Wednesday’s) we offer 2hr weekly in person groups. Within the 2 hour sessions, attendees will be engaging in activities including creativity and self-expression sessions, Tai Chi, bingo, music sessions and open discussions around our wellbeing. We follow up with a 1 hour ‘wellbeing space’, where we will offer more bespoke support around mental health support needs, how we can signpost into different courses we have available and also provide refreshments such as teas, coffees and sandwiches. Our goal is to help reduce social isolation, provide peer support that promotes connectedness, inclusion and a sense of community. Our wellbeing space is open to the public and offers the opportunity for chance to sign up and discuss what our groups consist of and any anxieties they may have.
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email wellbeing@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Addiction family support Helpline
address: Oakley Hall, 8 Castle Street, High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, HP13 6RF What we do Call us on 0300 888 3853 to talk with someone who understands the challenges caused by a loved one’s harmful use of alcohol, drugs or gambling.​ We listen with empathy and without judgment to understand your unique challenges. We provide relevant, specific, and targeted information, to improve your knowledge. We discuss your social support, and how you react, respond, and cope. We identify any additional needs and refer you, within our organisation or externally, for further support. Although we recommend calling the helpline, we also offer email support – email Addiction family support here
price: Free Organisation Addiction Family Support Telephone 0300 888 3853 Organisation Addiction Family Support

title: ADSS Beacon Day Centre
address: Safeharbour Memory Wellbeing Centre, Coldharbour Road, Northfleet, Kent, DA11 8AE What we do The Beacon provides a warm and welcoming day support environment for people living with dementia, offering them: A positive and supportive atmosphere Friendly and supportive staff trained in dementia support Stimulating, structured and planned activities to support cognition Person-led care Excellent staff to client ratios 2 course lunch When : Opening Hours: Monday – Friday 10 am – 3 pm Transport available Whether it is to provide a break for family and carers or to enable people living with dementia to have fun, company and stimulation, day support offers a specialist support service based on person-centred care. All our staff are trained in dementia support and offer the caring, friendly support that is so welcomed by our members. We provide a varied range of activities from reminiscence sessions to gentle physical exercises, from art and craft work to music and singing; we have ‘themed’ days and activities, based on events from the past or seasonal topics. Activities are based on cognitive stimulation therapy and are adapted to individuals skills and interests. No one is left out and if people prefer not to join in particular activities we provide smaller group sessions for people to enjoy. Escorted transport is available if required but people can also make their own way in if they prefer. A freshly prepared 2 course lunch is provided and any special dietary needs are catered for. Prices are available upon request. If you would like more information please contact us on 01474 533990, visit www.adss.org.uk or email info@adss.org.uk
price: Prices available on request Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: ADSS Capstone Day Centre
address: Park House, 92-95 Hopewell Dr, Chatham ME5 7PY What we do Capstone Dementia Centre provides specialist dementia day support for people at different stages of their dementia journey, offering: A welcoming and supportive environment Friendly and experienced staff trained in dementia support Meaningful activities designed to support wellbeing and independence Person-centred care and support Opportunities for friendship and social connection Specialist cognitive stimulation through our Mind & Memory Club Freshly prepared lunch Opening Hours: Monday – Friday, 10am – 3pm Escorted transport available for Day Centre attendees (subject to availability) Whether you are looking to maintain your independence following a diagnosis or require more specialist support as your needs change, Capstone offers a range of services tailored to your individual circumstances. Our Mind & Memory Club helps people in the earlier stages of dementia stay active, independent and socially connected through evidence-based cognitive stimulation activities. For those requiring more specialist support, our Day Centre provides person-centred care, companionship and meaningful activities in a safe and welcoming environment. We understand that attending a new service can feel daunting, which is why we offer an initial visit to help you meet the team, explore the centre and find the service that best meets your needs. Escorted transport is available for Day Centre attendees if required, although people attending the Mind & Memory Club make their own travel arrangements. Prices are available upon request. If you would like more information, please contact us on 01474 533990, visit our website www.adss.org.uk or email info@adss.org.uk .
price: Prices available on request Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: ADSS Carers Learning Programme
address: Various What we do People do not expect to be carers, but a diagnosis of dementia can change you from a partner, daughter or son to a carer, often with little understanding of how this will affect you and the person living with dementia. The Carers Learning Programme is designed to help support you and the person living with the diagnosis. The programme runs over 4 consecutive weeks, with 3-hour sessions each week, which are facilitated at different times throughout the year. Each session has set themes, which are interactive, informative and engaging. We will explain about the different types of dementia and how it can impact on the way some people may behave (and the reasons for this). How to manage if a person starts to lose their communication skills or forget words and offer you different techniques and skills to help you to manage and much more. We also provide you with practical resource materials, fact sheets and information from guest speakers where appropriate, and of course the opportunity to share your knowledge and experience with other carers. Your role as carer has the potential to be stressful and we aim to help alleviate that and support you as you journey on your dementia adventure. Whether you are caring for someone who has recently developed dementia or have been caring for someone for many years, this programme can help. Please contact us on 01474 533990 or email info@adss.org.uk to register your interest.
organisation: Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990

title: ADSS Creative Arts
address: Gravesend, Dartford and Walderslade What we do Our Creative Arts sessions provide people living with dementia and their carers with the opportunity to relax, get creative and enjoy time together in a friendly and supportive environment. From painting and drawing to seasonal crafts and hands-on projects, these sessions are all about having fun, expressing yourself and trying something new. There is no right or wrong way to take part and no artistic experience is needed. Creative activities can help stimulate the mind, encourage conversation and provide a sense of achievement. Just as importantly, they offer opportunities to make new friendships, share experiences and enjoy being part of a welcoming group. Whether you’re discovering a new hobby or rekindling a lifelong passion, everyone is welcome. If you would like more information, please contact us on 01474 533990, visit our website www.adss.org.uk or email info@adss.org.uk .
price: Donations welcome Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: ADSS Peer Support Groups
address: Swanley  Gravesend  Dartford  Medway  Rochester  Lordswood What we do Our Peer Support Groups offer people in the early stages of Dementia the opportunity to share experiences and talk to others in a similar situation. These groups offer: Practical and emotional support in a confidential environment Information and advice tailored to individual needs Signposting and advice on other support available The opportunity to share experiences Guest speakers Peer Groups take place in: Swanley Gravesend Dartford Medway Rochester Lordswood If you are interested in attending one of these groups please contact us and we will arrange to visit you and see how the group can meet your needs. All discussions within the group are confidential so Carers do not attend though they are welcome to meet up, in another space, where they can talk and share their own experiences with the other Carers. To find out more about location and times please visit our website, www.adss.org.uk. This is a free service however donations are welcomed to sustain the group. If you would like to join one of our Peer Support Groups please contact us on 01474 533990 or email info@adss.org.uk .
price: Donations welcome Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: Autistic Adult Support Group
address: Virtual What we do Our Autistic adult online support groups are for Autistic adults (or those that identify as Autistic) who are 18 years plus. The group provides individuals with the opportunity to meet others who share similar difficulties, share ideas, advice and problems, talk to others about the issues they face on a daily basis with others who understand. Unfortunately we are unable to offer this group to people who have support through the Learning Disability team within Social Services or deemed to have a Learning Disability (please be aware that this isn’t the same as an education need)For more information or to join our support group, please contact Emilymay Miles.
price: Free Organisation Kent Autistic Trust Email Emilymay@kentautistic.com Organisation Kent Autistic Trust

title: Adults Creative Contemporary
address: Jasmin Vardimon Company, JV H.O.M.E, Creative Enterprise Quarter, Javelin Way, Ashford, Kent, TN24 8FN What we do Join Jasmin Vardimon Company for Adult Creative Contemporary Classes. title: Adviceline (& generalist advice services)
address: Registered address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, TONBRIDGE, Kent, TN9 1BG.     (But anyone from Sevenoaks, Swanley, Dartford Gravesham, Tonbridge & Tunbridge Wells can call our Adviceline). What we do We can all face problems that seem complicated or intimidating. At Citizens Advice in North & West Kent (CANWK), we believe no one should have to face these problems without good quality, independent advice. Our goal is to help everyone find a way forward – no matter who you are, or what problems you face. Our freephone Adviceline telephone service is the quickest and easiest way to access our free, confidential, impartial and independent advice. We provide general advice on a huge range of topics, as well as specialist advice on areas including housing, immigration, energy and benefits rights.

title: Disability Assist Advocacy
address: Unit 9 & 10 Nimbus Enterprise Park, Disability Assist c/o R & B Star, Liphook Way, Maidstone, ME16 0LQ What we do When decisions are being made about our health and social welfare, it can be hard if we don’t feel listened to. Advocacy is where we support individuals to be heard and to make sure that your thoughts, wishes and feelings are communicated to the services and organisations that support you. Disability Assist’s advocates take time to understand the situation, explore the options and help you decide what you want to do and how you want to do it.

title: Advocacy
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Our advocacy service is designed to support you to express your wishes. This might be a simple situation, perhaps to challenge a utility bill, or may be far more complex; perhaps you are being made unhappy by those around you. Please get in touch if you or someone you know needs an Advocate. The support of someone on your side can make all the difference. Our advocates are not here to judge you or tell you what you should do, they are here to listen to your wishes and ensure others do too. Whether you are calling for yourself or on behalf of someone you know, you will need to complete a referral form and register with the service. Please ensure you have the persons permission before making a referral for somebody else.

title: Affordable Counselling
address: Dartford, Medway What we do The Service The Affordable Service at North Kent Mind is delivered by trainee counsellors and newly qualified who volunteer to take on this work. We currently have 36 counsellors, providing a service to around 110 clients at any one time. The service is available to clients by arrangement at various times from our Dartford and Chtham offices. Affordable Counselling and Therapy is available to anyone in the area, as long as they are aged 18 or over, and prepared to meet the fee. What kind of counselling is available? Our counsellors, from all genders and ages come from different walks of life and different cultural backgrounds. Our counsellors are trained in different modalities with a main focus on person centred and we strive to match the needs of the client with the skills and approach of the particular counsellor. How do I refer? Self referrals must be made by phone or using our online referral form. We will then contact you to discuss your needs, to explain the charging system, and to tell you of how long you can expect to wait before counselling will start. As this is a popular service, there is a waiting list. Sometimes we may suggest another service at North Kent Mind which may be more appropriate to your needs. So, how much do you charge? Initial assessment costs £15, with ongoing costs of £15 per session. Long term support with an annual review and possible extension availiable. There is a cap of 2 years to receive this service. The Affordable Counselling and Therapy Service is designed for people who have counselling needs not able to be met through the NHS service ,where long term work is more appropriate or who wish to work further on issues once their NHS-allocated therapy is completed. Aims of the Affordable Counselling and Therapy Service: To offer a high quality, low-cost counselling service. Minimum age of clients is 18 years. To provide clients with space and time to work with an individual counsellor to meet their individual needs. To create positive changes in clients’ lives by addressing specific problems, identifying ways of coping, developing personal insight, understanding thoughts and feelings, and considering the implications of actions that may be taken. To encourage clients to make appropriate use of other services provided by North Kent Mind or other agencies. To provide a counselling service which is also appropriate to the needs of people with significant mental health issues.
price: £15 per session Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email fee-payingservices@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: AI-Anon Family Groups Ashford
address: Ashford Volunteer Centre, Berwick House; 8 Elwick Road, Ashford, Kent, TN23 1PF What we do We understand that you may be full of questions ahead of your first Al-Anon meeting. Here is what you can expect. Expect to meet people from different walks of life all united through a common bond: a challenging relationship with a drinker. They may be a relative, a colleague or a friend to someone who drinks. Although shared experiences may be common, each member will have a unique perspective on the problem of dealing with alcoholism. If you keep an open mind, you are likely to hear something you can identify with, which will help you move forward. Meeting on Wednesday from 7:30pm. The Meeting is held at Ashford Volunteer Centre. Click here to find out more

title: Al-Anon Helpline
address: Virtual What we do We are here for anyone* affected by someone else’s drinking. Our Helpline is manned by a team of friendly and helpful volunteers who are also members of Al-Anon. Call us free of charge on 0800 0086 811 from 10 am – 10 pm, 365 days a year. Please do not leave a message as, to ensure callers’ confidentiality, we are unable to return calls. *Given the recent outbreak of coronavirus we wanted to reassure you that Al-Anon Family Groups is still providing support. Whilst we do have a contingency plan in place there may be times when the Helpline is covered by a limited number of volunteers so please bear with us while we try to answer your call. We suggest anyone wishing to attend a meeting calls the Helpline to request a group contact.

Our Helpline is manned by a team of friendly and helpful volunteers who are also members of Al-Anon. Call us free of charge on 0800 0086 811 from 10 am – 10 pm, 365 days a year. Please do not leave a message as, to ensure callers’ confidentiality, we are unable to return calls. *Given the recent outbreak of coronavirus we wanted to reassure you that Al-Anon Family Groups is still providing support. Whilst we do have a contingency plan in place there may be times when the Helpline is covered by a limited number of volunteers so please bear with us while we try to answer your call. We suggest anyone wishing to attend a meeting calls the Helpline to request a group contact. Price Free
categories: Befriending; Health; Helplines; Specific health conditions; Support Services and Self-help

title: Allington Tuesday Café
address: St Nicholas Church, Poplar Grove, Maidstone, ME16 0DE What we do This Community café welcomes all comers each Tuesday morning at 9.30am, although food and drinks are served from 10-12am. The café was set up to provide an opportunity for people to meet others, especially those living alone, recently bereaved, struggling with depression or just looking for company. It is also a place where people can share concerns confidentially and informally if wished. There is a popular table Quiz each week and several copies are available on each table.There is also a free book exchange, and a list of Traders personally recommended by people who come to the Cafe that many find very helpful. Taking part in this is entirely optional, but it has proved to be a good starting point for talking to others. There is wheelchair access. There is no charge, although it is possible to make a donation if wished. Please note that although this Café takes place inside a church (which ultimately pays for it) it is a Community provision. The Cafe is registered with Maidstone Environmental Health (5 star rating) and as a place that can be used for Social Prescribing. All Safeguarding recommendations are adhered to.
price: Free Organisation St Nicholas Allington Telephone 07434 156563 Email canonliz.walker@gmail.com Organisation St Nicholas Allington

title: Suicide Bereavement Service
address: Registered Address: Listening Ear Charity, Halewood, Liverpool, L26 6LB What we do Amparo offers practical and emotional support for anyone bereaved by suicide in Kent and Medway Amparo’s local Liaison Workers aim to make initial contact within 24 hours of a referral being made. To find out more about Amparo, or to make a referral, visit this page . If you’re a professional and want to learn more about the Amparo service and the support we offer, book onto one of our free briefing sessions here . Amparo Kent and Medway is a free and confidential service funded by the Kent and Medway STP. Amparo is also now available to support individuals living outside of the area, in the event that they are the immediate next of kin to an individual lost to suicide in Kent and Medway. This will include support at any following inquest held in Kent and Medway.

title: Anger Management Course
address: Dartford What we do Anger Management courses are for a period of 6 weeks for 2.5 hours per session on a Saturday. The total cost of the course being £150 with prior assessment necessary. This is a group experience (up to 10 participants at a time), which we find to be an effective way of helping people with their anger management issues. The course is available to anyone 18 and over, even if they live outside the area, as long as they are prepared to pay the cost upfront and as long as they do not present too high a risk to others. An assessment would need to be carried out for consideration to partake in the group, and a waiting list is held for anyone interested in being contacted for an assessment. To find out more, please email : fee-payingservices@northkentmind.co.uk
price: £150 Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email fee-payingservices@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Ashford Safe Haven MHM
address: Ashford Safe Haven, William Harvey Hospital, Kennington Rd, Willesborough, Ashford TN24 0LZ What we do Are you feeling distressed, frightened, overwhelmed, or that things are too much for you? Are your usual sources of support closed for the night? What can you expect from us: This is a walk-in service – there’s no need to be referred by your GP or Care Coordinator. Ashford Safe Haven offers a welcoming, safe, comfortable, non-judgmental and non-clinical environment. We can provide information and emotional support if you are in crisis or feel you are heading towards a crisis situation. You can come just for a chat, or you can access one-to-one emotional support from trained mental health professionals. We also offer help in creating staying well and crisis plans, and can support you to access other services and organisations that may be helpful. We work closely with GPs, Community Mental Health Teams, Crisis Teams, A&E Mental Health Liaison Teams, Police and Ambulance Services, Street Triage, and other front-line healthcare professionals to ensure that people in emotional distress have a safe, supportive place to go to, with appropriate mental health support available. Who is this service for? Anyone aged 18 and over can come to Ashford Safe Haven; you do not need a formal referral.
price: Free Organisation Ashford Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters Telephone 07811 593 219 Email ashford.mhm@nhs.net Organisation Ashford Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters

title: Asylum Support
address: Virtual What we do Those seeking asylum in the UK can come to us to seek help with applying for accommodation and financial support, to report issues with asylum accommodation, and any other advice needed during their asylum journey. We also provide support during the post-decision period, whether the decision has been positive or negative. Thanks to the hard work, resilience and dedication of our colleagues, we are pleased to provide a free 24-hour, 7 day a week telephone helpline supporting asylum seekers in the UK, delivered in partnership with Connect Assist.

title: Babies Matter Parenting Programme (Gillingham)
address: St Augustine's Church, Rock Avenue, Gillingham, ME7 5PW What we do Babies Matter is a programme to support both expectant parents and those with new babies who are facing disadvantages. Over six sessions, the programme helps parents and carers to build confidence, competence, and community as they welcome their new baby to the world. Babies Matter aims to help parents and carers lay a strong foundation for their family by building a strong attachment with their child, focusing on their understanding of their baby as well as looking after their own mental wellbeing. It is a group based course.

Over six sessions, the programme helps parents and carers to build confidence, competence, and community as they welcome their new baby to the world. title: Babons Cancer Support
address: 147 High Street, Tonbridge, TN9 1DH What we do Babons is here to help and support anyone who has been impacted by cancer in Kent. We have a centre on Tonbridge High Street which is open from Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm where you can “drop-in” for a cuppa and a chat but we can also offer 12 one hour 1:1 sessions of Counselling. In our centre we hold themed and structured Support Groups specifically for Women, Men, Carers, Bereaved and the LGBTQA+ community. These are 2 hour sessions and run for 10 weeks – booking is essential to secure a place. In addition to all these services we can support you with signposting. If we can’t offer it here, we will help you find the support that you need.

title: Baby Massage & Parental Wellbeing Group – Maidstone
address: Infozone, 3 Palace Avenue, Maidstone ME15 6NF What we do This is a relaxed and informal group where you can come together with other new parents, share your experiences and get tailored advice and support from our Wellbeing Coaches about how to look after your mental health. Each session will also include dedicated time for baby massage. Baby massage can bring many benefits including improved sleep and improved digestion; it can also help you feel more connected to your baby. Our instructors are fully trained and will provide baby safe oils and muslin cloths. You might like to bring a towel or blanket for your baby to lie on so they are extra comfy. We recommend this session is for parents whose babies are aged 8 weeks to 6 months. When : Every Tuesday from 14th April for 6 weeks from 9.30am to 11.30am. Where : Infozone, 3 Palace Avenue, Maidstone ME15 6NF Booking : To book your place, please use this link to fill out the form.

title: BACP Accredited Counselling
address: London Road, 49, Richmond Terrace, Tunbridge Well, TN1 1DT What we do Our counsellors offer psychodynamic counselling. They will help you understand how past experiences, worries about the future, or what goes on for you unconsciously may be affecting your life. Counselling is arranged on a once-weekly basis in fifty-minute sessions, and can be on either an open-ended basis or for a set number of sessions depending on your needs. This is discussed with you during your initial assessment appointment. We work with a very wide range of issues and problems including depression, anxiety, low self esteem, low mood, or issues which may not have any diagnosis or a label.

title: BACP Accredited Training
address: London Road, 49, Richmond Terrace, Tunbridge Well, TN1 1DT What we do Certificate in Psychodynamic Counselling Skills This foundation training in skills and theory is intended for those whose work or voluntary activities involve, or could benefit from basic counselling skills. Nurses, health visitors, doctors, surgery staff, teachers, clergy, social workers, police officers, probation officers and many others can benefit from this foundation course. The Certificate in Psychodynamic Counselling forms the foundation for the Diploma in Psychodynamic Counselling (BACP accredited). Our 2023 Certificate courses will run on a Tuesday or Thursday evening, 6.30 pm to 9.45 pm, over 32 weeks of the year divided into three terms. Numbers are limited for each group.

title: Barming Heath Community Café
address: St Andrew’s Church Hall, Tonbridge Road, Barming Heath, ME16 9LW (Enter Hall from back of Church – St Andrew’s Road ME16 9AL) What we do This Community café welcomes all comers each Wednesday morning. Cake, coffee and tea are served from 10am – 12 noon. The café was set up to provide an opportunity for people to meet others, especially those living alone, recently bereaved, struggling with depression or just looking for company. It is also a place where people can share concerns confidentially and informally if wished. There is wheelchair access. There is no charge, although it is possible to make a donation if wished. Please note that although this Café takes place inside a church building (which ultimately pays for it) it is a Community provision. All Safeguarding recommendations are adhered to.

title: Beat Adult Helpline
address: Virtual What we do Beat provides Helplines for people of all ages, offering support and information about eating disorders no matter where you are in your journey. These Helplines are free to call from all phones. You don’t need a formal diagnosis to use our Helpline Our Helplines are open from 3pm– 8pm, Monday to Friday. Call us on 0808 801 0677 or email us at help@beateatingdisorders.org.uk Support on the Helpline is available by phone, email, webchat through our social media accounts and by letter. Calls to the helpline are free from landlines and mobile phones within the UK and do not appear on itemised bills. How we can help If you contact our Helpline you will speak to a trained advisor experienced in providing support, information and signposting to people in a similar situation to you. Our helpline is action focused, giving you a space to explore your concerns and identify next steps. Our eating disorder Helpline advisors are trained to: Offer a supportive space for you to explore your feelings and thoughts around eating disorders. Provide information about eating disorders. When we do not have the information that someone is looking for, we might be able to give you some ideas about who might be able to help. Explore options for help with eating disorders and to enable you to come to your own decisions about what might be best for you. This might include NHS treatment, private therapy, support from charitable organisations, peer support or self-help.

title: Befriending
address: Thanet, Shepway and Dover/Deal What we do Our Befriending service is for people aged 60+ who are feeling isolated. We also offer opportunities to become a volunteer befriender. This service operates in Thanet, Shepway and Dover/Deal. It aims to reduce the feeling of loneliness and isolation among older people in our communities. We hope to help our members gain in confidence so that they can leave the house, use technology or simply be able to have a friendly conversation and build a lasting friendship through regular visits from the same volunteer, on a long-term basis. You can also become a volunteer befriender if you wish to. As a befriender you will be introduced to a client who lives close by and, between you, decide how regular your visits will be and what you both want to get out of those visits. This could be as simple as a weekly chat and a cup of tea around the client’s house, or a walk outside or to the local shops. Find out more about the service on our website. Contact us for more information.
description: Our Befriending service is for people aged 60+ who are feeling isolated. We also offer opportunities to become a volunteer befriender. This service operates in Thanet, Shepway and Dover/Deal. It aims to reduce the feeling of loneliness and isolation among older people in our communities. We hope to help our members gain in confidence so that they can leave the house, use technology or simply be able to have a friendly conversation and build a lasting friendship through regular visits from the same volunteer, on a long-term basis. You can also become a volunteer befriender if you wish to. As a befriender you will be introduced to a client who lives close by and, between you, decide how regular your visits will be and what you both want to get out of those visits. This could be as simple as a weekly chat and a cup of tea around the client’s house, or a walk outside or to the local shops. Find out more about the service on our website. Contact us for more information. Our Befriending service is for people aged 60+ who are feeling isolated. We also offer opportunities to become a volunteer befriender. title: Befriending
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Are you over 55 and feeling isolated or alone? Would you like to have someone to talk to? Someone who will not judge you for being yourself? Someone who would delight in the tales of your past? Someone who would love to chat about how life is for you now? Someone who would listen and hear what you would like for your future? If you answered YES to any of these questions then Age UK Tunbridge Wells’s Befriending service may be right for you! The charity has volunteers with a diverse range of backgrounds and interests (including from the LGBTQ+ community) who are ready to befriend older people.

title: Dance for fun (Aylesford – RBLI Capel Morris)
address: RBLI Capel Morris, Hall Road, Aylesford, Kent, ME20 7NL What we do Introduction to basic dance steps, short routines featuring difference dance styles. No experience needed just enthusiasm! ‘Dance For Fun’ is a low impact cardio workout and each week participants will learn a simple new routine, before a gentle cool down at the end. This class is all about having fun, meeting new friends and having some me time. Dancing is great for both your mental and physical health. It can help to prevent falls, improve posture and flexibility, lift our mood and ease anxiety. Dancing is also such a fun activity, that it helps us to build social bonds and improve our self-confidence. Session details: When: Fridays at 9:30am Where: RBLI Capel Morris Cost: £4.00 a session For more information and to book, please email natalie.williams@tmactive.co.uk
price: £4.00 Organisation TMActive Email natalie.williams@tmactive.co.uk Organisation TMActive

title: Bereavement Helpline
address: Virtual What we do We support those bereaved by suicide and open the conversation around suicide-related grief. Bereavement in all forms can be traumatic; however, suicide undoubtably adds a range of emotions to navigate – shock, guilt, shame and isolation to name a few. We have a Helpline run by bereavement counsellors that is open Monday-Friday 9am-9pm and acts as a listening service for those bereaved by suicide.

title: Bipolar UK eCommunity
address: Virtual What we do Join us online here – Our eCommunity is a supportive online forum for everyone affected by bipolar. The eCommunity is a safe and secure space where you can discuss the challenges you face, ask questions, and share your experiences. We have over 13,000 members and a team of friendly moderators to keep things running smoothly. The service is anonymous, free to use and open to anyone affected by bipolar, including those with the diagnosis, pre-diagnosis, family, friends and loved ones. We have spaces to talk about everything from pregnancy and parenting to therapies and self-management, as well as spaces for socialising, creativity and fun and games. To keep the eCommunity safe, everyone who joins agrees to abide by our House Rules, and completes a short online registration form, after which you’ll have access 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. Please note, if you joined before October 2018, you will need to register again.
price: Free Organisation Bipolar UK Telephone 0333 323 3880 Email ecommunity@bipolaruk.org Organisation Bipolar UK

title: Bipolar UK Family and Friends Support group meetings
address: Virtual What we do Where: Online When: 1st Friday of the month Time: 2pm – 3pm Register for free here. Our support groups are free and this group is open to anyone who is a family member, friend, loved one or carer of someone with bipolar or who is pre-diagnosis. Anyone over 18 is welcome. If you’re 16 or 17, please bring a parent or guardian. The format of the meetings vary, but you will have the opportunity to introduce yourself, say how you are feeling, and may listen to an invited speaker. The groups are organised and facilitated by volunteers who are affected by bipolar in some way. If you would like to join the next support meeting, please book a free ticket here. You will receive the link to join via email after booking a ticket. If you have any questions please email: supportgroups@bipolaruk.org We look forward to seeing you soon. Find out more on our website here.

title: Bipolar UK National Support group meetings
address: Virtual What we do Where: Online When: 2nd Tuesday of the month Time: 12.30pm – 1.30pm Our support groups are free and open to anyone affected by bipolar, including family, friends and loved ones who are unable to attend a local support group for any reason. If you don’t have a confirmed diagnosis and want to find out about bipolar, and related conditions such as cyclothymia, you are also welcome. Anyone over 18 is welcome. If you’re 16 or 17, please bring a parent or guardian. The format of the meetings vary, but you will have the opportunity to introduce yourself, say how you are feeling using the Bipolar UK mood scale and may listen to an invited speaker. The groups are organised and facilitated by volunteers who are affected by bipolar in some way. If you would like to join the next support meeting, please register here . If you have any questions please email: supportgroups@bipolaruk.org We look forward to seeing you soon.

title: Breakeven
address: 2 Romney Place, Maidstone, Kent ME15 6LE, UK What we do We offer support for anyone affected by harmful gambling, including the individual gambling, family members and friends. The aim of our treatment is to provide an opportunity for the gambler to work towards increasing their wellbeing by becoming free of harmful gambling. Breakeven offer 1:1 support as well as regular group support which include WINGS (Women In Need of Gambling Support, remote), Green Shoots Recovery which is an 8 weeks aftercare group for those who have successfully stopped gambling and require extra supported learning, and the Growth Hub which is a teams support service following on from Green Shoots. Once you contact us, we will work quickly by getting you assessed so we understand the extent of your addiction. Following the completion of a successful assessment you will be contacted by a counsellor usually within a few days. We work fast to get you the support you need without delay.
price: Free Organisation Breakeven Telephone 01273 833722 Email info@breakeven.org.uk Organisation Breakeven

title: 3D Care Support Service
address: 36b, Milwood House, Albion Place, Maidstone, ME14 5DZ What we do We’re committed to helping Deaf, Deafblind, and deaf and non-verbal people with additional needs and other disabilities in Kent, Medway, East Sussex and the surrounding areas to achieve their goals. Our friendly and experienced Support Workers offer personalised support, tailored to our client’s specific needs, to promote independence, improve wellbeing and make connections in the community. Regular access to our client’s preferred communication methods allows them to develop the skills to express themselves and understand their environment to live safely and confidently. Services include help to learn new skills, support to access services and the local community, help to understand and manage correspondence, and much more. Our support service is available for both adults and children, and can be provided in-person or remotely.

title: CAP Canterbury Central Life Skills Course
address: Emmanuel Church Canterbury, Hales Drive, Canterbury, UK What we do Learn practical tools to help you live well on a low income every day. Life skills is a friendly group that will give you the confidence and decision-making skills needed to live on a low income. You’ll learn practical money saving techniques, such as cooking on a budget and making money go further. What to expect from the group: Community – We know that when it comes to saving money and making lifestyle changes, there’s so much we can learn from each other. Everyone finds dealing with the pressures of life a stressful and lonely challenge at times. That’s why life skills is designed as a community group, where everyone gets together to share practical skills in the areas of life that are impacted by our income, such as shopping on a budget and looking after our wellbeing and mental health. Course – Life skills usually runs over eight sessions. The group is designed to equip you with skills for every day life, including top tips for dealing with life’s pressures and navigating life on a low income. The sessions happen in a relaxed group environment, with a mixture of practical activities and informal discussions. Coaching – You’ll have the opportunity to benefit from informal one-to-one support from a trained Money Coach, who will help you to think through your individual spending choices, and create a simple budget that works for you. This will help you gain better control over your money, lightening the pressures money can bring and building your confidence and self-esteem in managing your finances. Our Canterbury Life Skills group is run in partnership with: Emmanuel Church Contact your local coach for more information: Paul Lusk / Life skills coach / paullusk@caplifeskills.org
price: Free Organisation Christians Against Poverty Email paullusk@caplifeskills.org Organisation Christians Against Poverty

title: Canturbury Safe Haven MHM
address: 22-23 North Lane, Canterbury, Kent, CT2 7EE What we do Canterbury Safe Haven offers out-of-hours mental health support to anyone aged 18+ in the Kent area. Calls and emails will be answered by staff during Haven opening hours. You may call and leave a message at any other time of day, and these will be picked up and responded to as soon as possible during opening hours. When : Open nightly, including weekends and bank holidays, from 6 pm – 11 pm. What can you expect from us: This is a walk-in service – there’s no need to be referred by your GP or Care Coordinator. The Canterbury Safe Haven offers a welcoming, safe, comfortable, non-judgmental and non-clinical environment, sometimes known as ‘crisis cafes’. At The Havens, we will be able to provide information and emotional support if you are in crisis or feel you are heading towards a crisis situation. You can come just for a chat, or you can access one-to-one emotional support from trained mental health professionals. We also offer help in creating staying well and crisis plans, and support visitors to access other services and organisations that may be useful to them. We work closely with GPs, Community Mental Health Teams, Crisis Teams, A&E Mental Health Liaison Teams, Police and Ambulance Services, Street Triage, and other front-line healthcare professionals to ensure that people in emotional distress have a safe, supportive place to go to, with appropriate mental health support available. People using the Canterbury Safe Haven will also be supported by our 24/7 helpline, which offers access to emotional support and information even when The Haven is closed. If you are in immediate crisis and require urgent support from the Haven, please contact us by telephone or dial 999. Please be aware that email communications are not monitored in real time and may not receive an immediate response.
price: Free Organisation Canterbury Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters Telephone 07876 476 703 or 07483 163 953 Email canterbury.mhm@nhs.net Organisation Canterbury Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters

title: CAP Ashford Life Skills Course
address: Willesborough Baptist Church, Willesboroough Baptist Church, Hythe Road, Willesborough, Ashford, UK What we do Learn practical tools to help you live well on a low income every day. Life skills is a friendly group that will give you the confidence and decision-making skills needed to live on a low income. You’ll learn practical money saving techniques, such as cooking on a budget and making money go further. What to expect from the group: Community – We know that when it comes to saving money and making lifestyle changes, there’s so much we can learn from each other. Everyone finds dealing with the pressures of life a stressful and lonely challenge at times. That’s why life skills is designed as a community group, where everyone gets together to share practical skills in the areas of life that are impacted by our income, such as shopping on a budget and looking after our wellbeing and mental health. Course – Life skills usually runs over eight sessions. The group is designed to equip you with skills for every day life, including top tips for dealing with life’s pressures and navigating life on a low income. The sessions happen in a relaxed group environment, with a mixture of practical activities and informal discussions. Coaching – You’ll have the opportunity to benefit from informal one-to-one support from a trained Money Coach, who will help you to think through your individual spending choices, and create a simple budget that works for you. This will help you gain better control over your money, lightening the pressures money can bring and building your confidence and self-esteem in managing your finances. Our Ashford Life Skills group is run in partnership with: Willesborough Baptist Church Contact your local coach for more information: Anne Casse / Life skills coach / annecasse@caplifeskills.org
price: Free Organisation Christians Against Poverty Email annecasse@caplifeskills.org Organisation Christians Against Poverty

title: CAP Money coaching – Abbey Wood
address: Abbey Wood Community Church, New Road, London SE2 0AX, UK What we do A free service designed to empower you with the knowledge, budgeting skills and confidence to better manage your finances. Money coaching is a free service designed to empower you with the knowledge, skills, tools and confidence to better manage your finances. Anyone can benefit – from seasoned budgeting experts to those struggling to get to grips with their spending. Money coaching has been designed and developed by CAP. title: Care Act Advocacy
address: Registered Address, PO Box 375, Hastings, East Sussex, TN34 9HU What we do Some people find making decisions about health and care needs very difficult. The Care Act gives eligible people the right to have an independent advocate who can support them to prepare for care assessments, meetings or safeguarding enquiries, help them understand the care and support processes and options available to them, help them feel able to give their views and wishes about their care and support needs, work with them so that they are able to make their own decisions, support and represent them when appropriate, and to challenge decisions made if it is felt that their views have not been taken into account. This right is also extended to carers, for instance those who provide care to relatives or friends.
price: Free Organisation The Advocacy People Telephone 0330 440 9000 Email info@theadvocacypeople.org.uk Organisation The Advocacy People

This right is also extended to carers, for instance those who provide care to relatives or friends. Price Free

title: Care for People with Complex Needs
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Riverside provides tailored services for people with combined histories of homelessness, mental health and substance misuse. They may live in unstable housing and ‘fall in-between’ conventional services. As their needs are multiple and complex, they require specialised care to retain independence, choice and control into later life. By offering a suitable place to live for people who display challenging behaviours we can prevent frequent housing moves and provide stability.

title: Care Navigation and Coordination
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Skilled professionals, our care navigators, work alongside the customer to identify and remove barriers causing extended stays in hospital. title: Career Coaching for Unemployed Women
address: Face to Face or Virtual What we do This one-to-one, hour-long coaching session is delivered by one of our trained interview coaches and focusing on helping the client to feel positive about her job search and next steps. The session takes place either in person, over the phone or on Zoom and can include guidance on finding a job in the current climate, tips on developing skills from home, CV advice or an introduction to key interview skills. We can support women anywhere in the UK with our virtual service. To be eligible for a Smart Works Appointment, a Client must identify as female and be currently unemployed. If the client would like a First Dressing & Interview Preparation session, then they must also have a confirmed upcoming job or apprenticeship interview. To make a referral, please phone us on the number below.

title: Carers Short Breaks
address: Registered Address: The Oast, 170, Tonbridge Road, Wateringbury, ME18 5NS What we do Our Carers short breaks are a home base respite for adult Carers, allowing them to take a break from caring. Respite visits are usually weekly, but this can be flexible. Continuity of care means that the same Carer Support Worker visits each time in order to establish trust and understanding and they are able to carry out all the caring tasks that the Carer would normally do, including personal care tasks, during their visits. Office Hours 9-5 Mon to Friday. Service for over 18’s only.

title: Carers Support Groups
address: Safeharbour Memory Wellbeing Centre, Coldharbour Road, Northfleet, Kent, DA11 8AE What we do Caring for someone living with dementia can be rewarding, but it can also be exhausting, emotional and, at times, incredibly isolating. Many carers tell us that friends and family simply don’t understand the challenges they face day to day. Our Carers Peer Support Groups provide a welcoming space where you can connect with others who truly understand. Whether you are looking for advice, reassurance, friendship or simply a chance to talk openly, these groups offer support from people who are walking a similar path. You are welcome to attend on your own or with the person you care for. Volunteers are available to spend time with your loved one, giving you the opportunity to relax, chat and focus on your own wellbeing. Each group is shaped by the people who attend. Some members enjoy social activities, guest speakers and outings, while others value the opportunity to share experiences, exchange practical tips and gain emotional support. Our team regularly attends to provide information, guidance and signposting to other services that may help. Everyone’s dementia journey is different, but no one should have to face it alone. By joining a support group, you can build meaningful connections, gain valuable insight and find comfort in knowing that others understand what you’re going through. Carer Peer Support Groups take place in: Rochester Swanley Gravesend We know attending a new group for the first time can feel daunting. That’s why we’ll ensure you receive a warm welcome, with support to help you feel comfortable and introduced to other members. Our Carers Peer Support Groups are free to attend, although donations are always appreciated to help us continue providing this valuable service. To find out more or join a group, call us on 01474 533990, email info@adss.org.uk or visit our website www.adss.org.uk .
price: Free but donations welcome Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: Catering and Hospitality Courses
address: Various locations What we do Level 2 Food Safety in Catering – an essential qualification for anyone looking to work or volunteer in food preparation. It is the legal requirement in order to handle food in the workplace. This is a full day course. Level 2 Award in Food Allergen Awareness and Control in Catering – Designed to provide knowledge and understanding of food allergens and foods that commonly cause intolerances. This course is 5 hours. Level 1 Introduction to Customer Service – Provides an introduction to the skills and personal qualities required for careers and jobs within the customer service sector. This course is 6 hours. Level 1 Introduction to Hospitality – Provides and introduction to a range of environments and settings you can work in. This course is 4 hours. Barista Training – This will give you a good understanding of how to make the most popular coffees served in most coffee shops.

title: Chaplaincy and Spiritual Care
address: Hospice in the Weald, Maidstone Road, Pembury, Tunbridge Wells, TN2 4TA What we do Spiritual care comes in many forms. We try and appeal to the whole person offering creative expression through music and art as well as pleasure and relaxation through our various wellbeing therapies. Spiritual care is here for all our patients, families, carers, volunteers and staff. Whoever you are you are welcome. Our Chaplaincy volunteers work as part of the hospice multi-disciplinary team to offer spiritual and pastoral support throughout the Hospice community as requested. The team adheres to the national standards for Hospices and Palliative Care Chaplaincy. Any members of the team will be happy to meet with you to discuss anything of concern, and to support you in any way they can formally, or more informally, as you wish. Visits can also be arranged by a leader from a patients’ own faith community or religious tradition. Please contact us directly about accessing the Hospice.
price: Free Organisation Hospice in the Weald Telephone 01892 820548 Email enquiries@hospiceintheweald.org.uk Organisation Hospice in the Weald

title: Chaplaincy Free Service in Dartford & Bexley
address: Evergreen Care UK, 39 Broad Lane, Wilmington, Kent, DA2 7AQ What we do Sometimes life brings questions, change, or challenges that are hard to talk about, even with those closest to us. If you’re navigating a difficult season, reflecting on the future, or simply need someone to listen without judgment, our Chaplains are here for you.​ Chaplains offer a calm, confidential space to explore how you’re feeling, whether you’re facing uncertainty, grief, big life transitions, or simply feeling low or disconnected. They’re not here to fix or diagnose, but to walk with you, helping you reflect, find peace, and move forward at your own pace. If spirituality is important to you, they’re happy to bring faith into the conversation, and if it’s not, that’s respected too. At Evergreen, we believe true wellbeing includes the whole person, body, mind, and spirit. You don’t have to go through life’s hard moments alone. Our Chaplaincy Service is here to support you in a way that feels personal, respectful, and right for you.
price: Free Organisation Evergreen Care UK Telephone 01322 431765​ Email info@evergreencareuk.org Organisation Evergreen Care UK

title: Chatty Chums
address: Clarendon Royal Community, Royal Pier Road, Gravesend, DA12 2BE What we do Chatty Chums Lunch Club is run from the Community Hub at the Clarendon Royal Hotel on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays, and offers companionship, fun activities and lunch to older people in North West Kent. Our Lunch Club provides older people living in North West Kent with a space to meet new people, make new friends, enjoy a host of wellbeing services and activities, and enjoy a freshly prepared two-course lunch and refreshments. Customers can join in with a variety of activities, and benefit from our on-site ancillary services including Footcare and Hairdressing – all in the perfect location of the historic Clarendon Royal building on the banks of the River Thames. Our Lunch Club has many activities on offer, which focus on health, wellbeing and of course, fun! Join us and spend the afternoon taking part in some of the below activities: Arts and CraftS Knit and Natter Seated exercise and postural stability classes Themed event & party days Companionship Singing and music Quizzes, games and Bingo Professional entertainment Gardening such as plant potting.
price: £11.50 Telephone 01634 572616 Email enquiries@ageukkentrivers.org.uk Telephone 01634 572616

title: Children heard + seen: Parent/Carer Groups
address: Hill End Camp, Eynsham Road, Cumnor, OX2 9NJ What we do Our parent/carer support is for the non-offending parent/carer, whether or not they are still in a relationship with the parent in prison. Parents have the opportunity to meet others in a similar situation and form peer-support groups, aided by our trained staff. We 6-8 different adult support groups a week. These include groups for adults impacted by specific offence types, such as domestic abuse, and sexual offences. Our current group work programme is listed below, however it does regularly change due to need and demand. Online Adult Group s Comfort Group – group that shares hopes and advice that deals with the journey of families affected by parental imprisonment Exploring Trauma – Support for individuals that have been impacted by trauma Support group for mothers caring for children with a father in prison for sexual offences Kids Matter Parenting Programme – 6-week parenting support group Refer to Children Heard and Seen If you are caring for a child with a parent in prison and would like to access support for yourself or your child, please get in touch at info@childrenheardandseen.co.uk
price: Nationwide Organisation Children Heard and Seen Email info@childrenheardandseen.co.uk Organisation Children Heard and Seen

Parents have the opportunity to meet others in a similar situation and form peer-support groups, aided by our trained staff. We 6-8 different adult support groups a week. These include groups for adults impacted by specific offence types, such as domestic abuse, and sexual offences. Our current group work programme is listed below, however it does regularly change due to need and demand. Online Adult Group s Comfort Group – group that shares hopes and advice that deals with the journey of families affected by parental imprisonment Exploring Trauma – Support for individuals that have been impacted by trauma Support group for mothers caring for children with a father in prison for sexual offences Kids Matter Parenting Programme – 6-week parenting support group Refer to Children Heard and Seen If you are caring for a child with a parent in prison and would like to access support for yourself or your child, please get in touch at info@childrenheardandseen.co.uk Our parent/carer support is for the non-offending parent/carer, whether or not they are still in a relationship with the parent in prison. title: Chipstead Lake Activities Centre
address: Chipstead Lake, Chevening Road, Chipstead, Sevenoaks, TN13 2SD What we do Chipstead Lake activity and skills group is lively and inviting – where you can meet friends, have fun and achieve the things you want. Our centre is in a lovely setting beside Chipstead Lake, near Sevenoaks, Kent. We have extensive grounds offering lots of opportunities to enjoy the lake. It is ideal whether you like fishing, sailing or just taking in the views across the water. We aim to support people with disabilities by promoting independent living and offering opportunities to try a variety of activities. Experienced and friendly staff provide support, encouragement and guidance, with the following facilities: Cooking in our adapted accessible kitchen, for the individual or in groups A selection of art and craft activities. For the green-fingered person, we have a gardening club and a vegetable patch Sailing on the adjoining lake, with fully accessible boats. Regular day trips to local amenities and further afield are arranged throughout the year, ranging from trips to the seaside, to visits to nature reserves, larger shopping outlets, restaurants and cinema. We are open Monday to Friday from 9:30am to 3:30pm. Please call for more information

address: Virtual What we do If you think you have a problem with any mind-altering substance, please call the Helpline and speak to a member of Cocaine Anonymous, from 10:00 to 22:00, 7 days a week. title: Cognitive Stimulation Therapy
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Cognitive Stimulation Therapy, or CST for short, is a specific programme of group activity and stimulation suitable for use with people who are living with dementia or experiencing memory loss (aged 55+). Through the programme, we aim to slow the progression of dementia and improve the quality of your life, enhance your ability to communicate and help boost your mood. There will be up to six people in each group, along with the group leader and assistant. The atmosphere is relaxed, and the idea is to have fun and get to know the other group members, as well as benefit from the activities. Sessions always begin with a coffee or tea and a chat. After that, there will be an activity which is designed to stimulate different areas of the brain each week. These activities are geared to what you, as an individual, feel comfortable doing.
price: Free Organisation Age UK Tunbridge Wells Telephone 01892 522591 Email info@ageuktw.org.uk Organisation Age UK Tunbridge Wells

title: COGS Club (Maidstone)
address: Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge, Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Maidstone, ME15 0AL What we do We know that stimulation is crucial for anyone experiencing changes to their cognitive capabilities. COGS Groups have emotional wellbeing and happiness at their heart – ensuring people attending these sessions feel fulfilled. COGS sessions offer tailored support to individuals who are facing memory challenges. Utilising Cognitive Stimulation therapy, participants can expect to: Engage in purposeful and structured activities which keep their brain engaged, and improve cognitive function like memory, attention and language. Take part in group activities with other people who may have similar experiences, forming friendships and connections Participate in engaging themes which will facilitate discussions, activities and other practical tasks Experience a boost in confidence, and ability to cope with and overcome challenges faced as a part of every-day life These sessions are designed with wellbeing at their heart. We will help people with mild-to-moderate Dementia and Alzheimer’s diagnosis to maintain their level of cognitive capability, and crucially, to find fulfilment from everyday activities. Our COGS sessions are staffed by trained personnel, meaning caregivers are able to drop-off the person they are caring for, providing an invaluable opportunity for some respite. We hope that this service provides caregivers with the opportunity to take a well-deserved break, safe in the knowledge that their loved-one will be well-cared for. This will provide time for shopping, visiting the GP – or just spending time with friends. Our sessions aren’t just about promoting mental wellbeing through meaningful participation – they are about promoting wellbeing in an entire ‘care unit’, ensuring that both the person being cared for and the caregiver are empowered to thrive. When : Every Monday (9:45am – 2:45pm), Wednesday (9:45am – 2:45pm) and Friday (10am – 3pm). Where : Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge, Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Maidstone, ME15 0AL COGS Club sessions cost £28 per session. This cost covers the provision of trained personnel throughout the duration of the session, as well as a light lunch, snacks, and hot and cold beverages provided throughout.

We will help people with mild-to-moderate Dementia and Alzheimer’s diagnosis to maintain their level of cognitive capability, and crucially, to find fulfilment from everyday activities. Our COGS sessions are staffed by trained personnel, meaning caregivers are able to drop-off the person they are caring for, providing an invaluable opportunity for some respite. We hope that this service provides caregivers with the opportunity to take a well-deserved break, safe in the knowledge that their loved-one will be well-cared for. This will provide time for shopping, visiting the GP – or just spending time with friends. Our sessions aren’t just about promoting mental wellbeing through meaningful participation – they are about promoting wellbeing in an entire ‘care unit’, ensuring that both the person being cared for and the caregiver are empowered to thrive. When : Every Monday (9:45am – 2:45pm), Wednesday (9:45am – 2:45pm) and Friday (10am – 3pm). Where : Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge, Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Maidstone, ME15 0AL COGS Club sessions cost £28 per session. This cost covers the provision of trained personnel throughout the duration of the session, as well as a light lunch, snacks, and hot and cold beverages provided throughout. We know that stimulation is crucial for anyone experiencing changes to their cognitive capabilities. COGS Groups have emotional wellbeing and happiness at their heart – ensuring people attending these sessions feel fulfilled. title: Community Advocacy
address: Registered Address, PO Box 375, Hastings, East Sussex, TN34 9HU What we do Community Advocacy is a specialist service which supports adults who are most vulnerable, or who find themselves at a particularly challenging point in their lives. Our professional advocates enable people without the right support around them to have their voice heard when decisions are being taken about their health and social care and when support is needed to make informed choices about their life. Our advocates are skilled in providing support around issues relating to a person in a vulnerable situation, a person subject to a safeguarding alert, mental health illness or distress, learning disabilities, conditions on the autistic spectrum, an older person aged 65+ (aged 50+ in Kent), physical disabilities, sensory impairments, dementia and fluctuating mental capacity or acquired brain injury.
