title: Community Befriending Service (Maidstone)
address: Various including home visits What we do Loneliness and isolation can have big impact on the health and wellbeing of older people. Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge provides a Community Befriending service to help tackle the causes of isolation and loneliness. The service aims to enable older people to: • Overcome the barriers of isolation & loneliness • Share the company of others • Talk to someone • Pursue interests & activities in the local community • Maintain independence and gain information & enable clients to stay at home longer Befriending is for older people who are lonely and need a friendly chat and a bit of company. This is part of our paid for Independent Living Service. Please contact the office for more information.

title: Community Cooking & Healthy Living Course
address: Making A Difference To Maidstone, 48-49 Church Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME14 1DS What we do Discover the joy of healthy, affordable cooking with our new 6-week Community Cooking Course. Designed for residents in Maidstone who are managing health conditions like diabetes or hypertension—or those simply looking to improve their diet—this hands-on program turns the kitchen into a place of creativity and confidence. Using a fun, “Ready Steady Cook” style format, you’ll work with a professional chef from Blue Arrow Recruitment to create delicious meals using ingredients provided by The Bread and Butter Thing. Whether you are a beginner or looking for fresh inspiration, our small group sessions (maximum 6 people) ensure you get plenty of personalized support and a chance to meet new people in a friendly, relaxed setting. To help you bring your new skills home, all participants are referred to the Celebrate Family Food project, which provides six weeks of ingredients and recipe cards for your household. Dates, times and location to be confirmed.
price: Free Organisation MADM Making A Difference To Maidstone

Breakfast can also be provided if required. There are activities running which you can join in with or just sit, relax and catch up with friends. Our qualified staff provide care and support as needed throughout the day. Price Unknown
categories: Day centres and Dementia; Groups and Community Activities

title: Community Meals
address: Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford, Sandford House, Stade Street, Hythe, CT21 6BD What we do It’s estimated that one in ten people over 65 are at risk of malnutrition but, in most cases, malnutrition and dehydration are largely preventable. Our Community Meal service is available 365 days a year and has been a vital lifeline to many older people. The service provides a freshly prepared two course meal at lunchtime, hot and ready to eat, and our staff and volunteers provide a welfare check. The team get to know our clients and their regular visits can brighten their day. Our hot lunch comes with a home cooked dessert and cost £9.00. An optional tea time pack can be added for an extra £5.50*. *Please note, the tea time pack currently cannot be ordered without the hot lunch. This service is available 7 days a week and operates across Ashford, Folkestone and Hythe. Visit our website to find out more information.
price: £9.00 Organisation Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford Telephone 01303 269602 Email admin@ageukhla.org.uk Organisation Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford

title: Community Support Service
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do 21 Together is dedicated to supporting families and professionals to provide the best possible support for children and young people with Down’s syndrome in Kent. Whether it is support in pregnancy, early years, transition to school, education, health and care plans or how to navigate the health and care systems; we can help you. We have trained staff available to talk through your concerns and support you to access the right services for your family. We can help with support during pregnancy, helping to connect you with the Down’s syndrome community for peer support, help accessing services and signposting opportunities, support with the learning profile of a child with Down’s syndrome, support for inclusion of a child with Down’s syndrome, education, Health and Care Plan support and help understanding health, care and education systems. To access this service, please visit our website here and complete the form and someone will then be in touch.
price: Free Organisation 21 Together Telephone 01622 230720 Email info@21together.org.uk Organisation 21 Together

title: Community Table
address: Fusion HLC, 2 & 3 Cobb Way, Off Livingstone Walk, Park Wood, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 9XF What we do Each week 40 people can have access to a hot meal. There are up to 30 dine in spaces available and up to 20 takeaway spaces available. Take aways can be delivered if the client is in Park Wood; housebound for any reason, has a lack of mobility or has difficulty with transport. The way the service works is the community table dine in list gets a text on a Monday and the first 30 people to respond get a dine in meal and then a collection takeaway is offered.
price: Free Organisation Fusion HLC Telephone 01622 691177 Email enquiries@fusionhlc.org.uk Organisation Fusion HLC

title: Community Transport Service
address: Dover District, Folkestone and Hythe and Thanet plus surrounding villages. What we do Kent Coast Volunteering provides an essential, volunteer-led community transport service for the Dover District, Folkestone and Hythe and Thanet plus surrounding villages. This service is for people aged 65+ who are unable to use public transport, and provides access to essential medical appointments and social outings through our volunteer driver service. If you are interested in becoming a Volunteer Driver, we would love to hear from you. Our drivers, and the service they provide, are invaluable to the community. You can find more information on our website . Get in touch to find out more about any of our services or volunteering opportunities.
price: £25per year, plus mileage costs to volunteer drivers and a journey admin fee of £3 to KCV Organisation Kent Coast Volunteering Telephone 01304 367898 Email info@kcv.org.uk Organisation Kent Coast Volunteering

title: Compassionate Neighbours
address: Heart of Kent Hospice, Preston Hall, Aylesford, ME20 7PU What we do Compassionate Neighbours provides free, community-led, social, and emotional support for anyone who is socially isolated and/or lonely and living with an end-of-life illness and/or frailty (including carers). You do not need to be a patient/carer of the Hospice. Could you give just 1 -2 hours a week to offer friendship and support to a person in your local community affected by terminal illness or frailty and experiencing loneliness or social isolation? The project works by matching local people based on shared likes, experiences and interests. Volunteering as a Compassionate Neighbour is a rewarding role in which you build genuine friendships with local people who are socially isolated. We encourage you both to catch-up together, either on the phone or in person, at least once a week. As a Compassionate Neighbour, you’ll need to be aged 18 and complete an enhanced DBS check. You will be provided with a full training programme. The training courses are informal, interactive and give you the opportunity to explore how to use your compassion and time to help others. Don’t miss this opportunity to become part of an award-winning network of more than 1,750 Compassionate Neighbours nationwide and do something amazing in your local community! To find out more about becoming a Compassionate Neighbour volunteer contact CN@hokh.co.uk
price: Free Organisation Compassionate Neighbours (Maidstone, Tonbridge and Malling) Telephone 01622 792200 Email cn@hokh.co.uk Organisation Compassionate Neighbours (Maidstone, Tonbridge and Malling)

title: Computer Training & Support (Sevenoaks & Tonbridge)

title: Connect with Nature
address: Online What we do An online wellbeing programme! If you love nature and would like to improve your wellbeing – join our free 8 week online group programme where we explore nature together in a friendly and relaxing setting. Enjoy connecting with nature and other like minded people through a series of 8 short online group sessions where we will discuss and learn about different nature-based topics in a relaxed and informal manner. In between the sessions you will have the option to take on small, easy, and enjoyable nature challenges set for you during the group discussion. This programme is open to anyone and courses run regularly throughout the year. For more information or to sign up please email cmp@khwp.org.uk
price: Free Organisation Kent High Weald Partnership Email cmp@khwp.org.uk Organisation Kent High Weald Partnership

title: Volunteering for nature
address: Dunorlan park, Barnetts wood , Hilbert wood, Southborough common, Cinderhill woods. What we do Kent High Weald Partnership runs a weekly volunteer programme at local nature reserves and greenspaces in and around Tunbridge Wells. Tasks vary throughout the seasons and include coppicing, hedge laying, tree planting, meadow management, boardwalk construction, and much more. Any adult over the age of 18 is welcome to volunteer and all tools, gloves, and instruction are provided so you do not require any previous experience. Tasks are every Wednesday and Thursday all year and run from 10am to 3pm across various sites. During the winter, hot drinks will be provided but all volunteers should provide their own lunch and drinking water. Suitable outdoor clothing and footwear is essential. All new volunteer must contact KHWP before attending using the contact details provided below. Please do not hesitate to contact us with any questions. Our volunteer group is very friendly and welcoming and we look forward to meeting you! Please contact us if you are interested in joining: cmp@khwp.org.uk
price: Free Organisation Kent High Weald Partnership Email cmp@khwp.org.uk Organisation Kent High Weald Partnership

title: Cooking For Wellbeing
address: Blackthron Trust, St Andrews Road, Maidstone, ME16 9AN What we do Manage your health from the inside out with this Cooking for Health & Wellbeing course. Held in our professional training kitchen at Blackthorn Trust, this 8-week program is designed for individuals living with diabetes, hypertension, or respiratory conditions. Under the guidance of our experienced chef, you will learn to create nutritious, seasonal meals—often using fresh produce grown at Blackthorn. Our chef will discuss your specific health goals with you before the course starts to ensure the recipes are tailored to your needs. You’ll learn how to cook healthy dishes on a budget and leave each week with recipe cards to share with your family. When? Please contact us for information about availability.

title: Coping With Life DGS
address: Dartford, Gravesend, Swanley What we do Managing Depression Our 6-week Managing Depression course will focus on understanding depression and its interrelationship with our emotions, thoughts and behaviours, what we can do to challenge our negative thoughts and tips to staying well and maintaining our mental wellbeing. Managing Anxiety Over the course of 6 weeks, we will be working to understand anxiety, how it affects our daily routine and how we can empower ourselves to regain control over our emotions and challenge our anxiety. We will also be looking at ways to improve our self-esteem, improve our problem solving skills and relaxation skills to maintain mental wellbeing. Improving Self-Esteem, Confidence & Assertiveness A 6-week course centred around understanding what causes and maintains low self-esteem, how to improve our own self-worth, gain more confidence and teach strategies to become more assertive, such as learning how to say no and not be taken advantage of. Our course teaches participants how low self-esteem can result in poor mental wellbeing, poor decision making and destructive relationships and the skills we need to break that cycle. Stress Management and Relaxation Techniques During our 6 week course, we will be looking at practising relaxation techniques, how we can apply strategies into our daily routine and how to identifying the root causes of our stressors, such as work, family, health, finances, the things that affect us all, and how we can manage stress more effectively. Mindfulness Mindfulness is a concept that allows us to pay attention to the present, rather than being stuck in the past or worrying about the future. Over 6 weeks, we will learn how to apply mindfulness into our everyday life, understanding our thoughts and feelings and learning how to pay attention to the world around us. Mental Wellbeing Matters We would go to the doctors if we had a poor physical health, so why do we struggle when it comes to our mental health? Mental Wellbeing Matters is a new course focused around our mental wellbeing, how we maintain good mental health, looking into empowerment, resilience, self-care and self-expression.
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email wellbeing@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Cottage Hospital
address: Cottage Hospice, Five Ashes, Mayfield, TN20 6HY What we do Cottage Hospice is a home from home for patients and those important to them. At Cottage Hospice, patients are cared for by their loved ones (family caregivers), with support from Hospice staff and specially trained volunteers. Often, families are best placed to provide the care for their loved ones, but doing this at home can be daunting or distressing. At Cottage Hospice, families continue to care for their loved one in the same way that they would at home, but with extra support just outside their door 24 hours a day should they need it. Cottage Hospice is the first of its kind in the UK and has recently been awarded an Outstanding CQC rating.
price: Free Organisation Hospice in the Weald Telephone 01892 820515 Email enquiries@hospiceintheweald.org.uk Organisation Hospice in the Weald

title: The Hospice in the Weald Counselling & Support Service
address: Hospice in the Weald, Maidstone Road, Pembury, Tunbridge Wells, TN2 4TA What we do The Hospice in the Weald Counselling & Support Service offer their services free of charge to patients, families, and carers. If you are interested in counselling or would like to find out more you can contact us directly. Counselling sessions are 50 minutes long and usually take place weekly in one of our counselling rooms at the Hospice. However, we are a flexible service and the number and regularity of sessions can be discussed and arranged with you. Counsellors are required to make brief session notes and these are confidential within the organisation. This could only change if there is a risk of someone being harmed. However, this will always be discussed in a counselling session at the earliest opportunity. To book an introductory appointment, ring the Counselling & Support Service on 01892 820525.
price: Free Organisation Hospice in the Weald Telephone 01892 820525 Email enquiries@hospiceintheweald.org.uk Organisation Hospice in the Weald

title: Suicide & Co Bereavement Counselling Service
address: Virtual What we do We have built an incredible service that offers 12 sessions of free counselling, plus an assessment ahead of counselling, which is delivered either over webcam or telephone. The service is accessible to anyone bereaved by suicide, 18+ living in England and Wales, who is not in other professional support. We start the service once the person has passed their 6 month loss anniversary, due to the effectiveness of counselling at this time. You can apply via the application form linked below. Someone will be in touch within 48 hours to discuss your application.

title: We Stand counselling Services
address: Virtual What we do Our free counselling service provides a safe, confidential, non-judgemental space to help parents and carers feel supported and listened to. We offer face to face counselling at our London office and online counselling nationwide. We understand the impact that sexual abuse can have on you and your family. You may find yourself in an isolated position trying to deal with life changes and cope with feelings of loss, guilt, anger and anxiety. Counselling can help you process what has happened and empower you to move forwards and make positive changes in your life. Our one-to-one counselling sessions give you space to: Talk and be listened to Explore your experiences Understand your feelings Our counselling service is staffed by trained and experienced volunteer counsellors and counsellors on placement. To access our counselling service please call our free national helpline on 0800 980 1958
price: Free Organisation We Stand Telephone 0800 980 1958 Email support@westand.org.uk Organisation We Stand

title: Coxheath Walk
address: Coxheath Village Hall, Stockett Lane, Coxheath, ME17 4PT What we do Meet in the car park of Coxheath Village Hall, Stockett Lane, Coxheath, ME17 4PT at 0950 to leave at 10:00. These walks are all easy and readily accessed starting out from the centre of the village and can take in any of several attractive local woodland footpaths with good views of the surrounding countryside. They are all on safe terrain, led by trained Walk Leaders at a pace that will accommodate all on the walk. They last between one hour and an hour and a quarter. The walks take place on the second and fourth Friday of each month through the year. Start time: 10:00 am Estimated finish time: 11:15 am

title: Creative groups and activities
address: St Justus Church in Rochester: The Fairway, Rochester ME1 2LT What we do Every Wednesday from 13:00–15:00 at St Justus Church, we host creative workshops and intergenerational activities to foster self-expression, confidence, and community connection. Young people – including unaccompanied asylum seekers – can take part in arts and crafts, upcycling projects, handmade crafts, cooking classes from different cultural backgrounds, and collaborative community projects. Fortnightly Food Parcels After the Wednesday sessions, participants receive fortnightly food parcels, supporting young people with access to nutritious meals, including culturally appropriate and halal options, while encouraging community engagement. title: Criminal Justice Liaison and Diversion Service (CJLDS)
address: The Red House, Oakapple Lane, Maidstone, ME16 9NW What we do CJLDS provides early identification and screening of vulnerable people of all ages within the criminal justice system. The team adopts a multi-disciplinary approach consisting of nurses, social workers, a youth specialist, a speech and language therapist, consultants, psychology and support workers. The service screens for all health and social vulnerabilities that may be contributing to increased contact with the criminal justice system. The team, where appropriate, will support individuals through the criminal justice system and where eligible, provide follow up in the community to support access to services and resources to meet their identified needs. The team will make referrals to appropriate care providers when necessary and link in with existing care providers to ensure clear pathways for follow up. Based on screening/assessment, CJLDS practitioners offer advice and guidance to police officers, Magistrates and other colleagues within the criminal justice system, to help determine the most appropriate level of support and outcome for each person.
price: Free Organisation Criminal Justice Liaison and Diversion Service Telephone 0800 197 5542 Email kmpt.cjlds@nhs.net Organisation Criminal Justice Liaison and Diversion Service

title: Counselling
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 38 – 48 Marsham Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME14 1HH What we do Crossline offers open-ended counselling on a one to one basis for individuals or couples within a confidential and safe environment. There is an initial assessment for one hour to understand the situation you would like counselling for and it is also an opportunity for you to ask questions and decide whether Crossline suits your needs. Crossline currently charges £35 per session for individual counselling and £50 per session for couple counselling. However, our ethos is that we do not want finances to be a barrier to people accessing counselling and this is discussed at the assessment. Our counsellors are able to work with a range of issues helping people find positive and lasting change. The service is open Monday to Friday 8:30am – 6pm. If you would like to find out more about our counselling services or to book an assessment, visit our website here or call 01622 754447 and one of our coordinators will be happy to talk with you.

title: Crossroads Young Carers Project
address: The Oast, 170 Tonbridge Rd Wateringbury ME18 5NS What we do The young carers project supports young carers who are caring for a family member who suffer from a long term illness, disability, substance misuse or a mental health issue. We support children and young people between the ages of five and eighteen. We offer them respite time in the form of regular group sessions with fun activities, arts and crafts, cookery, games sport and workshops, these are shared with their young carer peers. During the school holidays we provide respite trips to various venues, a trip to Leeds Castle, the beach, bowling, cinema, swimming, Adventure Island and much much more. We run the regular groups in Maidstone, Swale and Tonbridge and Malling after school time. We also provide information, advice and signposting , work closely with schools and Children’s services to offer a holistic approach. We offer young carers support in reaching their potential and nurture their emotional wellbeing and resilience. We have a team of trained staff and volunteers who support the young carers at the group sessions and on all respite trips.

title: CSCS (Construction Skills Certification Scheme)
address: Various locations What we do Level 1 Health and Safety in a Construction Environment – 2 days of 6 hours. Supported by CITB (Construction Industry Training Board) this qualification teaches the principles of risk assessment for maintain and improving health and safety at work, the importance of safe manual handling and working safety at height in the workplace. You will then be supported to book into a test centre in Canterbury, Gravesend, Chatham or Tunbridge Wells to take the CITB operatives Health, Safety and Environment Knowledge test.

title: Cygnet Programme
address: The Howard De Walden Centre, Bluett Street, Maidstone, ME14 2UG What we do This programme is designed for parents and carers of children and young people aged 7-18 with an autistic spectrum condition. The programme is designed to, increase parents understanding of autistic spectrum conditions, help parents develop their knowledge on how a child on the autistic spectrum experiences the world and what drives their behaviour, guide parents through practical strategies they can use with children, direct parents to relevant ASC resources and to give parents the opportunity to meet with other parents who have had similar experiences and to gain support and learn from each other. It’s delivered over six, two and a half to three hour sessions followed by a seventh sessions a few weeks after the end of the course. Please contact us if you are interested in attending a course.

title: Dartford Borough Council One You Kent Service
address: Civic Centre, Home Gardens, Dartford, Kent DA1 1DR What we do Adult Healthy Lifestyle Service commissioned by KCC Public Health. The One You Kent service is a holistic service embedded into the local Councils. Small changes can add up to a huge difference to your health and wellbeing, it’s never too late to start. You don’t have to go it alone – One You Kent is here to help. We have lots of support on offer to help you take the first step towards a better you. Our One You Advisors can support you to make the small changes that can make a big difference. An Advisor can provide you with free one-to-one support to help you to: Lose weight – FREE 12 Week Adult Weight management groups Get fitter – FREE Walk & Talk Groups in Dartford Health Matters Lifestyle Programme – In Partnership with Fairfield Leisure Centre Falls Prevention Sessions Healthy Living Centre Dartford – Get more involved in your community, Improve your mental wellbeing Get housing, debt and employment advice Quit smoking If you would like a One You Advisor to contact you to chat about the service or to book an appointment, contact the Healthy Living Team on 01322 343067 or online: Contact the Healthy Living Team . Dartford Borough Council – Online Referral Form Adult Healthy Lifestyle Service commissioned by KCC Public Health.

title: Dartford Walk & Talk Groups
address: Virtual What we do Dartford Borough Council Walk & Talk Groups are free, short, accessible and sociable walks that last around 30-90 minutes. They aim to help people get the most out of walking for their physical and mental health, plus they provide a great opportunity to meet new people! The walks are FREE to take part in and are led by trained Health Walk Leaders. They are great for people getting little or no exercise or who are recovering from or suffering from an illness. Walkers of any age or ability are welcome at these weekly walks. Just dress for the weather and wear comfortable footwear. For more information on Health Walks please contact the Healthy Living Team on 01322 343067. Healthy Living Walk Mondays, 10am. This is a transition walk. Various starting and finishing points. Darenth Country Park Walk Tuesdays, 10.30am. Starting and finishing in the Darent Valley Hospital reception area Swanscombe Castle Hill Walk Tuesdays, 12:30. Starting and finishing at The Blue Bean Coffee Co in Swanscombe Castle Hill. This walk is takes about 40-45 minutes. Dartford Heath Walk Tuesdays, 10am. Starting and finishing at Upper Heath Lane. This walk takes you around Dartford Heath. The walk is generally 45-90 minutes. Greenhithe Walk Wednesdays, 10:30am. Starting and finishing at ‘The Place’ Community Café at St Mary’s Greenhithe, London Road. Stone Pavilion Walk Thursdays, 9:30am. Starting and finishing at Lizzie’s Cafe. This walk takes you around Stone and vicinity. After the walk you can meet up for a coffee & chat. Suitable for people who are looking to be more active. Central Park Walk Thursdays, 11:30am. Starting and finishing at the War Memorial outside Central Park, Dartford Swanscombe Walk Fridays, 10am. Starting and finishing outside the Old Fire Station, Church Street, Swanscombe. Swanscombe – Castle Hill Walk Sundays, 9am. Starting and finishing at Castle Hill Community Centre, Cherry Orchard, Ebbsfleet Valley, Swanscombe, DA10 1AD. A 30-40 minute walk around the new development at Castle Hill. The majority of the walk is on paved pathways and is suitable for pushchairs. The walk is run by ‘Active Church’. Walkers are welcome to stay for a light, healthy breakfast and a short interactive presentation on spiritual health from a Christian perspective. https://www.dartford.gov.uk/health-wellbeing/health-walks
price: Free Organisation One You Kent Email https://www.dartford.gov.uk/health-wellbeing/health-walks Organisation One You Kent

title: Day Care
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Our day care service gives older people including the most frail or vulnerable people the opportunity to socialise confidently, benefitting from social integration with like-minded individuals, whilst giving respite to carers and families (fee of £23 per day). Day centre features include refreshments served all day including breakfast, lunch and afternoon tea, fully trained staff to assist with bathing and personal care needs, exercise sessions, art and craft sessions, good disabled facilities and hairdressing and Chiropody services also available at an extra cost (see our services for more details).

title: Day Opportunity Service for Adults with Learning Disabilities
address: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, West Malling, Kent, ME19 5NA What we do Our vision is to empower individuals with learning disabilities to become more independent and lead less isolated, happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives. We believe in embracing change and helping everyone develop the skills to manage it. Participants will have the opportunity to engage in horticulture and other outdoor activities, creative arts such as crafts and woodworking, and life skills training like meal preparation, cooking vegetables, and making jams and chutneys for our farm shop. We also have a Trainee run tuck shop at lunchtime. Trainees may also have the chance to experience performing arts through taster sessions.

title: Day Service – Ashford
address: Farrow Court, Stanhope Road	Farrow Court, Ashford, Kent, TN23 5RF What we do Age UK Hythe, Lyminge, and Ashford’s Day Care service provides older adults with a safe, comfortable, and engaging environment to spend their day. Designed to support even the most frail or vulnerable individuals, the service fosters social interaction with like-minded peers, helping to build confidence while offering much-needed respite for carers and families. Each day includes a variety of morning and afternoon activities, such as seated exercise, new-age curling, art, and singing, along with a freshly prepared two-course lunch and refreshments throughout the day. For those who prefer a quieter experience, there’s the option to relax in the café or other hub spaces instead of joining the main activities. Our team of staff and volunteers are on hand to ensure you have the best experience possible. This service is available Monday to Friday 10am to 2:30pm. You can find out more about this service on our website
price: £29.35 Organisation Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford Telephone 01303 269602 Email wellbeingleadashford@ageukhla.org.uk Organisation Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford

title: Debt Advice
address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, Tonbridge, TN9 1BG What we do Are you struggling to manage your debts? Has the pandemic put a strain on your finances? Now is the time to take action to sort out your finances, before landlords, bailiffs or creditors take action against you. Our friendly advisers are here to help and support you. For the quickest response, please email us your phone number and we will call you back. If you can’t email, please call us on the below number and ask for a Debt Assessment. Advice is offered in Gravesham, Dartford, Sevenoaks, Swanley, Tonbridge and Malling and Tunbridge Wells.

title: Dementia Carers Group (Maidstone)
address: New Line Learning Academy, Boughton Lane, Loose, ME15 5QL What we do When someone experiences a Dementia Diagnosis, it doesn’t just have an impact on the person with the condition – it can have an impact on the people around them who take on caring responsibilities. We know that it is crucial for caregivers to have an opportunity to connect with other people in similar situations. It’s why we offer a Dementia Carers Group. As a part of this group, people have the opportunity to: Make friends with other caregivers who may have similar lived experience of caring responsibilities Discuss how they are feeling about and finding the experience of being a caregiver Ask questions about the support which is available in the local community Find out more from other caregivers about their strategies for coping with their caring obligations This is a safe environment where carers are encouraged to be honest about their experiences and the challenges that they are facing. We believe that supportive and constructive environments are crucial in ensuring caregivers have the help they deserve. When : This groups meets once-monthly on the third Tuesday of each month from 10am – 12pm. Where : New Line Learning Academy, Boughton Lane, Loose, ME15 5QL Booking : Contact traci.collins@ageukmst.org.uk or call 01622 753618
price: Free - suggested donation of £2 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email traci.collins@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Dementia Day Care
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Age UK Tunbridge Wells Dementia Day Care service is suitable for those who are living with dementia but are able, with support, to enjoy spending time with others in a group setting. The Dementia Day Care service combines a safe, enjoyable experience for clients whilst providing the carer with an opportunity to recharge and have a break. At the Day Centre we can offer people with dementia a secure, peaceful environment, activities designed to engage and stimulate, help with personal care if required, clients can socialise with others in small groups, a healthy and nutritious two course hot lunch and refreshments (fee of £65 per day).

title: Dementia Day Care (Maidstone)
address: Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Maidstone, ME15 0AL What we do Are you looking for a warm, friendly space where people living with dementia can feel supported, connect with others, and enjoy engaging activities? Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge’s Dementia Day Care sessions offer a welcoming and uplifting environment where people can spend time together and take part in activities that support wellbeing and enjoyment. Are you looking for a warm, friendly space where people living with dementia can feel supported, connect with others, and enjoy engaging activities? Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge’s Dementia Day Care sessions offer a welcoming and uplifting environment where people can spend time together and take part in activities that support wellbeing and enjoyment. Each session includes a variety of activities such as: •Games and group activities Reminiscing sessions Guest entertainers and performers Visits from specialist activity providers Michael*, who attends our Dementia Day Care sessions, shares: “I like having a chat and knowing that the staff are always here if we need them. They are good people and help me with problems. A short while ago I had a problem with my computer since everything is online now. I’m not a fan of technology but they helped me to find a solution.” Our caring team thoughtfully plan each session to ensure there is a balance of activities, conversation, and relaxation – ensuring people feel mentally, emotionally, and socially engaged. A full-day session costs £48, including lunch and refreshments. Transport by minibus may be available for an additional £17. Please let us know when making your enquiry if transport is required. When : Various Where : Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Maidstone, ME15 0AL Booking : Contact referral@ageukmst.org.uk or 01622 753618 to book.
price: £48.00 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email referral@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Dementia Day Care (Tonbridge)
address: Town Lock Day Centre, Lockside, Lyons Crescent, Tonbridge  TN9 1EA What we do At our Town Lock Day Centre in Tonbridge, we are proud to provide a quality, professional, person-centered Dementia service’s for people experiencing a range of memory concerns, whether formally diagnosed with Dementia or wanting to proactively reduce their symptoms. Dementia Day Centre is focused on understanding each individual and their needs, and personalised care is delivered through stimulating activities ranging from arts and crafts and reminiscence to sing alongs from 9.30 am – 2.30 pm. Please come along to our Town Lock Day Centre, Lockside, Tonbridge TN9 1EA or contact Faydra Mitchell Tel: 07846 238925 Email: faydra.mitchell@ageukmst.org.uk for more information.

title: Dementia Day Care
address: Sandford House, Stade Street, HYTHE, CT21 6BD What we do Age UK Hythe, Lyminge, and Ashford’s Dementia Day Service provides a supportive and engaging environment for individuals living with dementia, offering them a chance to socialise in small groups, participate in stimulating activities, and enjoy a nutritious two-course lunch with refreshments. This service not only ensures a safe and enjoyable day for your loved one but also provides carers with an opportunity to take a well-deserved break. Our peaceful and secure setting is designed to foster comfort and connection, with tailored activities that promote engagement. We also offer guidance to carers by signposting them to additional support services. If you think this service could benefit the person you care for, we will arrange a meeting to discuss this further. Please contact us on the details below or visit our website for more information .

title: Dementia Day Centre
address: Hawkhurst House, Heartenaoak Road, Hawkhurst, TN18 5EF What we do WRAP is a small friendly day centre for people with dementia and Alzheimer’s disease. We are open Tuesday and Thursday 9.30am – 3.30pm, with a two course lunch. Our experienced dementia trained staff work hard to get to know every person and make sure that they feel welcomed, comfortable and able to join in as they wish. Everyone experiences dementia differently, so we tailor all our activities to meet each persons needs. WRAP’s manager is a Occupational Therapist by profession and all activities are designed to help stimulate and maintain cognitive and physical skills and provide friendship, social interaction and mutual support.
price: £55 Organisation Hawkhurst Community Hospital Telephone 07471 240269 Email wrapdds@gmail.com Organisation Hawkhurst Community Hospital

title: Dementia Days
address: Meadowside Day Centre, Meadowside, Dartford, DA1 2RZ What we do Our Dementia Hub supports older people in North West Kent who are living with dementia in a welcoming, fun, safe and supportive environment where people can build new friendships and benefit from a variety of cognitive stimulation methods. The Dementia Hub is run from the Meadowside Centre in Dartford and is available on Mondays and Fridays from 9am to 4pm. The Dementia Hub is designed to support older people in the earlier stages of living with dementia, helping people feel valued, accepted and an integral part of the community, as well as providing a much needed respite break to their carers. The Dementia Hub promotes a person-centered approach where individuals with mild to moderate dementia take part in meaningful and stimulating activities, proven to help maintain memory and mental functioning. On a daily and weekly basis we provide a programme of facilities for reminiscence and memory therapies, discussions, activities, games and quizzes, arts, crafts, dining and relaxation. Physical activity and exercise are encouraged including access to our garden areas. Contact us to find out more.
price: Costs vary Telephone 01634 572616 Email enquiries@ageukkentrivers.org.uk Telephone 01634 572616
description: Our Dementia Hub supports older people in North West Kent who are living with dementia in a welcoming, fun, safe and supportive environment where people can build new friendships and benefit from a variety of cognitive stimulation methods. The Dementia Hub is run from the Meadowside Centre in Dartford and is available on Mondays and Fridays from 9am to 4pm. The Dementia Hub is designed to support older people in the earlier stages of living with dementia, helping people feel valued, accepted and an integral part of the community, as well as providing a much needed respite break to their carers. The Dementia Hub promotes a person-centered approach where individuals with mild to moderate dementia take part in meaningful and stimulating activities, proven to help maintain memory and mental functioning. On a daily and weekly basis we provide a programme of facilities for reminiscence and memory therapies, discussions, activities, games and quizzes, arts, crafts, dining and relaxation. Physical activity and exercise are encouraged including access to our garden areas. Contact us to find out more. title: Dementia Peer Support Group (Maidstone)
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham Street, ME14 1HH What we do We’re proud to provide a peer support group for people who are living with early-stage Dementia or Alzheimer’s. This group provides the opportunity to: Make new friends who have shared life experiences Discuss how you’re feeling about your Dementia or Alzheimer’s diagnosis Ask questions about the support which is available Find out more about how other people are coping with their diagnosis This is a safe space where people can be open and honest about their experiences living with cognitive conditions. We understand that the experience of a diagnosis is different for everyone, but having a space where people are encouraged to talk openly is invaluable. Our Dementia Peer Support Group is hosted at Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH. When: 2nd and 4th Thursday of every month from 11am – 2pm. title: Dementia Support
address: Safeharbour Wellbeing Centre, Coldharbour Road, Northfleet, DA11 8AE What we do If you have been diagnosed with dementia or you are supporting a friend or family member with dementia, we can help you. We empower people affected by dementia to carry on their chosen lifestyle. We equip people with knowledge and practical support to live well with dementia and we also understand the impact of dementia on family and friends. We will partner with you and discuss not only what your needs are but what you want out of life, make plans for the future and help you achieve this. We will help you to create an individual support plan and work with you to achieve the results you want. We can meet with you at your home, at our Safeharbour Memory Wellbeing Centre or somewhere else where you feel comfortable, alternatively we can discuss the support you need over the phone or via email. We will help you understand your diagnosis of dementia and signpost you to the right support for you. This service is free and anyone can refer to us. If you live in Dartford, Gravesham, Swanley, Medway, Swale or West Kent and want our support please contact us on 0800 035 2221 or email referrals@adss.org.uk
price: Free Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 0800 035 2221 Email referrals@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

address: Derwent Life Tonbridge Baptist Church, Darenth Avenue, Tonbridge, TN10 3HZ What we do For seniors with low to medium needs, our age range is 65 to 97+ years young! We are an active group, and each day consists of a chatty hour at 10am, followed with a morning activity, a two course freshly prepared nutritional meal. title: Design It Yourself: Online 3D CAD for Absolute Beginners
address: Virtual What we do Turn your ideas into reality with our hands-on, online 3D CAD workshop. Whether you’re a complete beginner or looking to develop your digital design skills, this course will give you the confidence to create functional 3D models for printing, prototyping, or personal projects – all from the comfort of your own home. What You’ll Learn and Do Software, Hardware & Materials Overview (30 mins): Understand 3D printing technologies, materials, and the tools you’ll use. TinkerCAD Demonstration (1 hr): See how easy it is to design with this beginner-friendly, browser-based tool. TinkerCAD Practice (1 hr): Create and edit your own 3D models with guided support. Ultimaker Cura Overview (30 mins): Learn how to simulate your print, and explore key concepts like overhangs, supports, and bed adhesion to ensure successful printing. Fusion 360 Demonstration (1 hr): Explore more advanced design tools for complex projects. Fusion 360 Practice (1 hr): Apply what you’ve learned to create functional designs and assemblies. Design Considerations (20 mins): Gain practical tips for fixings, magnets, and preparing models for real-world use. Location This is a fully online course – join from anywhere with a computer and internet access. The course will be hosted via Jitsi, a free, browser-based video conferencing platform. It runs entirely in your web browser – no downloads or sign-ups required. Invitations will be emailed nearer the date of the event – please check your spam folder. You will need a computer with internet access. A microphone is recommended so you can ask questions during the session. This workshop will be running on the following dates: 19 th of November 22025 17:00 – 22:30 18 th of February 2026 17:00 – 22:30 22 nd of April 2026 17:00 – 22:30 17 th of June 2026 17:00 – 22:30 19 th of August 2026 17:00 – 22:30 24 th of October 2026 17:00 – 22:30 9 th of December 2026 17:00 – 22:30 Booking to be made via Eventbrite – https://www.eventbrite.com/e/design-it-yourself-online-3d-cad-for-absolute-beginners-tickets-1554662079129?aff=oddtdtcreator

title: Digital Buddy Service
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Digital exclusion is an issue for many older people. Being able to get competitive utility deals, Zoom loved ones, bid for more suitable social housing or simply find out about what’s going on in our area is something many of us take for granted. At Age UK Tunbridge Wells knows that teaching anything to anyone is always much more successful when there is a relationship and trust between trainer and trainee. They also know that it takes more than one or two isolated sessions to really embed a new skill. So to help you get online, Age UK Tunbridge Wells has combined it’s Befriending Project with their Digital Inclusion work to create a new programme of remote support (for 55+).

title: Digital Employability Experience
address: Online What we do Unlock Your Future with DEX – The Digital Employability Experience DEX is a free online platform designed to help Kent residents aged 13+ build digital and employability skills. Whether you’re in school, changing careers, or seeking work, DEX offers a chance to explore career paths, gain practical skills, and boost confidence. Aligned with the Government’s Essential Digital Skills Framework, users learn to communicate online, manage information securely, and solve problems using digital tools—all through real-world scenarios and resources from local employers. Practical Skills, Real Opportunities DEX delivers interactive workbooks that simulate workplace environments. Users practice using digital tools, explore industry sectors, and earn certificates for completed tasks—ideal for CVs and job applications. The platform also supports development of key interpersonal skills like time management and adaptability. With its focus on closing the digital divide and preparing Kent’s workforce, DEX is a vital tool for anyone ready to take the next step in their career.
price: Free Organisation Kent County Council Telephone 03000 410950 Email digitalex@kent.gov.uk Organisation Kent County Council

title: Digital Skills and Support
address: Invicta House, KCC, Maidstone, ME14 1XX What we do Digital Kent is working to improve digital inclusion and capabilities in the county of Kent. Formed by Kent County Council in 2021 and part of the Helping Hands scheme and Financial Hardship programme, the Digital Kent service has been created as a response to the COVID-19 (Coronavirus) pandemic. Together, working with partners, the services and support that we are providing is currently time-limited, due to the nature of the funding we have available. We can offer digital support to individuals who require it, whether this be navigating websites, helping use a device or upskilling on simple tasks online. We can offer support on a 1-1 basis as well as through our group sessions being ran across the county. Find out more at: https://www.digitalkent.uk/information/projects/engagement

title: Disability Boccia
address: YMCA Maidstone, Melrose Close, Cripple Street, Maidstone, ME15 6BD What we do Boccia is a target ball sport which tests both muscle control and accuracy. From a seated position, players propel balls to land as close as possible to a white marker ball, known as the Jack. Balls can be rolled down a ramp, thrown or kicked. If a player is unable to release the ball with their hands, they can use assistive devices, such as a head pointer. We also have a fully equipped disability changing facility here which offers the customer a more comfortable place to change, as well as meeting the ‘Changing Places’ standards. The equipment includes a height adjustable adult sized changing bench and an electronic tracking hoist system. The area also includes a ‘non slip’ floor and wet room shower. Carers accompanying a user with a disability are entitled to FREE entrance into the facilities.

title: Domestic Abuse
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Riverside creates positive outcomes for women, men and children affected by domestic abuse through the services we provide. We operate specialist refuges for women and children fleeing domestic abuse, as well as working with perpetrators to change their abusive behaviours. Each of our customers is appointed a personal Riverside support worker to create a bespoke, structured support plan. Our aim is to empower those fleeing domestic abuse by equipping them with the necessary skills and knowledge to move on. Our support starts with equipping, those who face domestic abuse, to recognise abusive behaviour and understand how to keep themselves safe.

title: East Kent Walk and Talk Sessions
address: Virtual What we do Walk and Talk sessions are short, local walks designed to help you to get active, get outdoors and meet new people. They are led by friendly volunteers and usually take from 30 minutes to an hour and a half, leaving from central locations across east Kent, such as your local library. We have multiple walks across east Kent including, Dover, Deal, Ramsgate, Ashford, Canterbury, Whitstable, Margate, and Tenterden. Why is walking the perfect activity for health? Almost everyone can do it You can do it anywhere and anytime It’s a chance to make new friends It’s free and you don’t need special equipment You can start slowly and build up gently. Walking can: Make you feel good Give you more energy Reduce stress and help you sleep better Keep your heart strong and reduce blood pressure Help you manage your weight. There’s no need to book, just turn up and join in! Find your next walk on the website . Please contact the walk leader directly with any questions or contact the Walk and Talk Team directly rather than any venues listed.

title: Ecology Island
address: Darenth Country Park, 68 Darenth Park Ave, Dartford DA2 6NN What we do Ecology Island is an award-winning nature-based intervention for those living in the Dartford, Gravesham, Swanley and Medway! Participants get to take part in a range of nature-based activities which allow them to learn new skills in a supported environment, outdoors in nature. Examples include bush craft, campfire cooking, scrub clearance, wildlife ID, litter picking, bird and bat box building, wildflower planting and more. Ecology Island groups are run weekly at Darenth Country Park on Monday’s at 10-1:30 and you can self-refer via the website .
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email wellbeing@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Ecology Island
address: Darenth Country Park, Darenth Park Avenue, Dartford DA2 6NN What we do Wellbeing Gardens is a partnership project between North West Kent Countryside Partnership and Dartford Borough Council. The project enables people with mental health issues to join together to look after a garden site in Darenth Country Park, Dartford. Every week, the group improves the habitat for wildlife and grows fruit and vegetables in the garden. title: ellenor Complementary Therapies
address: ellenor, Coldharbour Road, Gravesend, DA11 7HQ What we do At ellenor, Complementary Therapies are used alongside conventional treatments with the aim of providing psychological and emotional support through the relief of symptoms. Complementary Therapies available include: Aromatherapy, Hypnotherapy, Massage, Reflexology, Relaxation and Reiki. Complementary Therapy is not an alternative to medical treatments, but rather works alongside them to promote feelings of relaxation and wellbeing. title: Employment Essentials
description: Employment Essentials – mix and match courses to gain the skills you need. Available online or face-to-face. All modules are 2 hours Personal Power and Confidence – Engage with your most confident, successful self and explore strategies to build confidence. Recreate moments from the past that bring out your best, most capable self. Uncover Your Genius – Transferable Skills What do you love? What sets you apart? What makes you unique? Use Mind Maps to identify skills, strengths, values and needs of specific jobs to inform decision making and applications. How to change jobs or work areas whilst benefiting from the experience you have. Personal Profiles and Tailored CVs – Look at what makes a great personal profile and how to tailor your CV to each application. Use the job description to tailor your CV. Hunt the Hidden Job Markets – Make social media work for you, making the most of online resources. Finding those jobs that are not advertised. Agency Work – Is it for You? – Working for an agency – what to expect and how it differs from a permanent position. Rights and responsibilities. Testing Times – What are Psychometrics? – What are they? Why are they used? Practice tests / Personality Tests / Situational judgement tests / Aptitude tests – numerical, verbal, inductive and deductive reasoning tests. How to Ace that Video Interview – Interview preparation and the differences with a Zoom interview. Stay Positive while Job Searching – Introduce ways to help you stay positive and be at your best while looking for a job. Explore areas that range from self-care and keeping healthy, to new ways of developing skills and confidence. Skills in Action – Interview Practice – Build confidence by looking at common interview questions and thinking about our own answers. Use strategies to make a strong first impression, explore stress management specific to interviews. Discuss and demonstrate answering common interview questions. title: Energy Advice
address: Registered address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, TONBRIDGE, Kent, TN9 1BG.     (But open to anyone from Sevenoaks, Swanley, Dartford Gravesham, Tonbridge & Tunbridge Wells). What we do If you live in North & West Kent and are struggling with energy costs or need energy advice, we can help you. Our experienced and specially-trained advisers can provide free, tailored energy consultations either over the phone or via email. This one-to-one support can include: ● Assessing energy spending and how to reduce your energy usage. ● Clarifying complicated or confusing energy bills and helping with meter reading. ● Exploring your eligibility for any energy grants or useful services. ● Discussing your options with energy debts ● Advising on energy efficiency improvements and grants that can help cover the costs. We can also provide a free Carbon Monoxide detector if you do not have one, alongside an advice session about how to protect yourself from Carbon Monoxide leaks.

title: Equine Assisted Services – Skills Based Learning or Therapeutic
address: AAHEP, High Lees Farm House, Wagon Lane, Paddock Wood, TN12 6PT What we do Spending time outdoors with animals is extremely therapeutic and has been proven to have neurological benefits as well as settling effects on breathing and heart rates and reducing the production of cortisol. Mindful based interactions provide an important personal skills such as self-awareness, social confidence and resilience as well as those which involve the care and wellbeing of the horse provide the opportunity to develop a deep connection with the participant’s own needs, such as self-worth and self-care. With a wide range of registered skills across the team means that AAHEP can offer both direct therapeutic support as well as learning activities framed around positive psychology such as developing mindfulness and personal emotional literacy, awareness and management. This activity is suitable for younger people, home education groups, vulnerable groups and people with learning difficulties. As well as corporate and team building groups. Each session is between 60 and 90 minutes and prices range from £65 – £95 and can be tailored for groups. Please contact for more information.

title: Equine Therapy
address: 39-48 Marsham St, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do Hearsay is delighted to offer Equine Therapy, a service suitable for children, young people, and adults. This one-on-one programme is led by a qualified facilitator of TheHorseCourse, a programme accredited by the Professional Standards Authority (PSA). TheHorseCourse provides a unique learning experience where participants develop eight core resilience skills through engaging tasks with horses, promoting mental health and well-being. Through equine therapy, individuals can enhance their communication, self-control, problem-solving and accountability while also boosting self-esteem, confidence, empathy, flexibility and independence.

title: Hi Kent Equipment
address: Hi Kent Head Office, 18, Brewer Street, Maidstone, ME14 1RU, & Hi Kent Head Office, 46 Northgate, Canterbury CT1 1BE What we do If you pay your Council tax to Kent County Council and are deaf or have a hearing loss, then you are entitled to a statutory assessment of your needs and to have equipment provided for you in your home on a free-of-charge long-term loan basis (free after assessment or also available to purchase outright). Hi Kent provides equipment if you are aged 18 years old or over and you do not have major sight loss (a dual sensory need), and you live in the KCC council tax area. Visit the Hi Kent website at hikent.org.uk for more information.

title: EU Settlement Scheme
address: Registered Address, Kemp House 160, City Road, London, EC1V 2NX What we do Migrant Help continues to provide advice and support with the applications to the EU Settlement Scheme. The deadline for most people to apply to the EU Settlement Scheme was 30 June 2021. If you or your family are from the EU, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland or Liechtenstein, you can still apply if you or a family member were living in the UK by 31 December 2020. You must also either: • meet one of the criteria for a later deadline to apply • have ‘reasonable grounds’ for not applying by 30 June 2021 Migrant Help offers this service in Sussex, Surrey, Kent, Birmingham (West Midlands), Stoke-on-Trent, Leicester, Coventry, Liverpool and Lancashire.

title: Every Mind Matters
address: Virtual What we do We all need good mental health and wellbeing, it’s essential to living happy and healthy lives. It can help us sleep better, feel better, do the things we want to do and have more positive relationships. It can also help us deal with difficult times in the future. If you’re suffering with stress, anxiety, low mood and sleep issues, the Better Health every mind matters website ( https://www.nhs.uk/every-mind-matters/ ) has lots of information to help you take self-care actions, as well as self-care tips for young people ( https://www.nhs.uk/every-mind-matters/mental-wellbeing-tips/youth-mental-health/ ).

title: Everyone health – Outreach Stop Smoking service
address: Across Kent County What we do Everyone Health Kent’s Outreach Stop Smoking Service is a community-based service dedicated to supporting individuals in quitting smoking. We offer free group sessions and one to one session either in person or virtually to help people on their smoke-free journey. Our support includes the supply of free Nicotine Replacement Therapies for those aged 18 and over. For young people aged 12 – 18 , we provide advice, training, and educational sessions on the impact of smoking to help them make informed choices about their health. Our service is accessible and tailored to the needs of the local community, ensuring that individuals receive the right support to successfully quit smoking and lead a healthier life.

title: Exercise and Socialise (Maidstone)
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham St, Maidstone ME14 1HH What we do Aged over 50? Why not come and enjoy a fun-filled exercise session aimed to help with posture, balance and overall wellbeing. We are currently running a Tai Chi class at the community centre in Marsham Street Maidstone. Exercise and Social classes are for older clients to help prioritise health and mobility through tailored, low-impact activities, emphasising functional movements and balance. These classes are provided in a supportive environment contributing to a fulfilling and active lifestyle. We are currently running a Tai Chi class which is a holistic exercise that addresses physical, mental, and emotional well-being. Its gentle nature helps promote overall health and quality of life. Before starting any new exercise program, it’s advisable for older adults to consult with their healthcare provider, especially if they have any pre-existing health conditions. It is also a chance to meet like minded people and enjoy a chat over a cup of tea or coffee. When : Fridays from 9:30am – 10:30am Where : Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham St, Maidstone ME14 1HH Booking : Contact info@ageukmaidstone.org.uk or 01622 753618 to book.

title: Explore Enterprise
address: Registered address: The Prince's Trust, 8, Glade Path, London, SE1 8EA What we do Do you have a business idea? or are you looking to start a business? We can help turn your big ideas into a reality. Over four days you’ll get to meet like-minded people. You also have the chance to tap into a business adviser, covering everything from business planning and marketing to sales, budgeting and tax. After the four days you will be provided with a mentor to support you as you plan and test your ideas. You will even have the opportunity to apply for a ‘will it work’ funding grant. Clients must be between the ages of 18 and 30.

title: Extra Care – Housing and Support
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Riverside is a leading provider for a range of housing, care and support services to people with varying levels of need and circumstances. Our extra care housing gives older people or adults with care needs, the choice to live in their own home with dignity and access to innovative, high quality and flexible service provision. Purpose-built, our extra care housing is an alternative to conventional care homes. You’ll have your own private accommodation, in one of our schemes which cater for couples or single people. As and when your care needs change over time, so too can our level of care and support. This means you can remain independent and have the peace of mind that our dedicated staff are on hand to provide you with the care you need.

title: Family Law Advice Line
address: Virtual What we do We can advise you on, domestic violence and abuse, divorce, finances and property on relationship breakdown, cohabitation, finances and property on relationship breakdown, parental responsibility and arrangements for children lesbian parenting. You can contact us for initial advice or at various stages of your legal case. You can call whether you have a solicitor or not. We offer free legal advice to women in England and Wales. title: Fast Track Counselling
address: Dartford | Medway | Online What we do The Service The Fast Track Service at North Kent Mind is delivered by fully qualified and experienced counsellors. It is a high quality service at modest prices. The service is available to clients by arrangement at various times and locations to suit you. Fast Track Counselling and Therapy is available to anyone in the area, as long as they are aged 18 or over, and prepared to meet the fee. What kind of counselling is available? Our counsellors, from all genders and ages come from different walks of life and different cultural backgrounds. Our counsellors are trained in different modalities and we strive to match the needs of the client with the skills and approach of the particular counsellor. How do I refer? Self referrals must be made by phone or using our online referral form. We will then contact you to discuss your needs, to explain the charging system, and to tell you of how long you can expect to wait before counselling will start. Sometimes we may suggest another service at North Kent Mind which may be more appropriate to your needs. So, how much do you charge? Prices for this service are £40 for an online session or £45 for an in person session The Fast Track Counselling and Therapy Service is designed for people who have counselling needs not able to be met through the NHS service, where long term work is more appropriate or who wish to work further on issues once their NHS-allocated therapy is completed. Aims of the Fast Track Counselling and Therapy Service: · To offer a high quality counselling at a modest price. Minimum age of clients is 18 years. · To provide clients with space and time to work with an individual fully qualified counsellor to meet their individual needs. · To create positive changes in clients’ lives by addressing specific problems, identifying ways of coping, developing personal insight, understanding thoughts and feelings, and considering the implications of actions that may be taken. · To encourage clients to make appropriate use of other services provided by North Kent Mind or other agencies. · To provide a counselling service which is also appropriate to the needs of people with significant mental health issues.
price: £40-45 per session Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email fee-payingservices@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Fibromyalgia Action UK Helpline
address: Virtual What we do FMA UK operates as a signpost to information and resource for people who are affected by fibromyalgia. From our initial beginnings we have seen the range of services and resources increase. What we can offer is closely tied to the level of donations we receive and other funding sources. Some of the services we provide include a National Helplines on fibromyalgia, an online support forum that is available 24/7 with office bearers of the charity being available to help e.g. our benefits advisor helps with benefits queries and sends information regularly and UK wide system of support groups. title: First Aid Courses
address: Various locations What we do Introduction to First Aid – learnings are given an introduction into the techniques of first aid. his course does not qualify people to carry out first aid. This course is 3.5 hours. Level 2 Award in Basic Life Support for Adults and Children – Provides a useful overview of techiniques covering resusciation, burns, bleeding, choking and the recovery position. This course does not qualify people to carry out first aid. This course is 5.5 hours. Level 3 Emergency First Aid at Work – This qualification is delivered through hands-on learning, practical teaching, demonstrations and a theory test. An Emergency First Aid at Work certificate will be issued upon successful completion of the course which is valid for three years. The course is a full day.

title: FitStones
address: The Stones Community Trust, Gallagher Stadium, James Whatman Way, Maidstone Kent ME14 1LQ What we do FitSTONES Habits for Health is a carefully crafted 12-week course that is based on scientific findings and first-hand experience. Participants learn how to put themselves at the centre of their own wellbeing journey through small, daily habit changes, rooted in the four foundational cornerstones of wellbeing: Mindset, Nutrition, Movement, and Lifestyle. Benefit from: 12 week programme consisting of a 2 hour weekly session of lifestyle coaching and exercise. Learn how to make small changes to make a big impact on your wellbeing. Experience different types of exercise and find something new or rediscover something old. title: Flourish Community Gardening Project
address: Southwood Road Allotments, Southwood Road, Rusthall, Kent, TN4 8UX What we do Flourish is a project for adults who are struggling with low to moderate mental health issues such as anxiety or depression. We aim to improve mental health through a series of gardening and nature-based activities. We create a safe environment where everyone is welcome and there is a strong sense of community. This is a hands-on project, gardening and growing primarily fruit and vegetables, and one we believe will be hugely rewarding and beneficial to mental wellbeing. Our clients will be involved in planning an allotment-style garden, preparing the soil, planting and tending to its seasonal needs, with help and guidance from our full time gardening project manager. Find out more on our website.

description: Flourish is a project for adults who are struggling with low to moderate mental health issues such as anxiety or depression. We aim to improve mental health through a series of gardening and nature-based activities. We create a safe environment where everyone is welcome and there is a strong sense of community. This is a hands-on project, gardening and growing primarily fruit and vegetables, and one we believe will be hugely rewarding and beneficial to mental wellbeing. Our clients will be involved in planning an allotment-style garden, preparing the soil, planting and tending to its seasonal needs, with help and guidance from our full time gardening project manager. Find out more on our website. Flourish is a project for adults who are struggling with low to moderate mental health issues such as anxiety or depression. We aim to improve mental health through a series of gardening and nature-based activities. We create a safe environment where everyone is welcome and there is a strong sense of community. Our clients will be involved in planning an allotment-style garden, preparing the soil, planting and tending to its seasonal needs, with help and guidance from our full time gardening project manager. Find out more on our website. Price Free

title: Folkestone Safe Haven MHM
address: 3 Rendezvous Street, Folkestone, Kent CT20 1EY What we do The Folkestone Safe Haven offers a welcoming, safe, comfortable, non-judgmental and non-clinical environment, sometimes known as ‘crisis cafes’. At the safe haven, we will be able to provide information and emotional support if you are in crisis or feel you are heading towards a crisis situation. You can come just for a chat, or you can access one-to-one emotional support from trained mental health professionals. We also offer help in creating staying well and crisis plans, and support visitors to access other services and organisations that may be useful to them. This is a walk-in service – there’s no need to be referred by your GP or Care Coordinator. When : Open nightly, including weekends and bank holidays, from 6 pm – 11 pm.
organisation: Folkestone Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters Telephone 07967877096

title: Food Bank & Support Service
address: Elim Church, 36a Cherry Avenue, Swanley, BR8 7DX What we do The Swanley Community Cupboard is set to become a lifeline for those in need. This invaluable initiative aims to provide essential food supplies and support to individuals and families facing hardship within the local community. Beyond providing food, the initiative also seeks to create a supportive and welcoming environment where individuals can connect with others and receive guidance. Volunteers will be on hand to offer a listening ear and provide information about local resources and services. The goal is to empower community members and help them overcome adversity. We believe that this initiative will not only provide essential food supplies but also foster a sense of belonging and hope for those in need. Open on: Mondays from 10am-3pm Thursdays 10am-3pm Saturdays 10am-12noon pm Visit our website or contact us to find out more.
price: Free Organisation The Community Cupboard Swanley Telephone 07871781624 Email communitycupboardswanley@gmail.com Organisation The Community Cupboard Swanley

title: Food Bank
address: Church of the Good Shepherd, Quarry Hill Road, Borough Green, TN15 8RE What we do The Church of the Good Shepherd operates a weekly food bank, offering support to local families and individuals who cannot afford the essentials of life. Parcels are collected (or if necessary, delivered) each Monday morning. We work with referral agencies to identify those in need. However, emergency parcels can be arranged, as we know life doesn’t always stick to a schedule! To get in touch contact Ian on 07801 135460 Donations : If you would like to donate to the food bank, the church building is open during the day, and non-perishable items may now be left in collection bins in the church porch. You can also donate via other local churches: Borough Green Baptist Church, St Joseph’s Roman Catholic Church, St George’s Wrotham, St Peter’s Ightham and St Mary’s Platt Non-perishable food items continue to be most welcome in supporting local struggling families during these difficult times As always, thank you so much for your generosity, it continues to make a real difference.
price: Free Organisation Church of the Good Shepherd Telephone 07801135460 Email vicar.cogs@gmail.com Organisation Church of the Good Shepherd

title: Food parcels
address: The Vicarage, 19 Worcester Avenue, Kings Hill, Kent, ME19 4FL What we do The St Gabriel’s Food Bank offers help to people living in Kings Hill (or are associated through school/work/activities), West Malling and Leybourne/Leybourne Chase area We are a crises provision for anyone whose income has been drastically reduced, or who are struggling to pay for food at this time due to the cost of living crisis. We are not a shopping service and can only distribute what we have in stock at any time. We are able to offer an initial 6 weeks worth of help (mainly taken weekly or bi weekly). This is due to current rising demands. Food parcels consist of the same or similar dry/canned food each week, and some other necessities for the number of people in your family. When we receive fresh fruit/veg/bread we also distribute that. To request a food parcel, please complete a request form on our website: https://www.kingshillchurch.org.uk/food-parcels/
price: Free Organisation St Gabriels Church on Kings Hill Telephone 01732 844194 Email secretary@stgabrielskingshill.org.uk Organisation St Gabriels Church on Kings Hill

title: Foot Care
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do The foot care service is run by trained independent healthcare professionals. They can check your feet for any problems, such as pain or swelling, help with cutting nails, removing dead or cracked skin and corns. This service is for anyone over the age of 55 and is available for home visits as well as clinic based. You will need to make an appointment and there is a charge for the service but we endeavour to keep this well below what you might pay for other private providers. Service cost £30.00 per clinic visit.
price: Set charge Organisation Age UK Tunbridge Wells Telephone 01892 522591 Email info@ageuktw.org.uk Organisation Age UK Tunbridge Wells

title: Foot Care (Maidstone)
address: Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Linton Road, Maidstone, ME15 0AL What we do If you are over 50 and in general good health, but are unable to manage looking after your feet, then we are here to help! Try our foot care service to help you to stay comfortable on your feet. We often take our feet for granted, but our feet are so important for our mobility and general wellbeing. By taking routine care of our feet, serious problems can usually be avoided (as well as reducing the risk of trips and falls) however, activities such as simple nail cutting can often become more challenging as we age. Our foot care service is run by trained healthcare professionals. They can check your feet for any problems, such as pain or swelling, help with cutting nails, removing dead or cracked skin and corns. The service is for anyone aged 55 and over. We have footcare clinic locations: – MCSC Marsham St ME14 1HH – Staplehurst Youth Club TN120BJ – Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Linton Road, ME15 0AL – Fusion Park Wood ME15 9XF – Rose Water Place Langley ME17 3ZB – Cavell Park Care Home Sittingbourne Road ME14 3EN – Crossroads Care Wateringbury ME18 5NS – Whether you are calling for yourself or on behalf of someone you know, you will need to complete a referral form and register with the service, we will then get back to you to arrange your first appointment. Please ensure you have the persons permission before making a referral for somebody else. To do so you can either call us on 01622 753618 or email office@ageukmst.org.uk.
price: £28 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email office@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Foot Care (Sevenoaks)
address: Hollybush Court, Hollybush Close, Sevenoaks, TN13 3UX What we do It’s important to take good care of your feet and give them a little TLC. As you get older your nails grow more slowly meaning they become thicker, more brittle and harder to cut. Our foot care service is here to assist and maintain good foot health with a nail trim and foot soak, leaving your feet softer and more comfortable.Our trained, professional staff will welcome you into a private room for your treatment at one of our community venues. If you need some help to remove your shoes and socks they can assist and will make sure you are comfortable for your treatment. Each treatment takes 30 minutes and includes a nail trim to toenails and soothing hand/foot cream to moisturise and prevent skin dryness or itchiness. The service is for anyone aged 55 and over. Whether you are calling for yourself or on behalf of someone you know, you will need to complete a referral form and register with the service, we will then get back to you to arrange your first appointment. Please ensure you have the persons permission before making a referral for somebody else. To do so you can either call us on 01732 454108 or email office@ageukmst.org.uk
price: £28.00 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01732 454108 Email office@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge
