title: Fort 4 Youth Foundation
address: Suite 2118 Ground Floor, 6-8 Revenge Road, Lordswood, Kent ME5 8UD What we do If you are aged 16–25 and from a diverse community or are an unaccompanied asylum seeker, Fort4youthFoundation is here for you. We understand that moving through new places, cultures, and experiences can sometimes feel overwhelming especially if you have faced trauma, loss, or major changes in your life. With us, you’ll find a safe and welcoming space where you can talk about what’s on your mind, build coping skills, and connect with others who understand. You can choose one-to-one support, join peer groups, or take part in creative activities that help boost your confidence and wellbeing. You don’t have to go through challenges alone. Fort4Youth Foundation walks beside you, helping you heal, grow stronger, and build a brighter future. How to get help Our Step-by-Step Path to Support and Empowerment Here’s how Fort-4-Youth Foundation walks alongside you at every step, providing support, guidance, and a path to well-being. 1. Contact Us Our hotline and online chat make it easy for youth and families to connect with us. We also offer initial consultations at community centers and schools, providing comfortable and accessible ways to reach out. 2. Assessment and Service Matching We conduct a caring and culturally sensitive intake assessment to understand each person’s mental health needs. Based on this, we develop a personalised plan and match them with services that best fit their unique journey. 3. Support and Follow-Up Our support doesn’t stop after the first visit. We follow up regularly to adjust plans as needed, ensuring ongoing care and improvement.

title: Digital Skills Training Sessions
address: Various community venues across West Kent What we do Compaid charity offer one to one Digital Skills Training for disabled, older or otherwise vulnerable adults living in the Sevenoaks, Tonbridge and Malling and Maidstone borough areas. Our dedicated trainers work with clients on a one-to-one basis, providing digital skills training that is tailored to their specific needs and objectives. Training takes place at various venues in West Kent and ‘Remote’ sessions may also be available if clients are unable to travel. They can learn basic skills or improve the skills they already have, at their own pace whilst in a supportive environment. They can use their own devices – but it’s not a problem if they don’t have any, as loaned equipment and access to the internet may be provided. title: Free Home Energy Support
address: North Kent, West Kent, Medway, and Swale. What we do Free Home Energy support for people affected by dementia. If you or someone you care for is living with dementia, you can access free, friendly home energy support through ADSS, who are working in partnership with SGN. We’re here to help you keep your home safe, warm, and energy efficient. Our Home Energy Service offers: Simple, practical advice to help you save energy and lower bills. Support with carbon monoxide safety and staying protected at home. Help signing up to the Priority Services Register for extra peace of mind. Guidance on spotting scams and staying safe. Connections to additional energy and benefits support. You can get support whether you’re the person living with dementia or a carer – even if you live at different addresses. Our service is available across North Kent, West Kent, Medway, and Swale. Get in touch with Kelly, our Home Energy Advisor, to find out more. 01474 533990 HomeEnergy@adss.org.uk

title: Free Legal Advice Clinic
address: Hill End Camp, Eynsham Road, Cumnor, OX2 9NJ What we do Our Legal Advice Clinic is aimed at filling a significant gap in legal services, encouraging stability and promoting justice for families impacted by parental imprisonment. A team of pro-bono solicitors, covering areas such as family, employment, housing, immigration and criminal law provide free 1:1 legal advice to adults caring for children with a parent in prison. The focus is on the family members outside, and not the person in prison. This service is open and free to anyone caring for children with a parent in prison, from any organisation. If you would like to access this service, please get in touch at info@childrenheardandseen.co.uk

title: Free Support to Get Healthier and Feel Better
address: One You Kent, Town Hall, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 1RS What we do Free support is available to people who would like to lose weight, move more, drink less and quit smoking across Kent. These lifestyle changes can help people to keep healthy and feel better, both physically and mentally. This support is available in a range of locations across the borough as well as online support so the client can choose whichever suits them best. After referring in, the client will be contacted within 2 working days, and be offered an initial telephone consultation. This consultation will establish what support is most suitable for them which could include 1:1 lifestyle, or group weight management support. To refer into our service, complete the online referral form: https://kentcc-self.achieveservice.com/en/AchieveForms/?form_uri=sandbox-publish://AF-Process-03b8a8e2-ab38-4fc9-a833-74457399da43/AF-Stage696c8cd3-42b0-4c6b-a821-2d25a0a32499/definition.json&redirectlink=%2Fen&cancelRedirectLink=%2Fen

title: Fuel Poverty Help and Support
address: All across Kent What we do As part of our service, we have a helpline that’s free to call where our team can give valuable tips on how to stay warm and well at home, energy debt advice and support, help to arrange free home energy visits, sign up those in need of additional support to the Priority Services Register and access grants for energy efficiency measures. When available, we can offer small measures to those who need them, as well as emergency support to eligible residents facing immediate energy hardship. We understand the physical and mental effects of living in a cold home, and we feel it’s more important than ever to ensure everyone is living in a warm and comfortable household.

title: Garden Gate Bereavement Support Group
address: Communigrow, East Malling, Communigrow, UK What we do Fridays 10.30am-12.30pm This is a free 2 hour session on Friday mornings, 10.30am-12.30pm, for people who feel that a community gardening experience may help them in their time of loss. title: Gardening (Maidstone)
address: At home What we do We are able to offer an affordable gardening service to those aged 55 and over living in the borough of Maidstone. Our Gardening Service can help with a variety of jobs within the garden. Our Gardeners can help with many jobs including low-level hedge and lawn cutting, pruning and cut back shrubs and trees, weeding and cleaning paths & patios. All our Gardeners will have completed a satisfactory DBS check (Criminal Records Check) and thorough references will have been obtained. They all carry identification and none of them will visit you without a pre-arranged appointment. Please note, we are unable to dispose of any garden waste. If there is something special that you need help with that is not on this list, please do not hesitate to contact us and enquire.

address: ESO Teaching Clinic, 104, Tonbridge Road, Maidstone, ME15 8SL What we do At the ESO Clinic we offer quality care for the entire family. title: Gentle Exercise Group
address: Blackthron Trust, St Andrews Road, Maidstone, ME16 9AN What we do Led by a qualified fitness trainer who specializes in health-related exercise, these sessions provide a slow, steady, and safe way to get your body moving again. Initially starting with 30-minute sessions and gradually building up over 8 weeks, the program focuses on the five essentials of wellbeing: cardiovascular fitness, strength, balance, flexibility, and coordination. When? Please contact us for information about availability. What to Expect Expert Assessment: A pre-course review to ensure the movements are safe for your specific condition. Structured Sessions: A guided 5-minute warm-up, intervals of exercise and rest, and a soothing cool-down. Progression: Gently increasing challenges.

address: Kent Wildlife Trust, Tyland Barn, Sandling, Maidstone, ME14 3BD What we do We are very fortunate at Kent Wildlife Trust to be joined by a large, friendly team of volunteers who help us in many different ways. From working on our nature reserves to greeting those who come to our Visitor Centres or helping young people to engage with wildlife they are all making a huge contribution to the work of the organisation. We truly value the time and commitment of all of our volunteers and work hard to ensure that they have a brilliant experience with us. We aim to ensure that volunteers benefit from spending time with us, whether that’s gaining new skills, using existing ones, meeting new people or spending time outside. title: Green Doctors Energy Advice Consultations
address: At the home of the client What we do For over 19 years we’ve been helping residents stay well and warm at home, and save money on their household bills. These are some of the ways we can support you when you sign up for a free phone consultation: Advising on suppliers or tariffs including explaining statements, energy caps, standing charges and unit rates. Advice on reducing energy use at home. Supporting with accessing the Warm Homes Discount or Priority Services Register. Support with applications for energy or water debt relief. Referring you for the installation of larger measures if needed, and help to access and apply for funded retrofitting. Referring you to other support organisations within your community if needed – such as food parcels, fuel vouchers, white goods. Delivering small efficiency measures, such as LED lightbulbs, draught proofing, powerdown units, carbon monoxide alarms, warm kits, power cut kits and more! Our consultations take place for free on a one-to-one basis to help you make the most out of your personal energy systems. The quickest direct referral route is via the website and click ‘free consultation’. Freephone : 0800 233 5255 – we encourage you to leave us a voicemail! Email : Greendoctorsouth@groundwork.org.uk

title: Green Letter Days
address: Various What we do Green Letter Days are dementia-inclusive day trips designed to help people living with dementia and their carers stay active, connected and engaged with the outdoors. Activities include cricket, cycling, boating, golf, garden visits and other shared experiences in welcoming and supportive environments. The days are carefully planned to reduce anxiety and build confidence, while encouraging social connection, enjoyment and wellbeing. Green Letter Days help families create positive memories together, reduce isolation and continue experiencing adventure, activity and community after a dementia diagnosis. When : these are scheduled throughout the year Booking : For more information contact Danine Irwin on 01245 237548 or email info@dementiaadventure.org

title: Group Writing Therapy
address: Online What we do This group is called Slant! after Emily Dickenson’s advice. Write all the truth but write it slant. Sometimes it is easier to sneak up behind your experience of yourself and tell it by re-writing a fairytale, writing as an object saying what they have seen or writing a shopping list of needs or wonderings. If you would like to heal by using your creativity, gently at your own pace, in a supportive group that listens to your words with their hearts then get in touch to find out more and sign up. When : Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday between 10am – 11:30am in blocks of ten throughout the year. Where : Online via zoom Find out more on our website.

title: Groupwork
address: Virtual What we do Meeting other men who have gone through similar experiences to you can be profoundly helpful. A group of survivors is a safe, confidential space where you can tell your story, share experiences, learn from other people’s coping strategies and really feel like you are not alone. We run a variety of groups, all there to support you emotionally, however some are specialist in areas to meet a range of diverse needs. This service is available to anyone in the UK. Most groups are held online. You can sign up for monthly group email updates by making a request to the groups team at groupwork@survivorsuk.org

title: Grow a healthy Swanley
address: Harvestfield Allotments, Daltons Road, Crockenhill, BR8 8EL What we do Grow a Healthy Swanley’ initiative incorporates community led crop growing projects in the local area. Harnessing an interest in healthy lifestyles and enabling the principles to create a healthier community. The project is currently being delivered in Crockenhill and we have plans to include Swanley. Crockenhill Parish Council has allocated 2 allotment plots for the project and these have been handed over and signed for. The beneficiaries of the project are both the local people who are tending and growing the fruit and vegetables on the allotments and eventually the recipients of the produce grown. The project offers those members of the community who may feel isolated by having difficulties socialising to engage with others. The group meets at Harvestfield Allotments on Wednesdays and Saturdays from 10am until 2pm. Please contact Jackie if you would like to join the group or find out more.

title: Growing Food and Gardening
address: Communigrow Centre, Park Farm, East Malling/Ditton, ME20 6PE What we do Join an exciting project and get involved in growing/gardening sessions with our Gardening Team and volunteers. These sessions include sowing, planting, potting, repotting, watering, weeding, harvesting, DIY and supporting seasonal tasks. This is an opportunity to support a community project, the environment; and also, to meet new people, learn new skills, keep active and improve your wellbeing. Growing sessions run between Wednesday and Friday (10am – 2pm). No previous experience is required. Our project members say they really enjoy outdoor activities and having somewhere peaceful and safe to come. Everyone is welcome but people who need special care or with additional needs will be requested to come with a relative, carer or tutor.

title: Headache Clinic
address: ESO Teaching Clinic, 104, Tonbridge Road, Maidstone, ME15 8SL What we do Our specialist Headache Clinic was founded to help those suffering with benign yet debilitating persistent or chronic headaches, which they have had for over 6 months. (It is not suitable for new onset headaches not previously seen by a doctor.) Consultations are provided by final year student practitioners, closely supervised by an experienced headache-trained osteopathic tutor. The team carry out recognised safety screening procedures and a thorough assessment, followed by tailor-made advice and, where appropriate, treatment; often this means working hand-in-hand with the patient’s own or specialist health professionals. The usual price for treatments is: New Patient £30, Continuing Patient £23 or evening £26. Please see our website for more information https://bcnogroup.ac.uk/clinics/eso-kent/
price: Set charge Organisation European School of Osteopathy Telephone 01622 685989 Email info.eso@bcnogroup.ac.uk Organisation European School of Osteopathy

title: Heal a Generation
address: The Workshop, 32-40 Tontine Street, Folkestone,  Kent , CT20 1JU What we do Heal A Generation CIC is a UK-based Community Interest Company providing trauma-informed coaching, leadership development, and community support focused on breaking cycles of generational trauma. The organisation offers 1:1 coaching, group programmes, workshops, retreats, educational resources, and a podcast designed to help individuals, parents, and leaders build healthier patterns, emotional resilience, and ethical leadership practices. Its approach combines personal development with community impact through responsibility-based, trauma-aware support.

title: Health and Safety, Manual Labour and Warehousing Courses
address: Various locations What we do Health and Safety in the Workplace – Level 1 and 2 are available and give a general introduction and understanding of health and safety, and the hazards and risks in the workplace. Level 1 and 2 are full day courses. Level 2 Award in Safe Moving and Handling – An introduction to the hazards and risks involved in the safe lifting and carrying of items. Outlines what is expected from a manual handling assessment and how to develop safter handling techniques. This course is a full day. Level 2 Principles of COSHH (Control of Substances Hazardous to Health) – designed for those who use substances hazardous to health at work. Introduces substances and the risks and controls available, and outlines what to expect from COSHH assessment. This course is 5 hours.

title: Hearing Aid Aftercare Clinic
address: Kent Wide What we do Hi Kent runs aftercare clinics for NHS hearing aids in towns, villages and communities throughout Kent. title: Hospice care Help Hub
address: Virtual What we do The Help Hub is a key feature on the Hospice in the Weald website. It is a searchable bank of information which patients and those important to them can browse through, or search, to find information about specific symptoms or other topics. The Help Hub meets the needs and concerns of patients and their loved ones at any time of the day or night. It is a hub of information that patients can refer to, as well as being able to talk to their Hospice Nurse. Visit the Help Hub at https://www.hospiceintheweald.org.uk/help-hub/

title: Helping You Home – Hospital Discharge Service
address: Registered address: Involve Kent, 30, Turkey Court, Maidstone, ME14 5PP What we do Involve Kent work alongside Maidstone Borough Council and Hospitals in and around Maidstone to provide a Helping You Home Service to assist with works such as those mentioned below. Criteria: Patient must live within the Maidstone Borough We offer a range of services including, but not exclusive to: Changing locks – Key Safes – Installing light bulbs – Moving Furniture – Prevent slips, trips and falls – Smoke Alarms – Stair rail – Home Safety Check – Installing Life Lines (lifeline24) Lifeline install subject to meeting certain criteria. We do not offer grab rails as these are completed by Hospital Technician Service. We can install on behalf of hospital technician service if it is discharge dependent if equipment and recommendations are provided. Alternatively a referral is completed to Adult Social Care and patient is followed up in the community.

title: Here Come The Girls running and walking group (Larkfield)
address: Larkfield Leisure Centre, New Hythe Lane, Larkfield, Aylesford, Kent, ME20 6RH What we do Here Come the Girls is a volunteer-led social running and walking group, designed to provide fun, friendly, and inclusive walking and running opportunities for women locally. We invite women of all abilities, ethnicities and demographics to join our inclusive, safe, accessible walk and running groups. We are all about community, friendships and wellbeing. Our run and walk leaders are very passionate about both the physical and mental health benefits of physical activity and the positive social benefits it brings. title: Hermitage Park Community Centre
address: Hermitage Park Community Centre, 31, Cranford Road, Maidstone, ME16 9FZ What we do Hermitage Park Community Centre offers a Main Hall, Multi-Purpose Creché/Childcare Room, Shapemaster Studio, Kitchen and Multi-Use Games Area (MUGA). There is also a children’s play park on the site, however this is not managed by the Hermitage Park Community Team. Swale Community Leisure (SCL) are a charity that runs leisure facilities across Kent, alongside an extensive social impact and engagement programme. As the operators of the Community Centre, SCL hopes to extend its charitable work to the residents of Hermitage Park and surrounding areas, through provision of high quality physical activity for children and adults alike. Supporting community groups is central to this ethos and epitomises the ethics of SCL. For more information, please visit – https://www.swaleleisure.org/

title: Borrow or hire a wheelchair
address: 36, British Red Cross, Pelham Rd, Gravesend, DA11 0HZ What we do The British Red Cross provides wheelchair hire services across the UK. We offer comfortable, safe, and reliable manual wheelchairs whether you’re looking for self-propelled or transit wheelchairs. Find out about hiring a wheelchair for short term use, the types of wheelchairs available here. The wheelchairs are suitable for anyone over the age of 5 and are available in a wide range of sizes, with fitted footrests as standard and free accessories such as leg elevators, cushions, and stump supports available to improve your comfort (subject to availability). You can hire a wheelchair for short-term use or we may be able to help you with a loan. Wheelchair hire details: wheelchair hire for £22 a week with variable delivery and collection options available at an additional cost. free wheelchair accessories flexible hire periods (minimum hire period 1 week) Making your booking: To hire a wheelchair, you will need to know: measurements of the wheelchair user at their widest point (usually hips and thighs) and add 1” / 2.5cm for comfort weight of the wheelchair user accessories needed Book your wheelchair here. Returning your wheelchair: When your hire period has come to an end, please call us on 0300 456 1914 to extend your hire or email us at wheelchaircollections@redcross.org.uk to book a collection. You will need to include your: booking reference (BK) preferred date of collection (Between Monday to Friday) collection address. Please ensure all items are returned, including your box (if applicable), cushions, leg elevators and footplates. Be aware any missing or damaged items may incur a fee.

title: HoardingUK Helpline
address: Virtual What we do Our helpline support for people with hoarding behaviour has been used by thousands of people impacted by hoarding related issues. title: Home Library Service
address: Kent History and Library Centre, James Whatman Way, Maidstone, ME14 1LQ What we do The Home Library Service takes books, DVDs and CDs to people who find it difficult to get along to their local library. This is usually because of ill health, disability or caring responsibilities, and we aim to be flexible about helping people who would benefit from the service. One of our friendly volunteers will visit regularly to bring books/other items to match your interests and will collect the ones that you have finished with. There is no charge to receive the Home Library Service, and books are delivered free of charge, with a longer loan period than for other customers. There is no charge for reservations from Kent libraries’ book stock.

title: Home Preparation Service
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Our Home Preparation Service team can undertake a range of simple, practical tasks that are not eligible for adult social care funding, but can make the difference in getting patients back home. title: Home Support Service Dartford & Bexley
address: Your home What we do Our Home Support service is designed to support your independence, not replace it. Some people prefer to work side-by-side with our team, while others simply appreciate a bit of extra help. However, you’d like to approach things, your preferences always guide the visit.​ Your wellbeing matters to us, and our trained team is here to help you maintain not only your home, but your dignity, your confidence, and your routine. If your needs ever change, we’ll work with you to adjust the support to make sure it still feels right. Because we know: it’s not just about getting things done, it’s about helping you feel your best in the place you call home. Please note – services in Dartford are limited. Please enquire for more information and availability.

title: HOPELINE 24/7
address: Virtual What we do If you are having thoughts of suicide or are concerned for a young person who might be you can contact HOPELINE247 for confidential support and practical advice. You can also text us on 07860 039967 and email us on pat@papyrus-uk.org. HOPELINE247 is a specialist telephone service staffed by trained professionals who give non-judgemental support, practical advice and information to children, teenagers and young people up to the age of 35 who are worried about how they are feeling or anyone who is concerned about a young person. PAPYRUS HOPELINE247 exists to help young people, children, family, parents, siblings, partners, carers, grandparents, concerned friends, health care professionals, teachers, mentors, education social workers, police, ambulance staff, prison staff, alcohol services and probation workers. Our service is available 24/7 all year round.

title: Hospice Community Team
address: Team is based at Heart of Kent Hospice, Preston Hall, Aylesford, Maidstone, Kent, ME20 7PU What we do The Hospice Community Team provides specialist care, advice and support to patients with a terminal illness in their own home environment. This includes a holistic assessment of needs, symptom management, sign-posting, planning for the future, urgent equipment support, medication advice, and end of life care support. We work closely with your GP and other community/hospice services to ensure patients and their loved ones are receiving the care and support they need. Referrals are accepted from healthcare professionals, or by self-referral (we would confirm medical history with your GP). Depending on circumstances, an outpatient appointment may be offered or a home visit.

title: Hospice Hub: Bereavement Drop-In
address: Magnolia Place, Heart of Kent Hospice, Preston Hall, Aylesford, Maidstone, Kent, ME20 7PU What we do This session is for the family and important people of patients who have died whilst under the care of the Hospice. There is no need to pre-book. Come and speak with someone about your grief, whether you are newly bereaved, or it’s been several months or even up to two years after your loss. Wednesdays, 10:00am-11:30am. At these sessions we aim to provide: Someone to listen to you Signposting you to other bereavement services within the Hospice – Grief Talks group, 1:1 counselling Signposting you to bereavement groups and activities in the locality A chance to talk with a counsellor or trained volunteer in confidence An opportunity to talk to others in a similar situation, for some knowing that they are not alone with what they are experiencing is helpful and comforting. Magnolia Place is a light, airy and cheerful space, where every week our Hospice colleagues and volunteers will offer a range of services, many of which operate on a flexible drop-in basis. Patients and carers can socialise, speak to members of our Drop-in Team, and attend optional supportive sessions. Members of our Drop-in Team include: Our dedicated Hub Co-ordinator Community Palliative Care Specialist Nurse Dementia Specialist Nurse Healthcare Assistant Complementary Therapist Welfare Advisor Chaplaincy and Spiritual Carer Counsellor Social Worker Occupational Therapist and Physiotherapist Hospice volunteers

title: Hospice Hub: Dementia Carers Group
address: Magnolia Place, Heart of Kent Hospice, Preston Hall, Aylesford, Maidstone, Kent, ME20 7PU What we do When someone you love has dementia, it can often feel as if you’re losing them while they’re still with you. The dementia carers group is a support group for carers of people with dementia who are under the care of the Hospice. It is led by our specialist palliative care Social Worker who will help the group members to express and explore their thoughts and feelings, and help them find ways to cope with their sense of loss, and with the challenges of the disease. Magnolia Place is a light, airy and cheerful space, where every week our Hospice colleagues and volunteers will offer a range of services, many of which operate on a flexible drop-in basis. title: Hospice Outreach Service
address: Hospice in the Weald, Maidstone Road, Pembury, Tunbridge Wells, TN2 4TA What we do Many of our patients are in their own homes, or in Care Homes, and this is where they choose to stay and be cared for until the end of their lives. The Hospice Outreach Service Team are there to help and facilitate this for as long as possible, or for as long as the patient wishes. If you wish to access this service, your GP, District Nurse, or other health care professionals may, with your consent, refer you to our Team. Patients and families can make self-referrals (including for bereavement follow up), however your GP would be contacted to confirm medical history. To self refer, please see our website.

title: Wellbeing & Therapeutic Services
address: ellenor, Coldharbour Road, Gravesend, DA11 7HQ What we do Our Wellbeing team helps improve quality of life for patients, carers and bereaved individuals in a relaxed and supportive environment. We offer a range of weekly activities such as support with hobbies, gentle exercise, and relaxation sessions. Patients can attend flexibly, based on their needs and interests. These sessions also offer the chance to meet others, manage symptoms, and stay independent. As part of the service, patients can have a personalised assessment, access clinical advice, take part in creative or social activities, and try out new skills and crafts. Additional support from our Therapeutic Services is also available, including counselling, family support, and Play Therapy for children. We also help patients and families connect with other local services where appropriate. We encourage patients to arrange their own transport where possible. Free parking is available on-site and in nearby residential roads. In some cases, volunteer drivers may be able to help. To find out more or make a referral, visit: ellenor.org/referral
price: Free Organisation ellenor Telephone 01474 320007 Email info@ellenor.org Organisation ellenor

title: Housing Advice
address: Registered address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, TONBRIDGE, Kent, TN9 1BG.     (But open to anyone from Sevenoaks, Swanley, Dartford Gravesham, Tonbridge & Tunbridge Wells). What we do Our experienced and specially-trained advisers can help people avoid homelessness and support people in housing crisis. This support can include: ● Advising and supporting you if you’re facing homelessness. ● Supporting and representing you if you’re facing possession proceedings or eviction. ● Liaising and negotiating with landlords, housing associations and mortgage advisers. ● Offering support with money management, debt, benefits and income maximisation. ● Helping to prevent a situation getting out of control and reaching crisis point.

title: Housing for Younger People
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do For younger customers independent living may represent a move away from the home for the first time. At this point shorter, more focussed, independence solutions may be most suitable for both the individual and their families. Transitional accommodation services can support both young customers and their families throughout this period of change. Aimed at individuals aged 16-25, we offer short-term stays, tasters and respite, to familiarise the customer and family with the service, offering phased entry for customers if they feel this is the right option for them.

title: Housing Support for Tunbridge Wells Residents in Temporary Accommodation
address: Beam, Senna Building, Gorsuch Place, London E2 8JF What we do Are you currently living in Temporary Accommodation in Tunbridge Wells and want to move into a privately rented property? Beam is working closely with Tunbridge Wells Borough Council to support residents to move out of temporary accommodation and into their new home. A dedicated caseworker will help you understand your budget and guide you through the house-hunting process, offering support with landlord applications and navigating the rental market. We can also explore options for help with upfront costs like deposits or the first month’s rent, and guarantor support if needed. Once you’ve secured a tenancy, our sustainment team is on hand to answer any questions as you transition and settle into your new home. Interested in finding out more? Please speak with your housing officer to see if you’re eligible for this support or email helen@beam.org for more information. ** Please note that this service is only available to people placed in temporary accommodation by Tunbridge Wells Borough Council**

title: Idea to Object: A Laser Cutting Experience
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, Marsham Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME141HH What we do Turn your ideas into reality with our full-day, hands-on laser cutting workshop at Maidstone Hackspace. Whether you’re a complete beginner or an experienced maker, this small-group course will give you the skills and confidence to design, cut, and assemble your own custom project – and take it home the same day. What You Could Make Over the years we’ve helped participants create all sorts of amazing pieces, including: Laser-engraved lino art and layered 3D sculptures Lanterns, night lights, and mood lamps Wall art, feature pieces, and personalised name signs Bunting, Christmas decorations, and unique gifts Phone stands and other practical designs For more inspiration, explore our Pinterest board of laser-cut projects. What You’ll Learn and Do Introduction & Demonstration (1 hr): Explore the tools, materials, and software used in laser cutting. Design Your Project (up to 3 hrs): With expert guidance, develop your own design using our professional laptops and software. Cut, Decorate & Assemble (2 hrs): Bring your project to life on our laser cutters and add finishing touches. Future Skills (40 mins): Learn how to apply your new skills to future projects. Location: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham Street Maidstone ME14 1HH This workshop will be running on the following dates: 11 th October 2025 – 09:00 – 17:00 8 th November 2025 – 09:00 – 17:00 17 th January 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 14 th February 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 7 th March 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 11 th of April 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 9 th of May 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 6 th of June 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 11 th of July 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 8 th of August 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 5 th of September 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 10 th of October 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 7 th of November 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 5 th of December 2026 – 09:00 – 17:00 Booking: To be made via Eventbrite – https://www.eventbrite.com/e/idea-to-object-a-laser-cutting-experience-tickets-1548085508429?aff=oddtdtcreator

title: Immigration Advice
address: Registered address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, TONBRIDGE, Kent, TN9 1BG.     (But open to anyone from Sevenoaks, Swanley, Dartford Gravesham, Tonbridge & Tunbridge Wells). What we do Immigration law is complex and very highly regulated and as a result expert advice is not readily available. Our free and expert services help clients navigate the processes so that our clients understand the issues and go on to make the best decisions for themselves. This support can include: ● Helping people to settle in the UK. ● Reuniting families where a partner or parent is abroad. ● Supporting people to make applications to the Home Office and advising them on appeals to Tribunals and Courts. ● Working with vulnerable refugees to help them settle in the UK.
price: Free Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent Telephone 0808 278 7810 Email immigration@nwkent.cab.org.uk Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent

title: Immigration and Asylum Law Advice Line
address: Virtual What we do We can advise you on claiming asylum, the rights of Europeans and their families in the UK, trafficking, immigration law including domestic violence, long residence and family life, financial support options including for women with no recourse to public funds. Call on Monday 10am to 4pm, Thursday 10am to 1pm and 2pm- to 5pm. The provision of immigration advice and services is a regulated activity and can only be undertaken by qualified individuals. We can only advise in English on the law as it applies in England and Wales. The advice line can be extremely busy. Please keep trying to get through. Do not send referrals to the below email address. Helpline Only.

title: In-Patient Ward
address: Hospice in the Weald, Maidstone Road, Pembury, Tunbridge Wells, TN2 4TA What we do The In-Patient Ward at Pembury has 15 individual rooms for symptom control and end of life care. We offer personalised care to suit each patient’s physical, emotional and spiritual needs, as well as caring for and supporting those important to the patient. Without seeing it for yourself, it can be difficult to imagine what our In-Patient Ward is like. It’s often described by patients and their loved ones as a calm, tranquil and peaceful place to be – almost a haven. Some rooms face out onto beautiful apple orchards, whilst others open into our stunning Hospice gardens. We will always be flexible and accommodating for each family, whatever their needs.

title: Independent Age – Advice Service
price: Free Organisation Independent Age Telephone 0800 319 6789 Email helpline@independentage.org Organisation Independent Age
description: Helpline team Our Helpline is a free and confidential service for older people and their families, carers and friends. Our areas of expertise are: money and benefits care and support housing options If you have a question or need support, our friendly Helpline team is here to listen. If we can help right away, we will. And if you need advice on something a bit more complicated, we’ll arrange a free chat with one of our specialist advisers. Examples of what we can help you with: Our advisers can support you to: explore whether you qualify for any financial support, like Pension Credit, Housing Benefit, Attendance Allowance and other entitlements apply for benefits or appeal a benefits decision understand your rights as a carer, including your entitlement to carers’ benefits look for grants you may be able to apply for know your rights if you’re a tenant think through your options if you need more support at home make a complaint about social care services We understand that each individual’s needs are unique, which is why the support we offer is personalised to you and your circumstances. We can give you information and support using a translation service, if English is not your preferred language, and we also offer British Sign Language support. You can contact our Helpline team for free and confidential expert advice: by phone on freephone 0800 319 6789 (Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5.30pm, excluding bank holidays) by email helpline@independentage.org text-to-speech, or web chat (available Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm, closed on bank holidays) You can find out more about the different ways of contacting us by visiting our website . title: Independent Age – Free Financial Support Check
price: Free Organisation Independent Age Telephone 0800 319 6789 Email helpline@independentage.org Organisation Independent Age
description: Description: When you reach State Pension age, you could apply for different support, but many older people miss out on extra money they could be getting each week. They may not know what financial support is available, or how to apply. Don’t let that be you. Our Helpline and specialist Advice team can help you find out what you’re entitled to, and how to apply for it. You might qualify for: Pension Credit Attendance Allowance Housing Benefit Council Tax Support And, if you need support to fill in the application form, one of our advisers can help you complete it. This is a free service. Even if you think you’re getting everything you’re entitled to, it’s always worth checking. If you would like to find out what financial support you could claim, you can contact our Helpline on 0800 319 6789 (Monday to Friday, 08.30am to 5.30pm), or email helpline@independentage.org , to arrange a free benefits check. You can find more information about us and the support we provide on our website: www.independentage.org Description: When you reach State Pension age, you could apply for different support, but many older people miss out on extra money they could be getting each week. title: Independent Living Support (Maidstone)
address: At home What we do Maybe you need someone to help you buy your granddaughter’s birthday present, or you’d like a companion for a picnic in the park. With the Independent Living Support service, the decisions are all yours. We can help with things like: Light Domestic Cleaning Laundry Ironing Changing bedding Shopping Dealing with correspondence Prescription collection Befriending We will offer as much or as little help as is required at a time that suits you within the hours the service is available. We tailor the service to your preferences and needs. We visit you at home in the first instance, to discuss the requirements and agree with you the tasks you would like carried out. Our coordinator will then match you to a suitable support worker who will always carry identification, visit at pre-arranged times and carry out the tasks we have agreed with you. All our staff are rigorously vetted including checks via the Disclosure and Barring Service and the Protection of Vulnerable Adults Register. They all receive comprehensive training and are regularly reviewed and monitored to ensure a consistently high quality of service.
price: £23.00 per hour Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Independent Mental Capacity Advocacy
address: Registered Address, PO Box 375, Hastings, East Sussex, TN34 9HU What we do When someone is considered not capable of making their own decisions, it is still important that their views and wishes are heard by those involved in their care and treatment. That is why local authorities are required to provide people who have been assessed as lacking capacity with access to an Independent Mental Capacity Advocate (an IMCA) when decisions are being made about them.
price: Free Organisation The Advocacy People Telephone 0330 440 9000 Email info@theadvocacypeople.org.uk Organisation The Advocacy People

title: Independent Mental Health Advocacy
address: Registered Address, PO Box 375, Hastings, East Sussex, TN34 9HU What we do When someone is admitted to hospital they can be detained under the Mental Health Act. This can be very distressing, and sometimes people who are being detained feel powerless and unable to ask questions or get information. Independent advocacy can help. The support of a trained advocate can help someone understand why they have been detained, explain their rights under the Mental Health Act and ensure their views and wishes are taken into account by professional staff involved in their care and treatment.
price: Free Organisation The Advocacy People Telephone 0330 440 9000 Email info@theadvocacypeople.org.uk Organisation The Advocacy People

title: Independent Sexual Violence Advisor (ISVA)
address: Virtual What we do Independent Sexual Violence Advisors (ISVAs) are trained to provide emotional and practical support to survivors of rape, sexual abuse and sexual assault who have reported to the police or are considering reporting to the police. Our main role is to support around the criminal justice process, but we are independent from the police and we are not legal advisors.

title: Family Matters Independent Sexual Violence Advisors (ISVA)
address: Various locations throughout Kent and Medway, remote sessions are also available. What we do If you find yourself overwhelmed by the amount of information and decisions ahead, uncertain about what to do next, our ISVA team is here to help. They can guide you through the next step and provide support throughout the process. When you have been abused you may feel alone and isolated. You may have lots of questions you need to ask. Our ISVA’s will provide you with emotional and practical support tailored to your needs. You may be considering or already have reported the incident to police. We can help keep you informed throughout the Criminal Justice Process. By working with you and other partners/agencies we will support you to have a voice when it may seem that everyone is speaking on your behalf. Ways in which our ISVA service can help: Provide you with information of Support Agencies within Kent Help signpost you to other Services dependent on your individual needs Clearly explain the Police Process & Legal System Accompany you to the Police station, Court visits and Trial. Help you understand your entitlements and to know your rights Assist you with compensation claims
price: Free Organisation Family Matters Telephone 01474 536661 option 2 Email isva@familymattersuk.org Organisation Family Matters

title: Individual Support
address: Dartford, Gravesend, Swanley What we do A support pathway of 12 weeks providing 1-1 person-centred support with one of our Wellbeing Coaches. Service Users co-produce support plans alongside coaches with goals, life goals and identify actions based on a shared understanding of barriers to recovery, identify a preferred means to achieve them alongside support, and work towards sustainable recovery. Weekly in person support sessions to support the journey to recovery, supporting partnerships, promoting connectedness, and social inclusion. Outside of which there can be calls and virtual ‘catch-ups’. Regular reviews of plans and goals will be undertaken jointly. Home visits where coaches would support those housebound or socially avoidant due to anxiety related disorders via coproduced gradual exposure plans. Walk and talk will be available as integral to all support plans for all individuals, encouraging behaviour motivation, ‘being outdoors’, and general wellbeing. A listening ear would be integral to the support offer, hence all service users can access support outside of booked sessions, although this would not be open-ended. Informal catch-ups in community settings i.e. public cafes, will encompass socialization and inclusion. Self-refer via the website.

title: Information and Advice Service
address: Age UK Hythe, Lyminge and Ashford, Sandford House, Stade Street, Hythe, CT21 6BD What we do Age UK Hythe, Lyminge, and Ashford offers a free, confidential, and impartial Information and Advice service for people aged 55 and over, as well as their families and carers. Our trained and experienced officers provide guidance on maximising income through benefit checks, assistance with form filling, such as Blue Badge applications or Attendance Allowance, and advice on care and support options to help adapt to changes in health or circumstances. This service is available Monday to Friday between 9:30am and 3:30pm. We also offer free information guides and leaflets, which can be accessed in person or downloaded from the Age UK website. While we cannot provide legal, financial, tax, or debt advice, or assist with Power of Attorney or will writing, we can connect you with an appropriate organisation who can. You can find out more about this service on our website

title: Inpatient Unit
address: Heart of Kent Hospice, Preston Hall, Aylesford, Maidstone, Kent, ME20 7PU What we do We provide specialist care for symptom control and end of life care. Our care is individualised to meet the physical, emotional, social, and spiritual needs of the person as well as providing support to their loved ones. Like the rest of the Hospice, our Inpatient Unit is bright, airy and welcoming. It’s a calm and peaceful place, but with its fair share of fun and laughter. Each one of our 10 single rooms has French doors opening out onto our garden or decking areas, where we regularly receive visits from local birdlife. Each room is ensuite and has a TV, DVD player and WIFI. As well as our beautiful garden and peaceful garden room, we have a family room with sofas, large screen TV, dining table and small kitchen. There’s a lounge room with toys and a games console for children, and the Hospice café for when you want to stretch your legs or spend time with loved ones in a more social environment.

title: Inpatient Ward
address: ellenor, Coldharbour Road, Gravesend, DA11 7HQ What we do Our Inpatient Ward at the Hospice in Gravesend provides inpatient care for more than 200 patients per year from the age of 14. We provide specialist care to patients and their families in a warm and safe environment. Our team of staff aims to make stays comfortable by promoting a homely atmosphere, whilst providing the professional care patients need to manage symptoms and improve quality of life. We treat each patient individually and our care is centered around them and their families. Our nurses and doctors are experienced in palliative care and will ensure that care needs are met with dignity and respect. Please see out website for more information or contact us for more information about how to access this service.

title: Inspirations – Empowering service users for Employment & Wellbeing
address: Medway, Gravesend What we do Our Inspirations employment support service is designed to support unemployed individuals aged 17 and over in Medway and Gravesend. Our 8-week group peer support sessions offer a mix of soft and hard skills training to improve employability while also addressing mental health and wellbeing. What We Offer: Mental Health Support: Learn techniques to manage anxiety, build resilience, and maintain overall wellbeing. Employment Focused: Receive help with CVs, job searching, interview preparation, and access to local volunteering and work experience opportunities. Group & 1:1 Support: Engage in group sessions with the option for additional one-on-one support tailored to your needs. Our Aims: Build confidence and readiness for employment. Promote wellbeing and community engagement for service users. Support individuals in accessing further support and encourage involvement in volunteering or work experience.You can self refer via our website!

title: Intermediate Care after Hospital Discharge
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Although not in need of the medical care provided in hospital, there are some people who are unable to be discharged, as their home may not be suitable or there is insufficient community support available. Riverside provides specialised, short-term accommodation that bridges this gap. For individuals leaving hospital we can offer fully-furnished, fully adapted accommodation with 24-hour onsite care. Our emphasis is always on getting home and our specialist housing accommodation is Disability Discrimination Act compliant.

title: Interview Coaching for Unemployed Women
address: Face to Face or Virtual What we do We support unemployed women with free interview clothing and coaching before any upcoming interviews they may have. Every woman will receive one-to-one interview coaching over the phone or via Zoom and a parcel containing a complete outfit for her interview, delivered straight to her door. Alternatively, you can come in to one of our centres where we provide the clothing and coaching to you. We can support women anywhere in the UK with our virtual service. To be eligible for a Smart Works Appointment, a Client must identify as female and be currently unemployed. If the client would like a First Dressing & Interview Preparation session, then they must also have a confirmed upcoming job or apprenticeship interview. To make a referral, please phone us on the number below.

title: INVOLVE craft workshops
address: St Andrew’s Road, Maidstone, Kent, ME16 9AN What we do Our 8-week INVOLVE craft courses are for people over 55 who are feeling isolated. These group workshops have a maximum of 6 people attending each week and are a chance to learn craft skills and chat in a relaxed, small group of like-minded people. These free workshops take place on Thursday mornings from 10.30am to 12.30pm; the courses run for 8 consecutive weeks (please contact us for details of upcoming start dates). People can book places for themselves (subject to availability) – there is no need for a referral from a medical professional, carer or link worker. For more details or to book your place please contact Carrie: carrie@blackthorn.org.uk /01622 828377. Please be aware that, while we can include people who have limited movement or muscle weakness, a certain level of dexterity is required to take part in these craft workshops (we are unable to offer places to anyone in need of 1:1 care).
price: Free Organisation Blackthorn Trust Telephone 01622 828377 Email carrie@blackthorn.org.uk Organisation Blackthorn Trust

title: IPS (Individual Placement Support)
address: Dartford, Gravesham and Swanley What we do If you are experiencing mental health difficulties and are 17 or over and struggling to find or maintain work, we could help you with our Individual Placement Support (IPS) service. We can offer weekly support with job searching, completing application forms, updating your CV, mock interviews, dealing with interview anxieties, identifying suitable opportunities and providing tools to move forward in competitive employment. We will meet you where you are at and will work with you to explore your goals, utilising your strengths to match you with the right opportunities. We will continue to support you once in employment, through continued check-ins and/or support sessions as required. As needed, we can also work with your employer to support them in supporting you. As well as this, it is possible for us to aid you in gaining volunteering, training or education opportunities in order to develop your skills and build your confidence before entering into paid employment. We know that challenges around finding and maintaining work can be stressful, especially if you have existing mental health difficulties. Our IPS workers understand this and are knowledgeable about various mental health conditions and symptoms, and can support you with this. We can help you with learning and coping mechanisms and anxiety management techniques, as well as engaging with employers to ensure you remain supported in your workplace. You can self refer via our website!
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email employmentservices@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

We will continue to support you once in employment, through continued check-ins and/or support sessions as required. As needed, we can also work with your employer to support them in supporting you. As well as this, it is possible for us to aid you in gaining volunteering, training or education opportunities in order to develop your skills and build your confidence before entering into paid employment. We know that challenges around finding and maintaining work can be stressful, especially if you have existing mental health difficulties. Our IPS workers understand this and are knowledgeable about various mental health conditions and symptoms, and can support you with this. We can help you with learning and coping mechanisms and anxiety management techniques, as well as engaging with employers to ensure you remain supported in your workplace. You can self refer via our website! If you are experiencing mental health difficulties and are 17 or over and struggling to find or maintain work, we could help you with our Individual Placement Support (IPS) service. We can offer weekly support with job searching, completing application forms, updating your CV, mock interviews, dealing with interview anxieties, identifying suitable opportunities and providing tools to move forward in competitive employment. We will meet you where you are at and will work with you to explore your goals, utilising your strengths to match you with the right opportunities. We will continue to support you once in employment, through continued check-ins and/or support sessions as required. As needed, we can also work with your employer to support them in supporting you. As well as this, it is possible for us to aid you in gaining volunteering, training or education opportunities in order to develop your skills and build your confidence before entering into paid employment. We know that challenges around finding and maintaining work can be stressful, especially if you have existing mental health difficulties. Our IPS workers understand this and are knowledgeable about various mental health conditions and symptoms, and can support you with this. We can help you with learning and coping mechanisms and anxiety management techniques, as well as engaging with employers to ensure you remain supported in your workplace. You can self refer via our website! Price Free

title: IT and Business Skills
address: Various locations What we do Basic Computer Skills This course offers basic skills for those who have never used a computer before or lack confidence. It includes starting a computer, the terminology, Windows basics, desktop icons and saving your work. Courses run 4-6 hours. Digital Focus: Staying Safe Online This is designed to show you how to stay safe online. You will look at computer safety, viruses and anti-virus software, the need for firewalls and internet security, limiting access for children and what to look out for in email attachments. Course is 3 hours. Digital Solutions: Zoom Basics Learn how to master the online meeting platform Zoom. You will learn how to create a meeting and invite participants, share your screen and manage backgrounds. Courses run 2 hours online. Digital Solutions: Using Social Media This course is designed to raise awareness of new technology and help learners to improve their general use of IT skills and be able to connect with social media. You will learn how to open Facebook, X (Twitter), Instagram, Tik Tok and use WhatsApp to make calls. Course is 3 hours. Introduction to Setting up your Own Business Learners will get an introduction to what it takes to set yourself up in business. It will look at what to consider legally and financially to set up on the best course for success. Course is 4 hours. Introduction to Assertiveness Explore how using assertiveness tools and skills helps you to feel more in control. Recognise how to manage your own feelings when you are challenged with new experiences and feel empowered to say no when you mean it without feeling guilty. You will practice listening and speaking with other s in smal l groups. Course is 4 hours. Level 2 Award in Conflict Management This course provides you with an opportunity to learn some skills to reduce the likelihood of conflict and challenging behaviour occurring in your job or in everyday life. You will learn a range of ways to deal with difficult si tuations, which will develop your knowledge and ability to deal with conflict situations and build your confi dence. Day 1 is 4 hours / Day 2 is 5 hours. Introduction to Conflict Management is also available. Course is 4 hours.

title: Karma Nirvana Helpline
address: Virtual What we do Are you being pressured to get married against your will? Do you fear to bring shame and dishonour on your family? Have you been emotionally manipulated or physically abused? Are you being told to get married because of your religion? Do you worry about being disowned? If you have suffered emotionally or physically, have been disowned or are thinking of leaving home we can help you. If you are LGBTQI and fear the consequences of coming out, we will stand by you. Karma Nirvana does not discriminate between age, gender, sexuality, faith or no faith. Our lines are open 9am – 5pm, Monday to Friday and we are here to listen.

title: Kent Dementia Helpline: Dementia & Me
address: Virtual What we do If you need to talk to someone about Dementia, we’re here to support you. Heart of Kent Hospice is a part of a new Dementia Helpline service across Kent in partnership with Age UK Herne Bay & Whitstable. 0800 500 3014 The Dementia & Me helpline is a free service to help offer support and advice for people living with dementia and their family or carers. Heart of Kent Hospice will answer calls to the helpline from 9:00am-5:00pm Monday-Friday whilst calls out of hours will be answered by Mental Health Matters. The helpline service will: Provide emotional support and advice. Offer an immediate point of contact in a crisis. Refer to Dementia & Me Co-ordinators. Refer to Dementia & Me Post-Diagnostic Services. Signpost to other services and organisations that can assist with dementia support.

title: Kent Karrier Transport Service
address: Kent County Council, Transport Service, County Hall, Maidstone, ME14 1XQ What we do Kent Karrier is a dial-a-ride service. It will take you from your home to set locations, such as the nearest town centre or supermarket. In most areas it is primarily a shopping service, but in some areas (North West Kent and Maidstone) a taxi-style service is available on a shared or individual basis. All services are operated with wheelchair accessible vehicles and our experienced drivers will give you all the help you need. You must join the scheme to be able to use it. Carers or companions can also travel. You will be eligible to access this service if you have a medical condition that makes travelling on public transport difficult OR you live in a rural area more than 500 metres from a bus route or railway station or you are aged 85+.
price: Set charge Organisation Kent Karrier Telephone 03000 413567 Email kent.karrier@kent.gov.uk Organisation Kent Karrier

title: Kent Money Advice Hub
address: Virtual and at various locations across Kent. What we do Are you struggling with the cost-of-living crisis? We can help. The Kent Money Advice Hub offers an opportunity to access money advice at an earlier stage, before you reach crisis point. You can access advice via video call at home or through Kent-wide community kiosks. We can guide you through: ● Options of how best you can deal with your debts or arrears. ● Drawing up a budget of money coming in and payments going out. ● Ways of managing communications and dealing with your creditors. ● Ways to reduce the amount of money you spend. ● Income maximisation through benefits and grants.
price: Free Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent Email www.kentmoneyadvicehub.com Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent

Are you struggling with the cost-of-living crisis? We can help. The Kent Money Advice Hub offers an opportunity to access money advice at an earlier stage, before you reach crisis point. You can access advice via video call at home or through Kent-wide community kiosks. We can guide you through: ● Options of how best you can deal with your debts or arrears. ● Drawing up a budget of money coming in and payments going out. ● Ways of managing communications and dealing with your creditors. ● Ways to reduce the amount of money you spend. ● Income maximisation through benefits and grants. Price Free

title: Kent and Medway Sheds Network
address: Various locations and online What we do Kent and Medway Sheds Network grew from the desire to provide places where people can come together, make social connections, build friendships, share skills and knowledge whilst having fun. WHAT WILL I DO AT A SHED? You can just come for a cuppa and a chat, or you can take part in many of the practical group activities Sheds offer, such as: Woodworking Gardening Community projects Boat repairs Upcycling furniture Social outings Repair cafes Whatever Shed you go to, you will share and learn new skills, as well as support other “shedders” by working with them “shoulder to shoulder”. WANT TO JOIN A SHED? If you are interested in joining a Shed or want to start your own, please contact Kent and Medway Shed Network Email: sheds@activmob.org Tel: 07783 022112

title: Kent Sling & Cloth Nappy Library (Aylesford Farmers Market)
address: Aylesford Farmers Market, The Friars Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, ME20 7BX What we do This is a drop in group for pregnant women, new parents, family and friends to come along and find out more about the benefits of using a sling, carrier or cloth nappies with their baby or toddler. Trained volunteers will work with you to find the best carrier for your family and teach you to use it safely. Slings can be a great parenting tool and can help with calming, bonding and sleeping all while having your hands free. Cloth nappies can help families save money and reduce household waste. These groups are free to attend. We bring a large number of different slings to each event for you to try out, or we can help you with your own sling or carrier. We can talk through the different types of cloth nappies and how to use them. You also have the opportunity to take them home for a small fee each month so that you can have a real test drive. Families receiving Healthy Start vouchers pay no fees. 3rd Sunday of the month 9:30am-1:30pm.
price: Free Organisation PSBreastfeeding CIC Telephone 07951 600777 Email karen@psbreastfeeding.co.uk Organisation PSBreastfeeding CIC

title: Kent & Surrey Bylines
address: 12 Victoria Road, Barnsley, South Yorkshire, S70 2BB What we do Kent & Surrey Bylines is a nonprofit publication which aims to keep people informed about what’s happening around the region, from important news stories to interesting events, things to do and places to visit. It seeks to give people from all backgrounds, whether or not they have previous writing experience, the chance to share their insights, thoughts and ideas. It provides editorial support and is professionally regulated. We are able to take creative contributions for our contributions so please send in articles or photo features about any groups you are involved with or important stories that come to your attention. If you are interested in getting involved or submitting any features, please contact Jennie.

title: Kenward Lodge
address: Kenward Trust, Kenward Road, Yalding, Maidstone, ME18 6AH What we do Kenward Lodge provides supported accommodation for men and women in recovery from substance misuse issues. Residents live independently at Kenward Lodge but are supported by regular contact with Kenward Trust staff. Residents also benefit from continuing to live amongst a group of peers, drawing strength and support from each other and reducing the dangers of isolation. Kenward Lodge helps sustain recovery and reintegration into the community by continuing to provide support until the day the residents return to the community either through support to move on to supported housing or as independent men and women. This service is for over 18’s. Those who wish to access this service or find out more, should visit the website to complete a residential rehabilitation application form.

title: Kenward Therapeutic Community
address: Kenward Trust, Kenward Road, Yalding, Maidstone, ME18 6AH What we do Our programme has been developed based on the principles of a Therapeutic Community, whilst retaining many of the aspects of the Recovery Model of treatment delivery. Alongside the structured group programme, we also offer a wide range of activities with both therapeutic and skills based training in our Social Enterprise, which will allow people to take evidence of recognised training skills with them into the work place. There is also a choice of optional evening and weekend groups and activities. If you would like to find out more about any aspect of a residential project, if you would like to visit or have any other queries, please contact us or visit our website to complete the residential rehabilitation form. This service is for over 18’s only. See website for cost details.

title: Kickstart Futures
address: Barnardos, The Triangle, 2 Coxwell Road, Upper Norwood, London, SE19 3BG What we do The Kickstart Futures Service provides key work support sessions to Care Leavers who predominantly entered the UK either as Unaccompanied Asylum Seeking Children or as Survivors of Human Trafficking. The Service provides a combination of 1:1 and group support sessions aimed at increasing and improving Young People’s independence skills, community integration, self-confidence and mental health. The support sessions may be either home-based through semi-independent homes, or community-based, depending on the needs and circumstances of each individual Young Person. The service can also match Young People with volunteers who act as befrienders and mentors, to support Young People’s development in line with their Support and Pathway Plans. You can find more information about this on our website here.

title: Kith’n’Kin parent support groups
address: Tonbridge Baptist Church, Darenth Avenue, Tonbridge, Kent, TN10 3HZ What we do Parent support groups for parents who are struggling with situations where a child is violent, aggressive or out of control: ie meltdowns, problems in emotional regulation, destructive behaviour. Find support in our groups with parents and facilitators who understand and don’t judge. Find connection, hope and a pathway forward towards change. Break the cycle of violence and stress. Recover your sense of wellbeing and competence. Learn new approaches from lived experience practitioners. Find your tribe. Groups are closed groups running for 10-12 weeks. We run them as often as we have funding. This should be at least 3 times a year. To find out more please conact Helen Genty by email on kithnkincic@gmail.com or by calling 07815442408

title: Knit & Natter Sessions (Maidstone)
address: Invicta Court Care Home, Gidds Pond Way, Weavering, Maidstone, ME14 5FY What we do Are you a keen knitter who would like to meet other people in a welcoming community setting? Or, are you someone who is looking to get out of the house and meet new people? If so, we’ve got the group for you! Our monthly Knit & Natter sessions offer a relaxed space for people living in and around Maidstone to come together, share stories, and enjoy a bit of creativity with yarn and needle. Whether you’re a seasoned stitcher or have no idea what a crochet hook is, don’t worry! Everyone is welcome to join us. The focus of this group is bringing people together to participate in a fun and engaging activity. We kindly ask for voluntary donations where possible to support the cost of the wool provided as a part of these sessions. title: Laser & Lino: Design Your Own Custom Stamp
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, Marsham Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME141HH What we do Turn your ideas into unique, reusable stamps with our hands-on laser engraving workshop at Maidstone Hackspace. Perfect for beginners and crafters alike, this small-group course will guide you through designing, engraving, and mounting your own custom rubber stamp – ready to use and take home the same day. What You’ll Learn and Do Introduction & Inspiration (30 mins): Explore examples of lino and laser-engraved stamps and learn about the tools and materials. Design Your Stamp (1 hrs): Create your own design by hand, digitally, or with AI support, guided by our instructors. Laser Engrave & Mount (1 hr): Engrave your design into rubber, then mount it to a laser cut handle. Stamp & Share (30 mins): Test your stamp, experiment with inks, and take it home for future use. What You Could Make Business branding stamps for logos, packaging, or stationery Intricate artistic designs for crafts and illustration Personal monograms, addresses, or signature stamps Playful children’s designs with animals, cartoons, or characters Personalised event stamps for weddings, birthdays, and holidays Location Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham Street Maidstone ME14 1HH This workshop will be running on the following dates: 22 nd of October 2025 13:00 – 17:00 28 th of January 2026 17:00 – 21:00 18 th of March 2026 13:00 – 17:00 20 th of May 2026 17:00 – 21:00 22 nd of July 2026 13:00 – 17:00 16 th of September 2026 17:00 – 21:00 25 th of November 2026 13:00 – 17:00 Booking to be made via Eventbrite – https://www.eventbrite.com/e/laser-lino-design-your-own-custom-stamp-tickets-1670673412149?aff=oddtdtcreator

title: Learning Disability Advocacy
address: Registered Address, PO Box 375, Hastings, East Sussex, TN34 9HU What we do Our qualified advocates can work with you, or someone you know with a learning disability, to make sure your rights are protected and ensure you can get the services and support you need to live life to the full. Our advocates will listen and talk to you, an other people who are important to you, to try and understand your wishes. Your advocate will speak on your behalf if you want them to and can find out information for you and support you with making choices about the things that matter to you. For example, your home, your day centre, your relationships, your money, your education or work and your health.
price: Free Organisation The Advocacy People Telephone 0330 440 9000 Email info@theadvocacypeople.org.uk Organisation The Advocacy People

title: Legal Services, Advice and Support
address: Virtual What we do Our legal services, advice and support service helps non-abusing parents and carers who have specific questions and require ongoing support. We provide specialist advice and support UK-wide around topics such as schooling, housing, benefits, criminal and civil court cases and child protection procedures. In addition, we offer access to qualified legal advice for families living in England and Wales. Specialist advice and support Parents and carers of children who have been sexually abused often have to navigate a complex system involving multiple organisations. We know how overwhelming this can feel, especially if you feel you are not being heard or understood. Our advice and support service aims to help you understand this complex system, and support you throughout the process. We can advise you on your options, help you to work effectively with other professionals, and write letters and supporting documentation on your behalf for agencies such as schools, housing and social services. Legal advice We recognise that many people struggle to access legal support when going through the family court, and that many lawyers lack specialist knowledge about child sexual abuse. Our legal services manager can offer qualified family law advice for people living in England and Wales. Whilst we cannot represent you in court, we can support you to act for yourself in court, or explain court processes for you if you already have someone to represent you. We can help you to prepare documents and ensure that you understand your rights and what may happen throughout the process. Scotland has a different legal system but the service is still able to offer non-qualified advice and guidance. To access our legal services, advice and support service please call our free national helpline on 0800 980 1958
price: Free Organisation We Stand Telephone 0800 980 1958 Email support@westand.org.uk Organisation We Stand

title: Let’s Cook Together Edenbridge
address: Edenbridge Village Hall, Station Road, Edenbridge, TN8 5LX What we do Learn how to cook healthy meals from scratch with our FREE face-to-face cooking course starting Tuesday 24th February 2026. Our five-week course will help you prepare cost-effective, nutritious and delicious meals, whilst learning how to budget and reduce food waste. You’ll gain confidence through hands-on cooking experience, creating dishes from stir-frys to pasta and soups to curries. All equipment and ingredients will be provided and everyone who completes the course will receive a free recipe book and spice kit. Please contact us to find out more and for course availability .
price: Free Organisation Sevenoaks District Council Telephone 01732227259 Email communities@sevenoaks.gov.uk Organisation Sevenoaks District Council

title: Library Activities and Groups in West Kent
address: Kent History and Library Centre, James Whatman Way, Maidstone, ME14 1LQ What we do Kent Libraries are host to a huge variety of free activities and groups, inclusive of all ages and backgrounds. Groups include reading, (including groups for people with learning difficulties and for people who are visually impaired), ‘meet and greet’ for those who do not speak English as a first language, knit and natter, sewing, crafting, creative writing, family history group, talk time and board games. We also have Hi Kent visit to provide hearing aid cleaning, re-tubing and battery replacement, as well as visits from Citizens Advice to offer free, confidential and impartial advice on a wide range of issues. We also run Storytime for children and their parents, and Spanish club for 5-11 year olds. If you would like to attend one of these groups, please visit this website: https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/libraries/clubs-and-activities-in-libraries
price: Free Organisation Kent Libraries Telephone 03000 412271 Email lindsay.prestage@kent.gov.uk Organisation Kent Libraries

title: Listening Books Online Audiobook Lending Service
address: Online What we do If you live in the UK, and cannot read or hold a book due to an illness, disability, learning or mental health condition, you can become a member of Listening Books. Members can borrow from our collection of over 10,000 fiction and non-fiction audiobooks, as well as access newspapers, magazines and comics. Our books can be listened to via our website, or using the free Libby app on a phone or tablet. Cost: £25 per year, or free to those that can’t afford this cost.

title: Little Stars
address: Tonbridge Baptist Church, Darenth Avenue, Tonbridge, TN10 3HZ What we do If you have a baby or pre-school child to care for, then join us every Monday morning in term time at 9:15 – 11:15 to meet new friends, have coffee and chat and the children can play. Mums, dads, grandparents or carers – you are welcome. Activities include toys, bikes, climbing frames and slides, craft, puzzles, a book corner, and singing . There is also a safe baby area. title: Live Well Kent Housing
address: West Kent and Swale What we do 1-2-1 housing-related support for individuals with a mental health or wellbeing need, who have an issue in relation to/directly impacting upon, their tenancy. Issues can include but are not limited to: • rent and mortgage arrears • risk of homelessness/threat of eviction • repairs • unsuitable accommodation • benefits and debts • anti-social behaviour • domestic violence *This is a floating support offer around tenancy sustainment; therefore, an individual must have an existing tenancy (or own their own home) to be eligible for support. (To clarify – we are unable to support people who are homeless.) *This service is unable to support individuals with student tenancies.
