title: Living Well Community Cafe
address: St Stephen's Church, Waterloo Road, Tonbridge, Kent, TN9 2SW What we do Our Community Cafe is open 3 days a week offering a place of hospitality and warmth to all. On Tuesdays we host a variety of outside agencies who can offer advice on a number of issues, including debt, benefits, homelessness, cancer support, digital skills training etc. On Thursdays we host a Play Space alongside the cafe. Toys, games and activities are available for pre-school children and their carers and the cafe remains open to all. On Saturdays we host the FEAST Community Larder and the Tonbridge Community Crafters and once a month we are joined by The Tonbridge Repair Shop. Open every Tuesday from 10am until 1pm, every Saturday from 10am until 12 noon and Thursdays in term-time only from 10am until 1pm.

title: Living Well
address: Hospice in the Weald, Maidstone Road, Pembury, Tunbridge Wells, TN2 4TA What we do Hospice Day Service is completely free of charge and provides a space that allows patients and those important to them to come and adjust, together and separately, to the reality that they or their loved one has been diagnosed with a terminal illness. There are a number of Groups and activities that you can choose from to suit your needs and goals. To ensure that you achieve these goals, you will get a personalised programme that you and our staff will devise, ensuring you are getting the most from your time at the Hospice.

title: Local Food Bank
address: Wesley Centre, Commercial  Road, Paddock Wood, TN12 6BP What we do The Community Storehouse is Paddock Wood’s food bank, enabling those who have to share with those who don’t have. We all know what it’s like to be anxious, but perhaps we don’t know what it’s like to feel worried about where the next meal is coming from to feed ourselves or our children. You can Self-Refer, by visiting us at The Community Storehouse during our opening hours (Tuesdays 1pm -3pm, Thursdays 9.30-11.30am, and the first Saturday of each month 9.30-11.30am). You are required to bring some I.D. with you, plus any documentation you have, which will assist us in assessing your current need for a food parcel. Examples of recent documentation could include: Job Centre letters Housing Association documents.
price: Free Organisation The Community Storehouse Telephone 07582142948 Email enquiries@communitystorehouse.co.uk Organisation The Community Storehouse

title: Macmillan Crossroads Volunteer Service
address: 16 Reculver Road, Herne Bay CT6 6LE                                                  The Oast, 170 Tonbridge Road Wateringbury ME18 5NS What we do The Macmillan Crossroads Volunteer Service supports people living with cancer and their carers across Kent and Medway. Our primary volunteer offer is companionship; a listening ear at a time when someone navigating cancer may be lonely or struggling to cope with their diagnosis. This companionship may include light housework, short external trips, wellbeing activities and / or gardening where available. We also offer volunteer transport support where possible – providing transport for a person or couple from their home to healthcare appointments including but not limited to Chemotherapy / Radiology sessions and GP appointments. In addition, our expert team provide information and guidance and signposting to other services, as well as support in grant applications to help with the financial impacts of a cancer diagnosis. Volunteers are fully trained and have been DBS checked

title: Macmillan Welfare Benefits Service East Kent
address: Virtual What we do The Macmillan Welfare Benefit Advice Service (East Kent) is aimed at any one in the East Kent area affected by cancer. It is also available to their families and carers. This service is free of charge to all regardless of income. Examples of services provided under this project are: Applications for and entitlement to benefits and tax credits Income maximisation Form filling Appeals against benefit decisions Health costs such as prescriptions and travel to hospital Charitable grants Housing costs Council Tax discounts Transport concessions, e.g. disabled parking badge Advice is by appointment only – to get in touch with us for an appointment please: Call us on 01227 452762, or Visit our reception at Canterbury Citizens Advice (3 Westgate Hall Rd, Canterbury CT1 2BT) on Monday, Wednesday or Thursday between 10am and 1pm, or Visit our reception at our Herne Bay office on Tuesday between 10am and 1pm, or Or email us on admin@canterburycab.cabnet.org.uk

address: Maidstone Baptist Church, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LU What we do Our network of over 400 macular support groups stretches across the UK. Each one offers practical and emotional support for people with macular disease, from those living with it today. Peer support groups are for anyone with macular degeneration, newly diagnosed or otherwise. It is an opportunity to learn more about the condition and what can help and a chance to meet others and to share a cup of tea.

title: Macular Society Telephone Befriending
address: Macular Society, Crown Chambers, South Street, Andover, Hampshire, SP10 2BN What we do It is not unusual for some people with macular disease to feel isolated, especially if it’s difficult to get out and about. However, there is no need to feel alone – our telephone befrienders provide a regular friendly phone call, which can help to rebuild confidence and independence. Our telephone befriending service may be especially helpful if you cannot get to one of our support group meetings, but would like to talk to someone who has experience of macular disease.
price: Free Organisation Macular Society Telephone 0300 3030 111 Email befriending@macularsociety.org Organisation Macular Society

title: MADM The King’s Pantry
address: MADM Outreach Centre, 48 Church Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME14 1DS What we do We have a five day a week Outreach Centre and food bank where we support people most in need that are referred to us by local organisations like Social Services, NHS, Maidstone Borough Council and others. Our Food bank has grown and has been re-branded The King’s Pantry. We offer bespoke food support. We collect from a number of supermarkets in Maidstone several times a week. We also received funding from Maidstone Borough Council for two industrial freezers and a fridge to enable us to take more fresh produce in addition to the tinned, packets, toiletries and cleaning products we provide. title: Maidstone CAN Litter Picking
address: Maidstone What we do Litter picking at various locations in Maidstone. Litterpicking helps reduce pollution by removing harmful waste that contaminates our environment. Plastics and other non-biodegradable materials can take hundreds of years to decompose, releasing toxins into the environment. By removing these items, we can reduce pollution and protect ecosystems. When? One Sunday a month 10am – 12pm. Please check the website calendar to see scheduled sessions , or email Shirley at maidstonecanlitterpick@gmail.com.

title: Maidstone Day Centre and Food Bank
address: Homeless Care, 15, Knightrider St, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Maidstone Homeless Care provides support for those over 18 who are living on the street and homeless locally. Some of the support we offer includes the use of the Day Centre as a postal address and telephone contact point, hot showers with toiletries and towels provided, a laundry service, clothing and shoes, sleeping bags and blankets, good home cooked meals, tea, coffee and cold drinks, a comfortable area with books, computer use, a safe place to relax for a while, regular nurse clinics and Mental Health support . Other clinics include: Complex Needs Nurse, Osteopath, CGL, Hep C Nurse, Dentaid and many others. At the end of the session, we provide a pack-up of sandwiches, fruit and a drink. Once the practical needs have been met, our staff can help with claiming benefits and dealing with problems such as health issues, obtaining identity documents and finding accommodation. We can also offer you someone to talk to, someone who understands. For more information please call. Our food parcel requests should be addressed to foodparcels@homelesscare.org.uk or admin@homelesscare.co.uk
price: Free Organisation Homeless Care Telephone 01622 674064 Email admin@homelesscare.org.uk Organisation Homeless Care

title: Maidstone Harriers Run and Tri Club
address: Maidstone Hockey Club, Armstrong Road, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 6AZ What we do One Club for All in the Community. Find Your Stride, Find Your People, Find the Fun. We’re an inclusive running and triathlon club that’s more than just training. From beginner to seasoned athlete, you’ll get expert coaching, competitive events and a social calendar packed with fun. Challenge yourself, make new friends and enjoy the journey. Joining Maidstone Harriers means more than just running or triathlon training. You become part of a supportive, motivated and welcoming social community. There’s so much to enjoy with your membership including: Structured Training & Expert Coaching Racing & Competition Opportunities Social & Community Member Perks & Discounts Weekly training sessions and more (from track to pool and spin bike to club run – you’ll find a weekly session that’s perfect for you). We also have guide runners for visually impaired runners. Visit our website to find out more including how to apply to become a member.
price: Variable - from £45 per year for club membership (or £30 per year for Student/Senior Concession) Organisation Maidstone Harriers Run and Tri Club Email club@maidstoneharriers.co.uk Organisation Maidstone Harriers Run and Tri Club

title: Maidstone Safe Haven MHM
address: Kestrel House, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 6LU What we do Maidstone Safe Haven offers free mental health support to anyone aged over 18, in a safe, comfortable and supportive environment. There is no need to be referred and no need for an appointment. We are open every night for face-to-face or virtual support. Our experienced staff team is available to provide non-clinical emotional, social and practical support if you are in crisis or feel you are heading toward a crisis situation. We also offer help in creating staying well and crisis plans, and support visitors to access other organisations that may be useful to them. Please be aware this service is not suitable for people needing urgent medical attention (for example, after self-harm); for urgent medical care please attend your nearest A&E or call 999. Open Monday – Sunday 6pm – 11pm
price: Free Organisation Maidstone Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters Telephone 01622 809 945, 07484 061684, 07816 362606 Email maidstone.mhm@nhs.net Organisation Maidstone Safe Haven - Mental Health Matters

title: Maidstone Town Walk
address: Meet in Jubilee Square, in front of the Brenchley, ME16 9QQ What we do This lovely walk shows you Maidstone from the waterside. Meet us outside the Brenchley in Jubilee Square, near the old Town Hall. From there the walk is led through the town centre into Brenchley Gardens and then takes you via the bridge over the road and river to Whatman Park along the river Medway and then over one of the bridges and back to town. Suitable for wheelchairs and buggies with care. Occasionally the walk heads down to the river by the Archbishop’s Palace, then over the footbridge and along the riverbank to just beyond the Tovil footbridge, returning the same way. Refreshments are available after the walk from the boat café on the river or the Brenchley. Thursdays Start time: 11:00 am Estimated finish time: 12:00 pm
price: Free Organisation One You Maidstone Telephone 07836 720643 Email linaker3@btinternet.com Organisation One You Maidstone

title: Maintenance Cognitive Stimulation Therapy
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Maintenance Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (MCST) follows on from the CST course and is suitable for people who have completed CST and would like to carry on with the weekly sessions (aged 55+). The Maintenance sessions follow similar themes to the CST sessions, with some new themes added, but it aims to use different materials where possible, to avoid repetition. The structure of the sessions is the same as CST, however, so it will feel familiar to you.

title: Making connections – Family History Service
address: 140 Balaam Street, London, E13 8RD What we do Making Connections works with adults who were in Barnardo’s care as children or were placed for adoption by the charity. Its core purpose is to provide access to records for adults seeking information about their past, and where follow-up is required, help with tracing and intermediary work is offered. Our work with adoptive adults is a statutory service and we therefore operate as part of Barnardo’s Adoption Agency. Making Connections holds the archive of records for every child who has been resident in a home, school or family placement since the organisation began. The service is also responsible for managing the Barnardo’s corporate archive collections. Making Connections offers a Family History Service to descendants of those who grew up in our care in the UK, or as part of the historic emigration schemes to Canada and Australia.

title: Man2Man Telephone Peer Support
address: Virtual What we do Man2Man is our telephone based peer support service from a trained StrongMen volunteer with first-hand experience of losing a loved one. Men who contact StrongMen for support will be offered the opportunity to speak with one of our trained volunteers for a friendly chat. Each beneficiary will receive up to six weeks of peer support from StrongMen. The service seeks to capitalise on the connection felt between two people with similar shared life experiences, simply knowing your volunteer has empathy with your situation maximises trust without fear of judgement.
price: Free Organisation StrongMen Telephone 0800 915 0400 Email hello@StrongMen.org.uk Organisation StrongMen

title: Managing Stress and Anxiety Online Webinar
address: Virtual What we do Our “Managing Stress and Anxiety” webinar is a four week self-help workshop based on the principles of Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) and aims to help you understand what keeps stress and anxiety going, as well as how to break those vicious cycles. This includes learning how to manage difficult or worrisome thoughts, as well as developing helpful ways to cope with stressful life circumstances.

title: Marcus Movers Walking Group
address: Mote Park, Mote Ave, Maidstone ME15 7RN What we do Join our new Marcus Movers walking group taking place each week around the beautiful site of Mote Park in Maidstone taking in the grassland, woodland and 30 acre lake. Each week, our experienced Wellbeing Coaches will lead a one hour walk giving you the opportunity to get outdoors, enjoy the fresh air and use The Five Ways to Wellbeing to improve your mental health. The route will remain on hard surface paths throughout ensuring accessibility for all. When : This is a 12 week programme starting on Monday 13th April and ending on 13th July. title: Marden Sporting Memories
address: Marden Sports Club, Maidstone Road, Marden, TN12 9AG What we do Marden Sporting Memories is a group where older adults meet to talk and reminisce about past sporting occasions; and catch up on the weekly sports events, whether that’s local sports, national or on the international circuit. The group occasionally have visitors who share their ‘Sporting memories’ from the world of rowing, golf, hockey umpires, football referees and a spitfire pilot to name a few. Each week there are ‘half-time’ refreshments, then the club members enjoy a variety of physical activities; all very light hearted – lots of laughing and reminiscing. This group meets weekly on a Tuesday afternoon Please note that there is a waiting list for this group. Please contact Sue Eltringham using the below details if you would like to join the waiting list for this group.
price: Voluntary weekly contribution (suggested £1.00) Organisation Marden Sporting Memories Telephone 01622 833053 Email mardensportingmemories@gmail.com Organisation Marden Sporting Memories

title: Marie Curie Information & Support Service
address: Virtual What we do Marie Curie provides free support over the phone in over 200 languages, and via webchat, to anyone with an illness they’re likely to die from and those close to them. Our team offers practical and emotional support on everything from symptom management and day-to-day care to financial information and bereavement support. Call our free Support Line on 0800 090 2309. Open 8am – 6pm Monday to Friday, and 11am – 5pm on Saturdays. Calls are free from landlines and mobile phones. Your call may be recorded for quality and training purposes.

title: Maternal Journal Online Circle
address: Online What we do Our monthly online creative evenings are for anyone who identifies as a mum, whether you are expecting, a new mum, a mum of older children, adopting/fostering or are a grandma or great grandma. We hope these sessions will give you a break from childcare (although we warmly welcome babes in arms). We hope you can get a night to yourself. We hold these monthly on a Monday evening from 8pm to 9:45pm. Each month’s session includes: Time to meet and make friends in our interactive gathering over Zoom A guided relaxation and visualisation which will lead you into a relaxing journalling activity Time to share and be heard A poem or reflection to finish Chill out time at the end to stay online and socialise You don’t need a babysitter to attend, or even leave the house. You can find out more on our website or please email me for further information.
price: pay-what-you-can on a sliding scale Organisation My Birth Support CIC Email aileen@mybirth.org.uk Organisation My Birth Support CIC
organisation: My Birth Support CIC Email aileen@mybirth.org.uk

title: Maternity Clinic
address: ESO Teaching Clinic, 104, Tonbridge Road, Maidstone, ME15 8SL What we do Osteopathic treatment is gentle and aims to help the body adapt to the changes more readily. Treatment is usually very relaxing and enjoyable. Treatment aims to prepare the body for the labour process. We can also advise on exercise and stretching techniques where appropriate and strategies and postures to help cope with labour pains. We also provide post-natal care, treating mothers and babies together in our Children’s clinic. The usual price for treatments is New Patient £23 and Continuing patient £17. Please see our website for more information https://bcnogroup.ac.uk/clinics/eso-kent/
price: Set charge Organisation European School of Osteopathy Telephone 01622 685989 Email info.eso@bcnogroup.ac.uk Organisation European School of Osteopathy

title: Mayah’s Legacy Online Resources
address: Online What we do Mayah’s Legacy offers self-advocacy training through its SisterZ Advocates programme, helping women build the confidence to speak up for themselves and others, especially during medical appointments and difficult health journeys. The training supports women to ask the right questions, understand their rights, communicate clearly, make informed decisions, and create safe, compassionate spaces where other women feel heard and supported. Alongside this, Mayah’s Legacy provides practical resources designed to turn overwhelm into confidence, including toolkits, workbooks, guides, cards, and downloadable materials that support self-advocacy, emotional wellbeing, informed consent, and confidence in healthcare conversations. Visit our website for more information.

The training supports women to ask the right questions, understand their rights, communicate clearly, make informed decisions, and create safe, compassionate spaces where other women feel heard and supported. Alongside this, Mayah’s Legacy provides practical resources designed to turn overwhelm into confidence, including toolkits, workbooks, guides, cards, and downloadable materials that support self-advocacy, emotional wellbeing, informed consent, and confidence in healthcare conversations. Visit our website for more information. Organisation Mayah’s Legacy Email info@mayahslegacy.com Organisation Mayah’s Legacy
categories: Advocacy and Information, advice and guidance; Support Services and Self-help

title: Meals on Wheels (Maidstone)
address: At home What we do Cooking is something many of us take for granted — but not everyone is able to prepare their own meals at home. That’s where Age UK MST’s Meals on Wheels service can help. Our service delivers hot, freshly cooked meals straight to your door. Prepared in our kitchen at Maidstone Community Support Centre, each meal includes two courses packed with nutrition and flavour. Eligibility for this service is flexible. Find cooking difficult? Appreciate the convenience of someone preparing a meal for you? Our Meals on Wheels service may be able to help. Our service provides freshly cooked meals delivered directly to your home by our friendly staff or volunteers. Many meals are delivered by the same person each week, which means people using the service often get to know their delivery driver. As well as receiving a nutritious meal, this provides valuable social contact and a friendly face at the door. Meals are available seven days a week, so you can choose the days that work best for you. Our menus run on a rotating basis and feature comforting dishes that our customers enjoy. Examples include: Fish and chips Casseroles Cottage pie Sausage and mash Every Sunday, you can also enjoy a traditional roast dinner. Each meal includes two courses — a hearty main meal followed by a dessert such as apple crumble, fresh fruit and cream, or lemon meringue pie. If you have any dietary requirements, please let us know when you contact us and we will do our best to accommodate them. Many of Age UK MST’s clients are aged 55+, but we understand that people may need support preparing meals for a variety of reasons. We are keen to support anyone within our catchment area who may benefit from this service. A two-course hot meal costs £9.80 per person, including delivery. You can also add a snack pack for £3.20, which will be delivered at the same time as your meal.
price: Set charge Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Medway Health Walks
address: Virtual What we do Medway Health Walks and the newly formed Ramblers Wellbeing Walks in Medway believe that walking is the key to a happier and healthier lifestyle. Almost everyone can do it, no matter your age, income, gender or ability. It’s the easiest and most accessible form of activity that can become part of your everyday life. We encourage everyone to try a group walk by joining our volunteer led walking groups. We have a number of walks from 10 minutes up to 90 minutes. You only need a pair of shoes and a fitness level appropriate to the walk you are joining. Each walk has a unique feel, from the distance, the terrain and the environment. To view all walks in Medway please visit: https://www.medway.gov.uk/info/200221/a_better_medway/449/health_walks/2 Rochester Riverside walk- Meeting outside Eastgate House, ME1 1EW. Mondays, leaves at: 10am. Walk lasts 60 minutes. Hoo – Just Beat It, Cancer rehab support group. Meeting outside Hoo Church, ME3 9AL. Every 1st and 3rd Monday of every month, leaves at: 10am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes. Bell Mast and Island walk (St. Mary’s Island). Meeting at Dockyard Visitor’s Entrance, ME4 4TY. Walkers must report to the walk leaders due to parking restrictions. Mondays, leaves at: 10.30am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes. Higham walk. Meeting outside Higham Village club, ME3 7DD. Every 2nd and 4th Monday of every month, leaves at: 10.45am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes. Walk In the Park, Rainham. Meeting at the outdoor gym equipment at Rainham Recreation Ground, ME8 7PR. Tuesdays, leaves at: 10am. Self-paced with rest stops. 20 to 30 minutes. Strood community walk. Meeting at Strood Community Hub, ME2 4TJ. Tuesdays, leaves at: 10.15am. Walk lasts for 75 minutes. Cuxton Hiking Group. Meeting outside Cuxton Library, ME2 1EY. Every 1st and 3rd Tuesday of every month, leaves at: 10.30am. Walk lasts for 90 minutes. Riverside Country Park wellbeing walk. Meeting at Riverside Country Park, ME7 2XH. Tuesdays, leaves at: 10.15am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes. Walk in the Park, Wainscott. Meeting at Choir Close, ME3 8FP. Wednesdays, leaves at: 10am. Walk may last for up to 20 minutes. The Great Lines wellbeing walk. Meeting at Gillingham Community Hub, ME7 1BG. Wednesdays, leaves at: 10am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes Walk in the Park, Capstone. Meeting at the cafe, ME7 3JG. Wednesdays, leaves at: 10am. Self-paced with rest stops. 20 to 30 minutes. Cliffe Woods. Meeting at Parks Medical Centre, ME3 8HX (meet outside). Wednesdays, leaves at: 10.30am. Walk lasts for 75 minutes. Rainham Wellbeing Walk. Meeting outside St. Margaret’s Church, ME8 8AN. Every 1st and 3rd Wednesday of every month, leaves at: 10.45am. Walk lasts for 75 minutes. Three Villages walk. Meeting at Village Hall, Lower Stoke, ME3 9ST. Thursdays, leaves at: 9.30am. Walk lasts for 75 minutes. Walk in the Park, Beechings Way, Twydall. Meeting at the community centre, ME8 6TQ. Thursdays, leaves at: 10am. Self-paced with rest stops. 20 to 30 minutes. Medway Carers walk. (Contact Roger at jollyrogerspeakeasy@live.co.uk for location and day). Leaves at: 11am. Walk lasts for 60 minutes.

title: Memory Café (Alpine Care Home)
address: Alpine Care Home, Sevenoaks, TN13 3LH What we do Why not join us at one of our FREE Memory Cafés taking place across Sevenoaks and Tonbridge? These sessions are open to everyone, and are more than just a hot drink and a slice of cake! You’ll get to meet others living with memory challenges, build new friendships, share your experiences and find common ground. When : 10am – 12pm monthly – Upcoming dates for 2026: 26th March, 23rd April, 21st May, 18th June, 16th July, 13th August, 10th September, 8th October, 5th November, 3rd December Where : Alpine Care Home, Sevenoaks, TN13 3LH Booking : Contact hayley.brown@ageukmst.org.uk or 01622 753618 to book. Our Cafés are relaxed, drop-in sessions. You can come along at any time during the session, and stay for as little or as long as you like. Partners and Carers are welcome to join, too! You can come along to all of our sessions each month, or just the one closest to you. Please note that you will need to arrange your own travel for our cafe sessions.

title: Memory Café
address: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, West Malling, Kent, ME19 5NA What we do Memory Café – for anyone with dementia and their partner/carer/family member to meet others in the same situation, make new friends and share knowledge and information. There is usually a speaker, an entertainer or a quiz. 1st Monday of every month (except bank holidays) AND 3rd Wednesday of every month, 2.30pm until 4.30pm. There is no charge but we hold a raffle to cover the costs of tea, coffee and cakes.

title: Memory Cafés
address: Various around Dartford, Gravesham and Swanley (North Kent) What we do An informal and social environment for people with concerns about their memory. A welcoming and warm atmosphere where everyone is included. We have a variety of activities at our cafés including guest speakers, entertainment, and refreshments, all provided free of charge in a safe and friendly environment. While the emphasis is on taking the opportunity to meet others, relax and generally have fun, we also offer: Practical and emotional support to let you know that you are not alone Information and advice tailored to your individual needs Signposting and information on other services that might help you. We manage a number of monthly Memory Cafés, in various locations, throughout the area North Kent, Medway and West Kent. The cafés are open to anyone to drop-in. If you would like to attend we would recommend you call us on 01474 533990, email info@adss.org.uk or visit www.adss.org.uk so that we can arrange for you to be met and introduced into the group. This is a free service however donations are welcomed to sustain the groups. These are the cafes we currently have: West Kingsdown Cafe Shorne Cafe Dartford Cafe Longfield Café Chatham Hoo Parkwood Aylesford New Ash Green Cafe Swanley Cafe Gravesend Cafe Gravesend Guru Nanak Cafe Strood Cafe
price: Donations welcome Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@alz-dem.org Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: Memory Cafes
address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Age UK Tunbridge Wells run a number of free Memory Cafes in and around Tunbridge Wells to offer a safe and calm environment to meet others living with dementia, share experiences, get information and advice, as well as to help you get out of the house and socialise. The charity also runs peer support groups to offer ongoing support, support for those with a new diagnosis of Dementia, support for family and carers and support for those with young onset Dementia.

title: Memory Meadows
address: The Riverside Centre, The Riverside Centre, Dover, Kent, CT147JP What we do Our Memory Meadows respite service is a safe space for those living with Dementia and any other memory related condition. We provide carers and family members the much needed time for themselves while we provide stimulating activities for their loved ones. Our programme is designed to engage participants in a variety of enriching activities and social interaction such as: art therapy music sessions gentle exercises cognitive games all tailored to their unique abilities and interests. The day starts at 10am finishing at 3pm and includes a 2 course lunch. When: Monday, Wednesday and Friday 10am – 3pm. 2 course lunch is included. Where: The Riverside Centre Booking: Contact us for more information
price: £45 Organisation The Riverside Community Hub Telephone 01304207268 Email debbie.beer@riversidedover.org.uk Organisation The Riverside Community Hub

title: Men2 Group
address: Maidstone Community Support, 39-48 Marsham St, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do A peer support group for Men. Our weekly meetings are held on Thursdays at 7pm an alternate between being held in person at Maidstone Community Support Centre and online. We encourage any men who have experienced mental health issues or are facing or have faced challenges to attend our Men 2 Groups, whether it be to share their stories, inspire others, or to get out of the house and socialise with like-minded people. All it takes is 2 Men to start a conversation.

title: Bipolar UK Men’s Support Group online
address: Virtual What we do Where: Online When: 3rd Tuesday of the month Time: 12.30pm – 1.30pm Register for free here. Our men’s support group is free and open to anyone identifying as a man affected by bipolar, including family, friends and loved ones. If you don’t have a confirmed diagnosis and want to find out about bipolar, and related conditions such as cyclothymia, you are also welcome. Anyone over 18 is welcome. If you’re 16 or 17, please bring a parent or guardian. The format of the meetings vary, but you will have the opportunity to introduce yourself, say how you are feeling using the Bipolar UK mood scale and may listen to an invited speaker. The groups are organised and facilitated by volunteers who are affected by bipolar in some way. If you would like to join the next support meeting, please book a free ticket here. You will receive the link to join via email after booking a ticket. If you have any questions please email: supportgroups@bipolaruk.org
price: Free Organisation Bipolar UK Telephone 0333 323 3880 Email supportgroups@bipolaruk.org Organisation Bipolar UK

title: Men’s Walking Football
address: Maidstone YMCA, Melrose Close, Cripple Street, Maidstone, ME15 6BD What we do Walking football is a growing sport and offers a multitude of health benefits to older people such as reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and stroke while improving blood pressure. Positive changes in postural balance, blood pressure and resting heart rate, lowered cholesterol, improved blood sugar levels, bone density, and improving reactions, whilst slashing the odds of suffering from type 2 diabetes – all indicators of general good health. We run walking football programmes for men (aged 50 upwards) and cater for all levels of football ability – if you are interested in joining one of our programmes simply turn up and have a chat. You can register your interest on the website , or call on 07904 992474 or send an email to walkingfootball@stonesinthecommunity.org When? Fridays at 6.45pm Where? Maidstone YMCA, Melrose Close, Cripple Street, Maidstone, ME15 6BD

title: Mental Health Programme
address: St Andrew's Road, Barming, Maidstone, ME16 9AN What we do We offer a six-month placement offering a range of social therapeutic workshops including breadmaking, café, craft, cooking, gardening, plant nursery, stained glass and woodwork. Placements may also include one-to-one therapy sessions. Our holistic therapies address the whole person and we endeavour to support individuals to manage their symptoms while at the same time strengthening their confidence and motivation. The therapies that we offer are Arts Counselling, Biographical Counselling, CranioSacral Therapy, Metal Colour Light Therapy and Rhythmical Massage. Everyone enrolled on a programme is accompanied by a staff mentor throughout their time at Blackthorn. Our mental health programme is designed to support individuals to: Strengthen confidence, spark motivation for change, broaden outlook and experience. Reduce isolation. Build resilience and manage well-being. Identify aspirations and learn practical skills. Explore next steps and access partner organisations. Access specialist advice on benefits, finance, housing and employment issues. Eligibility criteria and information on how to be referred to this service can be found on our website.
price: Free Organisation Blackthorn Trust Telephone 01622 828371  or  01622 828372 Email nathalie@blackthorn.org.uk  or  susanna@blackthorn.org.uk Organisation Blackthorn Trust

We offer a six-month placement offering a range of social therapeutic workshops including breadmaking, café, craft, cooking, gardening, plant nursery, stained glass and woodwork. Placements may also include one-to-one therapy sessions. Our holistic therapies address the whole person and we endeavour to support individuals to manage their symptoms while at the same time strengthening their confidence and motivation. The therapies that we offer are Arts Counselling, Biographical Counselling, CranioSacral Therapy, Metal Colour Light Therapy and Rhythmical Massage. Everyone enrolled on a programme is accompanied by a staff mentor throughout their time at Blackthorn. Our mental health programme is designed to support individuals to: Eligibility criteria and information on how to be referred to this service can be found on our website. Price Free

title: MindStones
address: Valley Park School, Huntsman Lane, Maidstone, UK What we do MindSTONES is a free football scheme for men aged 18+ who wish to improve their mental and physical fitness in a friendly, open and safe environment. The weekly sessions take place on a Thursday evening and, as a Head in the Game legacy project, follow the principles of that initiative using discussion and football to enable participants to support their wellbeing. Our sessions serve as a lifeline for many participants, providing invaluable support during their most challenging moments. Within our open, honest, and non- judgmental environment, we offer unwavering support, enabling people to navigate through their darkest times and embrace brighter days ahead. Monday 7 – 8pm and Thursdays 8 – 9pm based at Valley Park Find out more on the website.

title: Modern Slavery Victim Care Services
address: Registered Address, Kemp House 160, City Road, London, EC1V 2NX What we do We provide specialist support and accommodation to victims of human trafficking and modern slavery, and their dependants. Our dedicated casework teams provide safe accommodation, develop support plans and facilitate access to key services in order to empower clients as they recover from trauma. Our aim is to reduce the risk of re-trafficking and help the survivors to move onto a positive new chapter in their lives. We have put in place changes to how we deliver our regional services to asylum seekers, victims of modern slavery, EU citizens and refugees, so these services could continue throughout the lockdown.

title: Money Guidance
address: Dartford, Gravesham, Swanley, Medway What we do We understand that money issues can often be very stressful and can take a toll on your mental health and wellbeing, especially if you have existing mental health difficulties. Here at North Kent Mind we can help you navigate through your money worries by offering support and guidance through our money guidance advisors, help you take control of your finances and get the support you need to help you navigate through your money worries. Our Money Guidance service is to support individuals 17+ who are struggling with money difficulties and poor mental health, who may be stressed or anxious, have reduced wellbeing, and would benefit from the programme. The sessions will look at the individual’s financial situation and ways in which their circumstances could be improved, with individuals feeling supported, informed, and empowered to manage their money, and improve their wellbeing. Money Guider Individual Support Sessions can be offered weekly with each lasting up to one hour, depending on need. Our money guiders can support you with a range of money concerns, providing guidance and information on the following: • Budget guidance and planning support • Signposting and referrals to supporting agencies where required. • Support with transitions from benefits to work. • Guidance for managing debt • Awareness of fraud and scams. • Welfare & Benefits guidance and Support (including Universal Credit and Personal Independence payments) • Expert mental health support to encourage and develop resilience and independence, including stress management self-help tools. You can self refer via our website!
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email moneyguidance@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Money & Mental Health Project
address: Registered address: Tonbridge Castle, Castle Street, TONBRIDGE, Kent, TN9 1BG.     (But open to anyone from Sevenoaks, Swanley, Dartford Gravesham, Tonbridge & Tunbridge Wells). What we do The Money and Mental Health project is a service offered by Kent Citizens Advice offices to help clients living with mental health issues, who need advice dealing with debt and other related issues. Whilst the primary focus of the project is in helping with debt/money issues, the service we offer is holistic and where we identify other advice that might be needed, our project workers will make sure that appropriate help is given. We also have a specific perinatal arm of this project, to help new or soon-to-be parents who are struggling with their money and mental health.
price: Free Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent Telephone 0808 278 7810 Email referral@nwkent.cab.org.uk Organisation Citizens Advice in North and West Kent

title: Mote Park Long and Short Walks
address: Meet at car park end of new Café, ME16 9QQ What we do Walking in Mote Park is like walking in the countryside! The park is huge and has lovely views, a former stately home and a large lake. The walks are very accessible with parking on-site, well surfaced paths and tracks through woodland with only a few gentle slopes. Most routes are suitable for buggies and wheelchairs. Two walks are run each Tuesday from near the Café commencing at 10.30 sharp. The Long Walk is generally just over an hour in duration, taking in some the longer footpaths, whilst the Short Walk covers less distance and is at a more leisurely pace with more pauses for the less active and lasts up to an hour.

title: Move On
address: Kenward Trust, Kenward Road, Yalding, Maidstone, ME18 6AH What we do Move On is Kenward Trust’s resettlement project, for those having been through a primary stage rehabilitation project. We have five supported accommodation projects which help sustain recovery and reintegration into the community. These are located in Gravesend, Maidstone, Tunbridge Wells and Uckfield. The length of stay is generally up to two years. We offer regular support through key working and weekly house meetings with the project manager. We help with daily living skills, accessing training or volunteering opportunities and signposting to other agencies. If you would like to apply for this programme, or to find out more about the service, please visit the Kenward Trust Website to complete the residential rehabilitation application form. This service is for over 18’s.

title: Damn It! Dance It! Workshops
address: We currently run these workshops in the following areas: ME15 6AT, CT1 2RA, ME5 8TH, TN4 0NA, CT16 1BB, DA12 1BE, CT19 6HS What we do Damn It! Dance It! sessions are delivered by Moving Memory Dance Theatre Company for people (especially older people) who would like to loosen up and have a laugh with others. They are drop-in, free and take place in shopping centres. Sessions start by exploring basic movement skills, learning how to move well, and then finding creative ways of bringing personal experience to life. As a company of older women, we understand about the physical stresses and strains of everyday life and we love working with people of all ages, genders, shapes and sizes. The workshops are a chance to meet new people and work together to share ideas and movements. No experience is necessary and we adapt movements around individual needs. If you are interested, please email info@movingmemorydance.com and we’ll let you know about dates for the next workshops.
price: Free Organisation Moving Memory Dance Theatre Company Telephone 01304 800300 Email info@movingmemorydance.com Organisation Moving Memory Dance Theatre Company

title: Mums Matter Breakfasts
address: Thanington Resource Centre, Thanington Road, Canterbury, CT13XE What we do Hi there mums of all ages and stages! You are warmly invited to join us at our monthly Mums Matter breakfasts in Thanington, Canterbury – these are held once a month on Thursday from 9:30am until 11:30am. These gatherings are all about pampering you while your little one(s) rest or play. Our relaxed breakfasts are a great way to meet and make friends with other mums, you can come without children too. We always have some new people attending, so even if you’ve never been to a group with your little one, you are so welcome to be part of our circle of support. At every gathering we have specialist mental health support and infant feeding support available to help you get the help you need. Our breakfasts are free to book and we welcome donations on the day to help ensure our mental health support can continue. Please email Aileen if you would like to book to join!
price: Free to book but donations welcome Organisation My Birth Support CIC Email aileen@mybirth.org.uk Organisation My Birth Support CIC

title: Music Matters Group
address: ellenor, Coldharbour Road, Gravesend, DA11 7HQ What we do This group explores music in all its forms, including live performances from professional artists, sessions with ellenor’s Music Therapist, or sharing your own favourite songs and their meanings with others. Music is a powerful tool which allows people to “come alive”, supporting emotional, spiritual and social wellbeing. These sessions run every Wednesday from 11.00am to 12 noon.

title: Music Movement (dementia friendly)
address: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, West Malling, Kent, ME19 5NA What we do Our Music Movement session is exciting, relaxed movement classes in a safe, social, and welcoming environment. It provides an opportunity for people living with dementia, memory loss, Parkinson’s or other neurological conditions, and their carers to do something fun together. It also aims to give people a sense of community, lots of laughs and friendship. Session details When: On the 4th Monday of the month at 2:30pm and on the 2nd Wednesday of the month at 2:30pm Where: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, West Malling, Kent, ME19 5NA Cost: £3.00 For more information and to book, please email natalie.williams@tmactive.co.uk

title: My Birth Recovery Course
address: Ashford Road Community Association Hall, Ashford Road, Canterbury, TW15 3NJ What we do You can heal after a difficult pregnancy, bad birth or challenging postnatal period. You are not alone if it was traumatic and you don’t need to suffer in silence any more. This group series will provide you with as safe a place as possible to be heard, to belong and to explore what happened in your pregnancy, birth and postnatal period; teach you with a variety of creative and practical strategies to work through what happened. These will also be transferable to difficult situations you experience in the future; help you let go of unhelpful feelings/experiences, building your confidence and resilience. We also offer this support on a 1:1 or 1:couple basis for those who want private support. This 6 weekly course is offered periodically online and in person so please get in touch if you would like more information or to join the next course.
price: Pay-what-you-can on a sliding scale Organisation My Birth Support CIC Email aileen@mybirth.org.uk Organisation My Birth Support CIC

title: National LGBT+ Domestic Abuse Helpline
address: Virtual What we do Galop gives advice and support to people who have experienced biphobia, homophobia, transphobia, sexual violence or domestic abuse. We also support lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans and queer people who have had problems with the police or have questions about the criminal justice system. Our services include support and advice for people who have experienced hate crime, domestic abuse or sexual violence, specialist advocacy and support, help in reporting or getting what you need from police and other agencies, advice on the law and how the police work, applying for financial compensation for victims of crime and police complaints.
price: Free Organisation Galop Telephone 0800 999 5428 Email help@galop.org.uk Organisation Galop

title: National Male Survivor Helpline
address: Virtual What we do We support men who have been affected by sexual abuse or rape through our helpline and online support and we aim to provide a service that is accessible and fair to as many people as we can across England and Wales. Open Mon-Fri: 9am-8pm and Saturday and Sunday: 10am-2pm

title: National Stalking Helpline
address: Virtual What we do The National Stalking Helpline is run by Suzy Lamplugh Trust. Call our helpline on 0808 802 0300 Calls are free from all landline telephones and also from mobiles using the O2, Orange, T Mobile, Three (3), Virgin, and Vodafone networks. Calls will not be shown on BT landline bills. If you are using another telephone provider please enquire with them about whether 080880 numbers will appear on your phone bill. Do not send referrals to the below email address. Helpline only.

title: Live Well Kent Natural Environment
address: Online and around West Kent, Medway and Swale What we do A selection of outdoor, nature-based activities, taking place face-to-face at various locations within the community. We have a variety of different activities scheduled across the year, ranging from fishing to woodwork, from allotment growing to fire craft, and from gardening to woodland maintenance. We also offer an online ‘Connect with Nature’ programme for those who are unable to engage in person or who would prefer to engage from home; this programme includes informal discussion & learning about different nature-based topics, with the opportunity to take on simple nature challenges in between sessions.

title: Naturally Creative
address: Mount Pleasant Road, Royal Tunbridge Wells, TN1 1AW What we do Are you looking for a way to connect with like-minded people and make a positive impact on the environment? Join us for our new group, meeting at the Amelia Scott. Our meetings will take place fortnightly from 11:00 to 13:30, and we will alternate between gardening activities in our courtyard and green-themed craft activities. Our meetings are social and informal, providing a relaxed environment for you to meet new people and share your passion for your environment. title: No Panic Helpline
address: Virtual What we do No Panic is a registered charity that helps and supports those living with Panic Attacks, Phobias, Obsessive Compulsive Disorders and other related anxiety disorders. The No Panic Helpline operates between 10am and 10pm every day of the year. It is staffed by trained volunteers. During the night hours the crisis message is played. The crisis message is a recorded breathing exercise that can help you through a panic attack and help you learn diaphragmatic breathing. You can also access the crisis message 24hrs a day by calling 01952 680835. The helpline is busiest during the first hour of each shift and the last hour of the last shift of night.
price: Free Organisation No Panic Telephone 0300 7729844 Email info@nopanic.org.uk Organisation No Panic

title: No Walls Garden
address: Secret Garden, The Hill, Northfleet, DA11 9EU What we do No Walls Gardens is a resident led not for profit based in Northfleet North that offers programmes for over 55’s and vulnerable adults under 55 to get out and meet new people and learn new skills whilst beautifying a much unloved area. Activities include – Maintaining & improving current green spaces Fruit and Veg growing in Secret Garden Creating edible spots in the community where residents can access free fruit and veg Woodwork, arts and craft activities in the shed Providing residents with resources to grow their own Providing participants with skill development (soft skills and employability skills), signposting to further training, employment opportunities and support networks Time to talk over tea / coffee If you are interested in coming along, contact Peter on 07515 877263 or email nowallsgardens@gmail.com

address: Oakwood Park Community Group, Oakwood Park, Maidstone, ME16 8AE What we do Oakwood Park Community group will be open during the Summer months everyday outside of school hours. title: Oasis Cafe
address: Oasis, Northumberland Road, Maidstone, ME15 7LP What we do Join us at Oasis – our cafe serves everything from fried eggs and bacon to steaming cups or tea and cake. Our community worker is there to help and advise those that need it. All our food and drink is at cost prices. Our community cafe is open on Mondays and Fridays from 9.30am to 12.00pm and Wednesdays from 9.30am to 1.30pm. Entry is free but attendees must pay for their own food/drinks (first hot drink is free). As well as running a cafe The Vine also has a resource area as part of Oasis – donated household goods, clothes and toys are given for a donation. This is open at the same time as the cafe. title: One to One Support
address: Registered Address: SSAFA, EC3R 8AD What we do Our SSAFA Volunteers offer one to one support to members of the Armed Forces’ family. Our help may be financial, it may be advice, or it may be signposting. Our volunteer caseworkers will visit clients in their homes to assess their welfare needs and work out how we may be able to help. Clients must have served at least one day’s paid service in the Armed Forces or be an immediate family member. We are not an emergency service.
price: Free Organisation SSAFA Kent Telephone 0204 566 9126 Email southeast.region@ssafa.org.uk Organisation SSAFA Kent

title: BEAT One-to-One Web Chat
address: Virtual What we do Talk one-to-one with a Beat Helpline Advisor using our secure instant messaging service. Beat’s Advisors are highly trained, and available to talk to anyone affected by an eating disorder. Share your concerns, and we can help you decide on your next steps in the recovery journey with our action-focused approach. We’re able to offer signposting and support with those next steps, but as of right now we don’t offer a listening service and we may not be able to be a long-term source of support. You can access our web chat service here. Our web chat is available Monday to Friday from 3 pm to 8pm. When our chat service is open click on the ‘Chat’ image below to access. If you find yourself in the queue after 8pm, we will not be able to answer your chat.

title: One You Lifestyle Service

We use behaviour change skills and techniques to support people to adopt small changes that can have a big impact on their overall health and wellbeing. We work with you for up to one year with face-to-face or remote telephone/video call support. We aim to empower you with the motivation and focus to help you take control of your own lifestyle and behaviour change.

title: One You Smokefree
address: Virtual What we do Quitting smoking is one of the best decisions you can make for your health. It can: improve your overall health by reducing your risk of heart disease, stroke, lung cancer, and other serious illnesses. save you money: smoking is expensive and quitting can save you hundreds or even thousands of pounds each year. improve your quality of life: you’ll have more energy, breathe easier, and smell better. Many people try to quit smoking with willpower alone, but it’s much easier to go smoke-free with the right help. There are lots of support options available to help you on your quitting journey. Our stop smoking service offers a range of support and advice with face-to-face, telephone or video support, access to prescription products and your own personal quit plan. Get started today and fill in a referral form. A friendly advisor will then call you to talk through the options in your area. For free advice and support, or to register your interest in your own personal quit plan, call us now on 0300 123 1220 , text QUIT to 60163 or fill in our fast referral form . You can also find out more information on our website.
price: Free Organisation One You Kent Telephone 0300 123 1220 Organisation One You Kent

title: Weight Management Groups
address: One You Kent, Town Hall, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 1RS What we do This is a free 12 week programme (running both online and in person at a local venue) aimed at people with a BMI over 30 – other eligibility criteria applies. Topics covered include: portion sizes, label reading and more. Exercise sessions are suitable for beginners. Pre-course materials include workbooks, timetables, and further information. You can expect friendly support and advice from One You Advisors and private weekly weight submissions to keep you on track. Enjoy a warm and friendly group environment, set goals that are specific and achievable to you and aim to lose 5% of your body weight. title: One You Weight Loss
address: Virtual What we do If you’re overweight, losing just 5% of your weight can reduce the risk of several diseases such as heart disease, colon and breast cancer and type 2 diabetes. It will also lower your cholesterol and blood pressure, reduce stress on your joints, improve your breathing, reduce fatigue and help you sleep better. If you’re ready to lose weight and improve your health we can help. One You Kent offers professional and friendly support to help you achieve your weight loss goals with our free 12-week programme. You will be offered weekly support to learn how to manage your weight through healthy eating, physical activity and behaviour change. This practical and personal approach enables you to lose weight, but also to keep it off and be a healthy weight for life. Support is available online or with face-to-face appointments. You can find more information on our website.

title: Online Dementia Support Sessions
address: Online What we do Dementia Adventure offers free support sessions for people living with dementia, carers, family members and professionals. Our sessions provide practical advice, emotional support and opportunities to connect with others who understand the realities of dementia. Through shared experiences, expert guidance and peer support, we help people build confidence, reduce isolation and improve wellbeing. Sessions encourage people to stay active, maintain independence and discover positive ways to continue enjoying life, relationships and the outdoors after a dementia diagnosis. When : There are regular sessions throughout the month. Booking : For more information please contact Danine Irwin on 01245 237548 or training@dementiaadventure.org

title: Living well with arthritis
address: Virtual What we do Online Self-Management Events Arthritis Action’s ‘Living well with arthritis’ events are informative online events designed to introduce our self-management approach , to help you learn more strategies to help improve your physical health and mental wellbeing when living with arthritis. Held over zoom, they cover a range of topics including: Healthy Eating Nutrition Pain and fatigue management How to cope with flare-ups Dealing with emotions Depression management Distraction techniques How to stay active Physical therapies Relaxation Goal Setting Our tailored ‘Living well with arthritis’ events will give you the opportunity to put some of what you learn into practice and share your experiences with others. The events are free to attend and open to people with all forms of arthritis, from any location. You can find our calendar of events here. You can email us your questions at events@arthritisaction.org.uk , or call us on 0203 781 7120.
price: Free Organisation Arthritis Action Telephone 0203 781 7120 Email events@arthritisaction.org.uk Organisation Arthritis Action

title: Online Helpline
address: Virtual What we do SurvivorsUK runs the National Male Survivors Online Helpline – a webchat and SMS service for men, boys and non-binary people who have experienced sexual abuse at any time in their lives. We are also here for family, friends, allies and loved ones supporting a survivor in their lives. Our specialist support team can chat with you for up to 45 minutes per day by webchat or by text (SMS). Click ‘Chat online now’, below, or send a text to 020 3322 1860. We are also here for loved ones. Mon – Sun 12pm-8pm Chat Online Now Chat via SMS text 020 3322 1860 Our specialist support workers are experienced at supporting you to share things at your own pace, when you are ready. The chat is under your control so you can choose to finish whenever it feels right. There’s no pressure – you can contact us as little or often as you like, for up to 45 minutes per day.

address: Virtual What we do Find a Voice supports children, young people and adults with communication difficulties. title: Oxygen Therapy
address: Kent Neuro Therapy Centre, Bradbury House, Merton Lane (North), Canterbury, Kent, CT4 7DZ What we do Oxygen Therapy is not a cure for MS but it can certainly be effective in alleviating some of the symptoms such as fatigue, bladder urgency and frequency, aiding general mobility. Additional oxygen can extend the body’s ability to heal and can limit some of the damage which the disease causes. Some of our MS members have been receiving Oxygen Therapy at the centre for 25 years. When : Daily between 8.30am – 4.30pm Where : Kent Neuro Therapy Centre Booking : Contact Kent Neuro Therapy Centre directly for more information

title: Parental Alienation Awareness county support groups
address: Various locations across the UK What we do The county support groups for the alienated parents, partners and extended family are safe spaces, in non-public venues, placing Safeguarding as priority. This alienated community group comes together each month, bringing them out of isolation. Those attending our county support groups for alienated parents share a desire to learn, understand, express, and reach out to others experiencing what’s commonly known as ‘Parental Alienation’. These are vulnerable people suffering from a form of ‘domestic abuse’, ‘intimate partner violence’. They are targeted parents of children experiencing ‘child psychological abuse’. We bring this alienated community out of isolation, validate them, & signpost to other community support services when needed. You can find out more information on our website: https://paawareness.co.uk/county-support-groups/ Please contact us for more information.

title: Parental Alienation helpline
address: Remote What we do Parental Alienation Awareness offers a confidential and free helpline and legal service for parents in England and Wales. Our volunteers are here to listen, and we can provide emotional support and practical guidance – whether you’re needing some legal advice or feeling overwhelmed with the pressures of The Family Court process and child contact denial. We can also signpost you to additional help if needed and support you to think practically through problems. Most of all, we’ll listen without judgement. This phoneline is open Monday – Thursday from 6pm to 9pm. You can find out more about our helpline here: https://paawareness.co.uk/helpline/

title: Pathway to Working In the Care Sector
address: Various locations What we do Level 1 Introduction to Health and Social Care – Provides an introduction to working relationships in health and social care; an understanding of the legislation that informs the sector and principles relating to confidentiality at work and the importance of communication with a range of individuals. Course is 4 hours or two sessions of 2 hours and is accredited by Laser Awards. Level 2 Award in Health and Safety in Health and Social Care – Intended for people who want to work directly with patients/clients in a health and social care setting eg hospitals, care homes and day care centres. It develops their awareness of health and safety issues and covers the importance of good personal hygiene, risk assessments and what to do in response to accidents and incidents at work. The course is a full day. Introduction to Safeguarding Vulnerable Adults or Safeguarding Children – Learners will be familiarised with the reporting procedures around the safeguarding of vulnerable adults. It will include definitions of Safeguarding, Safeguarding principles, how to recognise abuse, how to seek help and information sharing. Course is 3 hours. Awareness Courses – Introduction to British Sign Language / Dementia Awareness / Autism Awareness These half-day courses will look at the knowledge and skills required to support people with various health conditions and challenges. It is an opportunity to grow your background knowledge.

title: Peer Led Medway
address: Medway What we do Self-Help Condition Based Groups The programme, of 2-hour self-help groups supporting individuals for 3 months. Attendees are 17+ with mental health concerns can engage in group work. Through empowerment, training plans, recovery, and support, service users can become volunteers and service facilitators. The process facilitated and supported by the peer support worker who has lived experience, with support and appropriate training. Social Anxiety and Agoraphobia Over the course of 3 months, we will be running a course designed for those who struggle with social anxiety and agoraphobia. Our course will alternate weekly running online and in person, gently motivating those who otherwise struggle to get out into the community to stretch their comfort zones and learn the skills they require to overcome their fears. We will look to understand our agoraphobia, challenge our negative beliefs and build our confidence. Depression Over the course of 12 weeks, we will be offering peer support and engagement around how we manage depression, the root causes of depression and our personal triggers of depression. We will also explore how our emotions effect our thoughts and behaviours, the impact our depression had on our daily routine and coping strategies that can help support our recovery, as well as ways to challenge our negative thinking patterns and greater improve our wellbeing.

title: Peer Partnership Project
address: Dartford, Gravesham, Swanley What we do Our Peer Partnership Project is a crisis recovery and prevention service, supporting individuals aged 17+ who live, or have a GP, in Dartford, Gravesham or Swanley, and who have recently experienced, or are at risk of experiencing, a mental health crisis. The work follows a Peer Support model, utilising the lived experience of mental health issues of both the Peer Support Worker and the individual being supported. The service offers both groups and/or 1:1 support. Our 12 week groups will be a structured program of sessions that blend educational sessions on recovery-focused subjects around crisis prevention and better mental health, along with client-led sessions. Some of the topics we will cover include: self-esteem, empowerment and resilience, the impact of trauma, self-care, managing emotions, coping strategies, and inter-personal relationships. You can self refer via our website!
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email ppp@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Peer Support
address: The Challenging Behaviour Foundation, The Old Courthouse, New Road Avenue, Chatham, Kent, ME4 6BE What we do We currently have a number of peer support services that families may find helpful. Our Family Carer Email Network gives families the chance to ask questions and post answers anonymously from people who just ‘get it’. We have a Carers’ Catch-Up session that offers families the chance to chat via Zoom. Our listening ear appointments give family carers the opportunity to speak to someone trained in active listening and offer a safe space to chat, vent, laugh or cry on a 1:1 basis. title: Peer Support Group
address: Dartford What we do Our peer support group is a 12 week programme based in Dartford, offering a 2 hour session based around building positive relationships, recovery and making changes that benefit our lives. Our 2 hour sessions will then be followed up with a further 2 hours activity. This could be going for a coffee, having a group meal, going bowling or watching a film together. Our peer groups will rely on what the attendees need and their own ideas and suggestions, ensuring our peer support programme is entirely co-produced by ourselves and our attendees. We will also be offering one to one individual support or telephone one-to-one sessions, motivating behaviour, inspiring recovery, and engagement with our services Each client would be allocated to a Wellbeing Coach for a total of 12 weeks. Self refer via the website!
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email wellbeing@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Bipolar UK Peer Support Line
address: Virtual What we do Feeling worried or anxious? Got a question? We’re here to give practical information, advice and support by phone and email. Call us on 0333 323 3880 leave a message and we’ll call you back. Or email us at info@bipolaruk.org With an emphasis on self-management, the Peer Support Line provides: advice on how to support a loved one information on how to manage your bipolar coping strategies to stay well links to useful support services We can talk through any issues that are on your mind and share our own experiences as people also affected by bipolar. We’re here to help you find a way forward. Please note: the Peer Support Line is open to UK phone numbers only. You must be based in the UK to access this service and be aged 18 or over. Even if you have a UK number, we are unable to speak to you if you’re outside the UK at the time of the call. This service does not provide crisis support, but you can find information around crisis support here. If you’d like to talk over email you can contact us at info@bipolaruk.org with any questions, concerns or information you need. We aim to respond within three working days, although during holidays or busier periods it may take slightly longer. Thank you for your patience. Find out more about our service here.
price: Free Organisation Bipolar UK Telephone 0333 323 3880 Email info@bipolaruk.org Organisation Bipolar UK

title: Fusion Pantomime – Snow White and the Seven Tall People
address: Holy Family Church Hall, Bicknor Rd, Maidstone ME15 9PS What we do Mirror, mirror on the wall… the fairest panto of them all is coming to Fusion this December! For the third year in a row Fusion are bringing you a magical adventure! This year Snow White takes to the stage with her seven tall friends, a wicked Queen, and plenty of surprises along the way! Get ready for bigger laughs, dazzling performances, and magical moments the whole family will love. We have two shows on Saturday the 6th December and this year both shows come with a meal! The Matinee show is at 2pm, doors 1:30pm. Tickets are £3 each or £10 for a family ticket (2 adults, 2 children) The Evening show is at 6pm, doors 5:30pm. Tickets are £5 each or £18 for a family ticket (2 adults, 2 children) Don’t miss out – tickets are ON SALE NOW! Book yours today before they’re gone! To do so pop into the office today, or phone 01622 691177. There will be an option to book online soon, details to follow soon! If you are driving to the show you will need to register you car for parking on arrival; to do this you will need to use the tablet that is in the Holy Family Church Hall.

title: PMRGCA Helpline
address: PMRGCAuk, BM, London, WC1N 3XX What we do We run a telephone Helpline, where our volunteers who have experience of PMR and/or GCA can talk with you and offer support. You can call the Helpline Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm. The number is 0300 111 5090. Please remember we are unable to give medical advice and we always advise that you talk to your doctor. If your call is not answered, please do leave a short message and your contact details, and we will get back to you as soon as possible.

title: Positively Connected Kent – Dartford, Gravesham & Swanley (DGS)
address: Groundwork South, Riverside Centre, Dickens Rd, Gravesend DA12 2JY What we do To ensure every person in Kent has the energy they need to live with dignity, comfort and opportunity – regardless of income, circumstance or postcode. Positively Connected Kent is more than a service… it’s a movement. A partnership of organisations across Kent and Medway working together to tackle energy inequality and make sure support reaches the people who need it most. We believe that no one should have to choose between heating and living. By coming together, we are creating a joined-up system of support that is easier to access, fairer, and built around people. A partnership for change We are starting this journey with strong local partners: Medway Voluntary Action , Social Enterprise Kent , and Groundwork , with Involve leading the partnership. These organisations, along with hopefully many to come, are working together to to create county-wide energy support. Together, we are building something bigger than any single organisation, a connected and coordinated response to energy inequality.

title: Post Natal Illness Helpline
address: Fulham Park House, 1a Chesilton Road, Fulham SW6 5AA What we do Charity offering a helpline, online chat and email advice and support to mums and families affected by postnatal illness. CALL US Call us on 0207 386 0868 (from 10.00am to 2.00pm Monday to Friday) Or CHAT TO US Chat with us using the Chat box on our website. REQUEST A VOLUNTEER If you are suffering from postnatal illness we do advise you to try talking to one of our volunteers. You can call our helpline on 0207 386 0868 (Monday to Friday) or email us at info@apni.org and we will gladly answer any enquiries that you may have. Postnatally depressed women may feel unable to go out of their house, and meeting people may cause them a great deal of stress, therefore contact made on the phone or by e-mail can be ideal. Women often find that talking, or writing to someone, who has had the illness and recovered, allows them to discuss the most distressing symptoms of the illness. The phone and e-mail volunteers give women hope that they will eventually recover. This service is also available to women who would prefer to be supported by post. The Association has a countrywide network of phone and e-mail volunteers, who have had, and recovered from post-natal illness. Our opening hours are Monday to Friday 10am to 2pm.
price: Free Organisation Association for Post Natal Illness Telephone 0207 386 0868 Email info@apni.org Organisation Association for Post Natal Illness

title: Professional and affordable counselling
address: Sevenoaks Counselling, The Bridge, Littlecourt Road, Sevenoaks, Kent, TN13 2JG What we do Sevenoaks Counselling is a professional service offered to the whole community and we have provided a trusted, affordable counselling service to Sevenoaks and the surrounding district since 1983. title: Rainbow Call – LGBT Telephone Befriending
address: Virtual What we do Rainbow Call is a UK-wide, LGBT+ specific service for older people 75+ called rainbow call companion. title: Tension & Trauma Release Exercises
address: St Faiths Centre, Moncktons Lsne, Maidstone ME14 2PY What we do We offer funded weekly sessions to teach regulation skills and the TRE™ technique, which supports the nervous system to come back into balance through reflexive muscle vibrations, over time supporting and promoting personal health and wellness. (Please note – 1. If you have a history of seizures or epilepsy, it is important to consult with your doctor before attempting TRE.   2. If you have a herniated disk or other serious back injury, you should not attempt TRE without first consulting with a medical professional. 3. If you are experiencing acute pain or discomfort or have recently undergone surgery or a major medical procedure, it is recommended to wait until you have fully recovered before attempting TRE.  4. If you are pregnant or have recently given birth, it is best to avoid TRE until after childbirth and until you have fully recovered.  5. If you have a history of serious mental health conditions such as schizophrenia or bipolar disorder, you should avoid TRE without consulting with a mental health professional.  Overall, it is always recommended to consult with a medical professional before attempting any new exercise or wellness program, especially if you have any pre-existing medical conditions. ) When? Fridays 10 – 11.30am
price: Free Organisation Kenward Trust Email sian.williams@kenwardtrust.org.uk Organisation Kenward Trust

title: Recovery College Health & Wellbeing Courses and Workshops
address: Virtual or in person in Thanet, Canterbury, Ashford, Folkestone & Dover, Maidstone, Medway, Dartford, Gravesend & Swanley What we do The Kent & Medway Recovery & Wellbeing College (RC) provides health & wellbeing related educational courses, for Kent or Medway residents over 18yrs of age. All courses are provided free of charge and are accessible via self-enrolment ( please see webpage for online enrolment link ). All courses are provided in welcoming, supportive, accessible and safe community learning spaces across the district or remotely for those who cannot come in person. Through shared learning and education, people can make sense of their experiences and enhance self-care knowledge and skills – for themselves and others. We believe that by recognising resourcefulness, talents and skills, people become experts in their own health and wellbeing, make better informed choices and achieve what they want in life. We currently provide courses Virtually and at venues in Thanet, Canterbury, Ashford, Folkestone & Dover, Maidstone, Medway, Dartford, Gravesend & Swanley View our website and prospectus here.

title: Rethink Medway Peer Support Group
address: Improvement Room, 2nd Floor Balmoral Gardens HLC, Gillingham, Kent ME7 4PN What we do We are a monthly peer support group led by individuals with lived experience of mental health challenges. Our role is to offer understanding, connection, and a safe place to talk. We do not provide counselling, therapy, or advice, but we are able to signpost to services that attendees request. What we do offer each month is a confidential, supportive space where anyone experiencing mental health difficulties or social isolation can meet others, share openly without judgement, and feel heard. Confidentiality is always respected, with the only exception being situations where someone may be at risk of harm to themselves or others — in those cases, we would involve the appropriate services to ensure safety. When : We meet monthly on the 2nd Monday of each calendar month from 13:00 until 15:00 Where : Improvement Room, 2nd Floor Balmoral Gardens HLC, Gillingham, Kent ME7 4PN

title: Rethink Sahayak one to one support DGS & Ashford & Group Sessions DGS
address: Rethink Sahayak, 4 - 5 High Street, Gravesend, DA11 0BQ What we do One-to-one individual support – provided through referrals including Professionals, self-referral or family/friends, offering practical and emotional support also advice and information, available for DGS and Ashford. Rethink Sahayak groups –A key opportunity to experience educational enhancement on mental health, where to access support and learn about culturally specific topics which impact minoritised communities i.e., domestic abuse. Groups are offered 2 hour sessions for both men and women and available for individuals in DGS. Rethink Sangam group – A peer led support group, focusing on Mental H & wellbeing through activities, combatting isolation, and aiding with mental health recovery, takes place weekly on a Weds (10am – 12pm).

title: Rewrite Your Story
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48, Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do Rewrite Your Story is a charitable organisation founded in 2017, supporting the mental health of children & young people, adults, families, professionals, carers and everyone else inbetween! Our holistic systemic approach across all sectors has a special focus on raising awareness and early intervention. Our story began with a clinical study funded by the NHS clinical commissioning groups within North and East Kent. We researched the effectiveness of early intervention during our work promoting and protecting the emotional wellbeing of children and young people.

address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do These are social groups for young people with Down’s syndrome aged 11 to early 20s. Following on from speech and language therapy groups for younger children, the club is split into three sessions; smiLE, skills and social. The young people will follow a block programme of smiLE therapy, a social time to connect with others and skills sessions focussing on communication, relationships, community and identity.
