title: Safe and Well Visit
address: Kent Fire and Rescue Service, The Godlands, Straw Mill Hill, Maidstone, ME15 6XB What we do Safe and Well visits allow us to offer our customers advice on staying safe in their home. As well as providing advice, our safe and well officers may fit fire safety equipment such as smoke alarms free of charge, help plan escape routes and more, with a typical visit lasting around 45 minutes. Link to the referral form can be found below. Those eligible for a free visit include those aged over 65, children under 5 years of age, pregnant woman, smokers and those living with a disability or long term health condition.

title: Samaritans Face-to-Face
address: https://www.samaritans.org/branches/ What we do If you’d like to talk to someone face-to-face in a quiet, private space, some of our branches are offering in-person visits. Please check the details for your local branch to make sure this service is offered before visiting in person. There’s no pressure and no judgement from us, and no ‘right’ or ‘wrong’ thing you can say. You don’t even have tell us your name if you don’t want to. Find your local branch, and check their opening hours on our website . Our branches tend to be located in residential houses or other small buildings that have been donated to us.

title: Samaritans Helpline
address: https://www.samaritans.org/ What we do Whatever you’re going through, a Samaritan will face it with you. We’re here 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. 116 123 is the number and it’s free to call. We are now open every day from 11am to 10pm (except Sunday when we are open 5pm to 10pm). Write us an email Sometimes writing down your thoughts and feelings can help you understand them better. jo@samaritans.org Response time: It may take several days to get a response by email. Write a letter Sometimes writing down your thoughts and feelings can help you better understand them. title: SANE Helpline
address: Virtual What we do SANEline is a national out-of-hours mental health helpline offering specialist emotional support, guidance and information to anyone affected by mental illness, including family, friends and carers. SANE`s Services remain open every day of the year from 4pm to 10pm on 0300 304 7000. SANE will retain the callback service we opened during the pandemic, so you can still leave us a message on 07984 967 708 giving us your first name and phone number, and we will call you back within a few days. Alternatively, visit our Emotional Support page to see ways we can support you by email or text. The helpline is a confidential service for those aged 16 or over.
price: Free Organisation SANE Telephone 0300 304 7000 Email info@sane.org.uk Organisation SANE

title: Seated Exercise Group
address: ellenor, Coldharbour Road, Gravesend, DA11 7HQ What we do A low impact exercise session, aimed at getting people moving to the sound of music whilst sitting in a chair, within a fun and sociable atmosphere. The group caters to a range of functional abilities with the optional use of light weights for added resistance. It is particularly suited to anyone who finds it difficult to exercise whilst standing, suffers from fatigue, or has a condition which limits movement or breathing. Physical benefits include; improvements in mobility, strength, range of movement, co-ordination and overall fitness levels, with increased exercise tolerance. It is also a great place to meet and interact with a range of people from different walks of life and build a support network of friends. If unable to attend in person, the seated exercise sessions can also be accessed live via Facebook on Thursdays from the comfort of your own home, with the videos uploaded to Facebook after each group to view at any time. Every Tuesday & Thursday from 11.00am until 12.00 noon.
price: Free Organisation ellenor Telephone 01474 320007 Email info@ellenor.org Organisation ellenor

title: Self-Pay Therapeutic Services
address: Blackthorn Trust, St Andrew’s Road, Maidstone, ME16 9AN. What we do Blackthorn Trust is a Health and Community Hub offering life enhancing programmes and therapy for overall wellbeing. Our holistic therapies address ongoing conditions including mental health challenges, persisting pain, fatigue and long Covid. There are two self-pay options available: individual therapy combination of individual therapy and creative workshop/group activity People attending our self-pay therapeutic services help fund Blackthorn Trust’s work supporting the local community. Individual Therapies Our holistic therapies address the whole person and we endeavour to support you to manage your symptoms while at the same time strengthening your confidence and motivation. The individual therapies we offer are listed below (click on the therapy name for more details): Arts Counselling / Biographical Counselling / CranioSacral Therapy / Eurythmy Therapy / Metal Colour Light Therapy / Rhythmical Massage Therapy Individual therapy sessions are £55 and there are discount packages for pre-paid treatment blocks. You can find out more by visiting our website.

title: Send an Email
address: Virtual What we do Send an email to a Women’s Aid domestic abuse support worker. This email address is for any women and children who are experiencing, or have experienced, domestic abuse, friends, family, colleagues and neighbours seeking to support women and children who are experiencing, or have experienced, domestic abuse, and professionals supporting women and children experiencing domestic abuse. You can also find more information regarding our dedicated service for professionals here. We will respond to your email within 5 working days. Email us on helpline@womensaid.org.uk *When you email us it’s very important that you specify if it is safe to respond to your email address. This would mean that no-one else has access to that email account. If you are emailing from what appears to be a shared email account, we will not be able to respond for safety reasons. If we have reason to believe that your email address is unsafe to respond to or your devices are unsafe (for example, if you tell us you’re not sure if your abuser has access to your account), we may be unable to respond.*
price: Free Organisation Women's Aid Email helpline@womensaid.org.uk Organisation Women's Aid

title: Service User Network (SUN)
address: Kent and Medway What we do What is SUN? The Service User Network (SUN) is a free, and easy to access peer support service for adults experiencing difficulties with complex emotions often associated with a personality disorder. The service provides ongoing, open-ended support in the form of groups, aiming to build member’s connections with others experiencing similar difficulties, fostering an inclusive and mutually supportive environment. How does SUN work? We are excited to be working in partnership with Kent and Medway Social Care and Partnership Trust (KMPT) to deliver The Service User Network (SUN) across Kent and Medway! Mind in Kent are providing the Lived Experience element of the SUN model, and are working together with KMPT’s Clinical Facilitators to enable SUN groups to run both in person and online at a variety of locations across the county. Who is SUN for? You do not need a diagnosis to access SUN and may, or may not, be accessing other support alongside SUN. SUN is for anyone who: Is aged 18+, and registered with a GP in Kent or Medway Describes themselves as having complex emotional needs or difficulties with intense emotions and Wants to be empowered to manage challenging situations and relationships Wants to learn from others who have had similar experiences Looking for additional support in the community. How do I access SUN? SUN membership is through self-referral which involves completing a short registration form along with a questionnaire. To request a registration form or to find out more (including our current group timetable), you can contact the team on the details below: Email: sun.kentandmedway@nhs.net | Tel: 01622 926802 (answerphone only) | SUN Website
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01622 926802 Email sun.kentandmedway@nhs.net Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Sevenoaks (Loaves & Fishes) Foodbank
address: Sevenoaks(Loaves & Fishes) Foodbank, Hall of St John the Baptist church, 62 Quakers Hall lane, Sevenoaks, Kent, TN13 3TX What we do We provide long lasting and fresh food as well as hygiene and cleaning products to people in need in Sevenoaks and the surrounding area. The foodbank is open on Thursdays between 1pm and 3pm for people to choose and collect their food – we do not deliver. In emergency situations, people can self refer through our website https://sevenoaksfoodbank.co.uk/get-help-now/ and are welcome for two weeks to collect any item. After that, they will need to obtain a referral from another charity such as CAB, Crosslight, WKHA, etc. We also run a free café where people are welcome to have chat with our volunteers or other clients. We have weekly visitors from other charities who are happy to provide more help and support. Please visit our website or contact us for more information.
price: Free Organisation Sevenoaks (Loaves & Fishes) Foodbank Telephone info@sevenoaksfoodbank.co.uk Email sevenoaksfoodbank@gmail.com Organisation Sevenoaks (Loaves & Fishes) Foodbank

title: Sheppey Leisure Centre
address: Sheppey Leisure Complex, Sheerness, Kent, ME12 1HH What we do Sheppey Leisure Centre is run by Swale Community Leisure . At Sheppey Leisure Complex we offer a fantastic range of facilities suitable for all ages and abilities. The Healthy Living Building includes a fully air-conditioned gym, a versatile sports hall and a workout area where we hold a variety of workout classes. Our Pool Side Building includes two swimming pools and a Health Studio. Our brilliant Health Studio is complete with Shapemaster power-assisted exercise equipment suitable for everybody, irrespective of age, mobility, weight or fitness level. title: Shine (Gillingham)
address: St Augustine's Church, Rock Avenue, Gillingham, ME7 5PW What we do Shine is a 7 week course with the aim of building self-esteem, self-worth and a sense of purpose in women and girls. The Shine Course helps girls and women to better understand their value, strengths and potential. The course celebrates everyone’s uniqueness, and explores the fact that we all have the power of choice and a voice; that every girl and woman can purposefully make decisions that shape their future in a positive way, set goals and build confidence to live out a life filled with purpose. Please contact us for more information.

title: SignHealth Domestic Abuse Service
address: Services offered nationwide What we do We support deaf victims/survivors who are experiencing domestic or sexual abuse. The support includes: Allocation of qualified deaf IDVA (Independent Domestic Violence Advisor) Working with women’s refuges to provide support to deaf women Referral when necessary to SignHealth’s one-to-one psychological therapy in British Sign Language (BSL) Freedom Programme workshops for deaf survivors Working in partnership with key national and local agencies to develop guidelines for best practice Guidance on laws, policy and practice to ensure the needs of deaf people at risk of domestic abuse are recognised and fully met Workshops for the community and sector professionals. Visit our website for more information.

title: Singing Back The Memories
address: Around North Kent and Swale What we do A group of people who are living with or caring for someone with dementia, who enjoy coming together, belting out their favourite tunes and having a laugh in-between. Singing can help a person living with dementia connect with the past by evoking memories, feelings and emotions that they might otherwise find hard to express. If you enjoy singing (even if you can’t sing!) and having a good laugh with a group of like-minded people, why not give Singing Back the Memories a try! The Singing Back The Memories group takes place in the following areas: • Northfleet • Dartford • Swanley • Sheppey • Faversham • Sittingbourne • Leysdown Please contact us on 01474 533990 or email info@adss.org.uk for more information or to book a place.

title: Slow Cooker Course
address: Fusion HLC, 2 & 3 Cobb Way, Off Livingstone Walk, Park Wood, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 9XF What we do Eight week course that runs at various points throughout the year at Fusion Maidstone. Participants keep the slow cooker if they attend all 8 sessions. At the heart of this course is healthy eating using a slow cooker. Participants prepare a recipe each week to take home and cook in their slow cooker. A second recipe is also demonstrated which participants can cook at home if they wish. Sessions also look at stress management, the benefits of healthy eating, particularly in relation to the heart, and the benefits of moderate exercise. Spaces are strictly limited and must be booked in advance. Please contact us for more information.
price: Free Organisation Fusion HLC Telephone 01622 691177 Email enquiries@fusionhlc.org.uk Organisation Fusion HLC

title: Social & Emotional Wellbeing Group
address: Asda Community Room, Thames Way, Gravesend DA11 0DQ What we do MEGAN CIC offer support for adults (aged 17+) who have mental health issues living in Medway, Swale, West Kent, Dartford, Gravesham and Swanley. We offer an open-ended membership and run a programme of peer support groups for individuals experiencing depression, anxiety, bipolar, personality disorders and neurodiverse conditions. We also offer informal sessions including coffee & chat groups, workshops and social groups to reduce social isolation and awareness sessions to support service users. title: Social Day Care (Maidstone)
address: Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge, Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Linton Road, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 0AL What we do Our day centre services provide an opportunity for clients who are at risk of social isolation a chance to interact with like minded people and reduce loneliness. Whether you are enquiring for yourself or someone you know that would like to make some new friends or think it’s just about time to try something new, Age UK Maidstones day centre services could be the answer. Our clients enjoy a two course lunch, hot and cold refreshments and snacks throughout their day with us. The clients can enjoy various activities including bingo, games and puzzles. Occasionally we provide live entertainment for all to enjoy and at Christmas we organize Christmas lunches and parties. Our day centre currently runs every Tuesday and Friday from 10am until 2:30pm. The centre is situated in Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Linton Road, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 0AL. The price is £20 for the day including lunch and refreshments. If you live on one of our transport links and require transport there is an additional charge of £17. We will be able to confirm whether transport is available to you when you refer for the service.
price: £20 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Social Day Care (Sevenoaks)
address: Hollybush Court, Hollybush Close, Sevenoaks, TN13 3UX What we do Our Hollybush Day Centre in Sevenoaks provides people in later life with the opportunity to meet new people, make new friends, to enjoy a nutritional hot meal and to take part in a full range of recreational activities. Optional activities may include: Bingo Quizzes Reminiscence sessions Sing-alongs Indoor games Seated exercises Active games to promote movement Entertainment is also regularly provided by local performers, and relevant talks given by local authorities such as the Police and the Fire Brigade. General Day Care is currently available on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We may be able to provide minibus transport to take you to or from the Day Centre.

title: Social Farming and Gardening
address: AAHEP, High Lees Farm House, Wagon Lane, Paddock Wood, TN12 6PT What we do AAHEP has access to over 35 acres of land on which it grows its hay, cares for various animals, including a herd 14 horses, and maintains private orchard. Social farming provides a person-centred approach, focusing on the whole person abilities rather than limitations, which promote positive engagement and the development of practical skills. It also builds social inclusion, expanding social contacts and promoting greater involvement in community life for those who may otherwise be socially isolated. We work with other organisations to provide solutions that are appropriate for their learners and service users.
price: Prices are framed around solutions adapted for organisational requirements, as well as the opportunity of accessing funding. title: Social Prescribing
address: Registered address: Involve Kent, 30, Turkey Court, Maidstone, ME14 5PP What we do If you feel as though you could benefit from extra support and would like to take steps to improving your wellbeing, our Social Prescribing Link Workers are here to help. Our Link Workers can meet with you regularly to listen and offer guidance in a safe and confidential one to one setting, by connecting you to a range of support. This support can include mental health support, housing support, transport, exercise classes, gardening groups, singing groups and much more. If you think you might benefit from seeing a Social Prescribing Link Worker, please contact your GP surgery in the first instance.
price: Free Organisation Involve Kent Telephone 03000 810005 Email hello@involvekent.org.uk Organisation Involve Kent

title: Something to Look Forward to: Project Kent
address: Registered address: Something To Look Forward To, 84, Coleridge Road, Cambridge, CB1 3PJ What we do To promote the well-being, relieve the stress and aid the recuperation of those who are experiencing the effects of cancer and its treatment, together with their families. In particular by the provision of positive individual and/or family experiences. To provide a comprehensive website where people with cancer and their families can access a variety of free ‘gifts’ donated by companies and individuals. These include free breaks in holiday cottages; restaurant meals; a day at the races; hotel stays; theatre tickets; beauty treatments; tickets for attractions and much more.

title: Songsters (Dementia singing group)
address: Hythe Sports Pavilion, South Road, Hythe, CT21 6AH What we do Songsters is a fun and rewarding inclusive music, dementia and wellbeing singing group, for people living with a dementia, people in caregiving roles and Health & Social Care professionals. Delivered by Musical Walkabout CIC, we are committed to improving the provision of creative care, supporting participants to gain confidence and enjoy themselves, and leverage the power of music to support wellbeing in our communities impacted by dementia. We are a welcoming group, and no musical experience is necessary to take part. We encourage PLWD and Caregivers to attend together where possible, for maximum benefits, and promote an ethos of daily music engagement. 1hr sessions are joyous social experiences, widely enjoyed by everyone involved. We have a series of 20 sessions between Aug 2024 – March 2025. These sessions are on selected Mondays at either 11.00am or 14.15pm. Please contact Nina via email to attend, or for the full list of session dates.
price: Free Organisation Musical Walkabout CIC Telephone 07977924124 Email nina@musicalwalkabout.com Organisation Musical Walkabout CIC

title: Space 2 Talk
address: Various locations - check website What we do Our Space 2 talk meetings are for parents and carers of children with additional needs or disabilities. This is an opportunity to connect with other parents and speak to one of our Family Support Officers. Providing support and advice on: Emotions and Behaviour Concerns Health Education Transport Social Care issues. These sessions are open to all parent/carers in West Kent (boroughs of Maidstone, Tonbridge & Malling and Tunbridge Wells), who have a young person with additional needs. No formal diagnosis is required to access any of our services. We will be using a booking system for these sessions. To book, please go to https://space2beme.org.uk/services/space2talk/

title: Specialist Baby Loss Counselling
address: PO BOX 741, Rochester, Kent ME1 9PZ What we do Abigail’s Footsteps offers funded baby loss counselling sessions, provided by qualified counsellors, we recognise that losing a baby has an impact on the entire family and that each family member can grieve in different ways. We support bereaved parents who have experienced late miscarriage (from 16 weeks onwards) termination for medical reasons, stillbirths and neonatal deaths. We have 3 locations where we can offer baby loss counselling in Kent and the surrounding areas. You can choose from one of our locations for face-to-face sessions, in DA9, TN2 and TN14. There is also the option of online sessions if suitable.

title: 3D Care Specialist Parenting Service
address: Various What we do Our nationwide Parenting service specialises in working with families with deafness. This can be deaf parents with deaf or hearing children or hearing parents with deaf children. Our aim is to create better parenting through access to information, advice and support. Our Parenting Mentors are deaf, and fully trained in parenting skills, with significant experience of frontline Children and Family work. We cater for many different communication needs including: British Sign Language (BSL), Sign Supported English (SSE), speech and lipreading, Deafblind communication and visual methods. Services include parent advocacy, parenting workshops, Deaf parenting awareness sessions for professionals, and more.

title: Spill the Tea
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, Marsham Street, Maidstone, UK What we do “Spill the Tea” is a fantastic eight-week program designed to support new and expectant parents. It’s all about providing a holistic experience that covers everything from practical advice to self-care. The program brings together experienced professionals who share their knowledge on breastfeeding, caring for newborns, and navigating those early parenting days. You’ll learn valuable tips on baby care, understand the amazing changes your body goes through, and discover effective ways to manage your time and stay organised. But “Spill the Tea” is more than just learning. It’s also about taking care of yourself. The program includes access to professional wellness services, like hair and nail treatments. These sessions take place on a Wednesday from 11am until 2pm. Contact Philly Scott to find out more!
price: Free Organisation Temple of Kulture Telephone 07756077110 Email info@templeofkulture.co.uk Organisation Temple of Kulture

title: Sports Clinic
address: ESO Teaching Clinic, 104, Tonbridge Road, Maidstone, ME15 8SL What we do The Sports Clinic is staffed by final year students who have a keen interest in the subject. It offers individual, holistic advice and treatment for amateur and professional sportspeople. Practitioners investigate the cause of the injury, assess the individual’s training methods and tailor advice and rehabilitation to the patient and their sport; the Sports Clinic team, and their patients, also have exclusive access to the Clinic’s rehabilitation room. As well as injury treatment and prevention, the team looks to aid performance through: increased awareness; improved strength and flexibility; a focused mindset. The usual price for treatments is: New Patient £30, Continuing Patient £23 or evening £26. Please see our website for more information https://bcnogroup.ac.uk/clinics/eso-kent/
price: Set charge Organisation European School of Osteopathy Telephone 01622 685989 Email info.eso@bcnogroup.ac.uk Organisation European School of Osteopathy

title: St. Matt’s Community Larder
address: The Bethel Hall, High Brooms Road, Tunbridge Wells, Kent, TN4 9DA What we do Our community larder offers good quality food for a small donation to anyone who needs it. No referral is needed. Just turn up on a Friday morning between 9.30am – 10.45am at the Bethel Hall on High Brooms Road and you can get a big bag of food and hygiene products for a donation of £5. There is a large variety of fresh, frozen and tinned foods. We also run a café alongside the larder each Friday, where you can sit, chill, chat …and eat cake! The cost of living is affecting so many people, so everyone is welcome. Please contact us or visit our website for more information.

title: St Peter & St Paul’s Church Aylesford food bank
address: The Brassey Centre, Station Road, Aylesford, Kent, ME20 7QR What we do We operate a food bank that will provide food to anyone in the local community who is struggling to put food in the table, whatever the reason or their circumstances. If you find yourself struggling to put food on the table then please get in touch with us as our wish is that no one in the community should go hungry. Our foodbank is open for collection of pre-booked food parcels twice a week usually on Tuesdays from 5-7pm and on Saturdays from 10am-12pm. How to get a food parcel Food parcels should be requested in one of the following ways: By email (aylesfordfoodbank@gmail.com) By phone ( 07877 074029 9am-5pm Mon-Sat) By completing the form on our website here You can find more information about our foodbank on our website

address: The Stones Community Trust, Gallagher Stadium, James Whatman Way, Maidstone, Kent ME14 1LQ What we do Stones Raiders provides footballing opportunities for adults and children who have disabilities or mental health difficulties, in a fun friendly and supportive environment. A place where everybody can feel part of our team. Established in 2004, the Raiders are the disability, mental health and Deaf part of the Maidstone United Football Club family. We offer PAN disability footballing opportunities to both children and adults in a fun and supportive environment. As proud members of the Kent Disability Football League we have teams in every division and are defending champions of several of them. As part of the Stones in the community, we help create opportunities for people who often feel marginalised or excluded from the local community. When : Saturdays from 9.30am Booking : Send us an email at lorraine.humm@stonesinthecommunity.org

title: StrongMen Weekenders
address: Registered address: Amelia House, Crescent Road, Worthing, BN11 1RL What we do StrongMen supports any man over the age of 18 who has suffered the bereavement of another significant person at any point in their life and who is currently finding life a struggle. The StrongMen ‘Weekender Retreat’ is our unique premier service, where groups of men have the opportunity to relax and enjoy beautiful remote surroundings whilst engaging in conversation and physical activities in a safe, secure environment with other men with shared experiences. The Weekender Retreat seeks to quickly develop long lasting brotherhoods unique to each group, building on the shared experience of the weekend.
price: Free Organisation StrongMen Telephone 0800 915 0400 Email hello@StrongMen.org.uk Organisation StrongMen

title: Subsidised Counselling
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48, Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do Counselling at subsidised rates, with additional concessionary rates for those on benefits. Prices vary according to clents. We have x3 BACP counsellors with a variety of experience clients who are living with a variety of impairing conditions impacting their well being, we also address difficulties experienced by young people/adults experiencing difficulties with ADHD and ASD. People can self refer via our website www.hearsay-trust.org.uk We aim to respond to referrals within 48hrs. We have an additional service, Equine Therapy. This service has proved very successful particularly for young people/adolescents suffering from poor self esteem, anxiety.

title: Support and Guidance for Newly Diagnosed Cancer Patients
address: Virtual What we do We offer multidimensional supervised support and guidance for newly diagnosed cancer patients. We would take a baseline assessment of your functional ability, anxiety levels and nutritional assessment. Any other chronic lifestyle conditions (smoking for example) that you may need help managing would be assessed. Following the assessment we would design a specific programme to prepare you for your cancer treatment- be it an operation, chemo, immune or radio therapy. Supervised and personalised exercise sessions twice a week, individualised to your health, condition, lifestyle and ability, shall commence. A session with a therapist to help with anxiety management and building resilience (and more if needed) is offered. You can also download a personalised and specialist Prehab app, to your smart phone, for more healthy lifestyle advice. Virtual sessions are offered in the safety of your home or venue of your choice via telephone or video calling.
price: Free Organisation Quest Prehab Telephone 07521 077097 Email tara.rampal@questprehab.com Organisation Quest Prehab

title: Support at Home
address: Safeharbour Memory Wellbeing Centre, Coldharbour Road, Northfleet, Kent, DA11 8AE What we do Support at Home is a specialist support service offering one-to-one support for people in their own homes. Do you have problems going out on your own? Have you lost your confidence to do the things that you used to do, or need a little help? Our Support at Home service is provided by staff who have been trained in dementia care and understand how important it is for everyone to maintain their independence. Our support workers will work with you to help you to maintain your interests and skills; they can come shopping with you, accompany you on outings or visits, help you manage bills and correspondence, or just visit you at home to help you with whatever activities you enjoy. The Support at Home Service is available for a minimum of 2 hours a week or for as long as you’d like. When : Service can be provided Monday – Sunday 7 am – 10 pm. Prices are available on request.
price: Available on request Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS) Telephone 01474 533990 Email info@adss.org.uk Organisation Alzheimer's and Dementia Support Services (ADSS)

title: Support for Homeless Families
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do We offer a range of services to homeless families, including temporary accommodation as an alternative to B&B whilst awaiting a formal housing offer from Local Authorities, to a full support service which enables people to identify the reasons for becoming homeless in order to avoid these in the future. Staff work on tenancy sustainment and resilience with individuals in order to ensure future or repeated homelessness is avoided. Where support is available/commissioned, key workers will work closely with other agencies to ensure the family receives the tailored support needed to meet the needs of each family member, making resettlement more achievable and providing a more stable future for the children and entire family unit. This ensures that the homeless children today do not become the homeless adults of the future.

title: Support for People with Mental Health Difficulties
address: Lilly Smith House, 15, Knightrider Street, Maidstone, ME15 6LP What we do Riverside offers a full range of services for people experiencing mental health difficulties from 24 hour supported accommodation to support promoting recovery in the community. We aim for our customers to progress to living the most fulfilling role they can within society, so support is tapered as customers gain more independence. Riverside’s mental health pathway of services includes, recovery accommodation services to prevent hospital admissions during mental health crisis, Step-down accommodation services for customers leaving specialist inpatient services, 24-hour care services for customers with moderate care and support needs, Community-based floating support for people in their own homes who are on the way to recovery and who now only need low level support to maintain independence.
price: Unknown Organisation Riverside Telephone 0345 111 0000 Email info@riverside.org.uk Organisation Riverside
description: Riverside offers a full range of services for people experiencing mental health difficulties from 24 hour supported accommodation to support promoting recovery in the community. We aim for our customers to progress to living the most fulfilling role they can within society, so support is tapered as customers gain more independence. Riverside’s mental health pathway of services includes, recovery accommodation services to prevent hospital admissions during mental health crisis, Step-down accommodation services for customers leaving specialist inpatient services, 24-hour care services for customers with moderate care and support needs, Community-based floating support for people in their own homes who are on the way to recovery and who now only need low level support to maintain independence. Price Unknown
categories: Independent living and help at home; Support Services and Self-help

title: Bipolar UK Support Group (Maidstone)
address: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48, Marsham Street, Maidstone, ME14 1HH What we do Our support groups are free and open to anyone affected by bipolar, including family, friends and carers. If you don’t have a confirmed diagnosis and want to find out about bipolar, and related conditions such as cyclothymia, you’re also welcome. Maidstone Support Group details: Meets: 2nd Monday of each month. Please note that there will be no meeting in January. Location: Maidstone Community Support Centre, 39-48 Marsham Street, Maidstone, Kent, ME14 1HH Time: 7.30pm – 9.30pm The format of the meetings vary, but you will have the opportunity to introduce yourself, say how you are feeling using the Bipolar UK mood scale and may listen to an invited speaker. The groups are organised and facilitated by volunteers who are affected by bipolar in some way. If you’re interested in joining the group meetings, please email supportgroups@bipolaruk.org with “Maidstone 2nd Monday support group” as the subject line. Anyone over 18 is welcome. If you’re 16 or 17, please bring a parent or guardian. You can find out more information here.

title: Support Groups (West Kent)
address: 10-15 Notre Dame Mews, National Office for the MND Association, David Niven House, Hunton Road, Northampton, NN1 2BG What we do The West Kent Branch of the Motor Neurone Disease Association has the sole aim of serving all those people in the area affected by Motor Neurone Disease (MND). This includes people living with MND their family, carers and friends. We hold regular ‘drop in’ support meetings. title: Supported Accommodation
address: Pathways to Independence, 25, Victoria Street, Rochester, ME1 1XJ What we do Pathways to Independence offer supported accommodation to those in Medway who are homeless and those in Kent and Medway who homeless and have offending in their background. Please remember that in order to access our supported housing you must have additional support needs. These could include one or more of the following: a history of offending (we support all categories of offenders aged 18 or over, including those who have committed more serious offences), a need for support in managing a tenancy or to live independently, debt and difficulties accessing benefits and inability to manage money, an addiction to drugs or alcohol and difficulties with gaining employment. title: Supported Housing
address: Dartford, Gravesham, Swanley What we do North Kent Mind provide a supported housing service for 34 people who have experienced mental health problems and need support to live independently. Accommodation is provided in 6 sites in the area: Dartford a block of 8 self-contained flats near the town centre. North fleet a block of 6 self-contained flats. Gravesend a large house converted into 5 self-contained studio flats, and also 2 self-contained flats within one house. Block of 7 self-contained flats Swanley 6 self-contained flats within one house. Most of the schemes also contain a communal room where tenants can meet together if they wish. All the flats are for single people. Living in North Kent Mind Accommodation: We offer short-hold tenancies – which means that tenants living within them are expected to become independent enough to move on to general housing within two years. We do not offer accommodation for longer than 24 months. Our service is a low-level Supported Housing Scheme. Staff visit the properties, but are not available on site. Our tenants receive low level of support, which means they will have around 2 hours support from their allocated support worker per week. All flats are fully furnished and equipped when a tenant moves in. Rent payments are normally covered by Housing Benefits if the individual is eligible to claim them. Council tax will be in line with the local authority’s council tax reduction scheme. Tenants pay their own utility bills to encourage independent living. The Aims of Supported Housing Service are: Support individuals on their mental health recovery process Liaise with external professionals & agencies to work in partnership to offer holistic support. Promote independent living skills Empower people to achieve their goals Support & assist improving each tenant’s quality of life Promote social inclusion of people struggling with mental health conditions Click here to see more including eligibility
price: Free Organisation North Kent Mind Telephone 01322 291380 Email supportedhousing@northkentmind.co.uk Organisation North Kent Mind

title: Supported Studios
address: Rockets Blue room Studio, Phoenix Art Space, 10-14 Waterloo Place Brighton, Brighton, BN2 9NB    ​ What we do Join our Supported Studio to discover, develop and share your creativity with unique support. As an accredited day service provider with the Learning Disability day service, Brighton & Hove City Council we run dedicated Studio sessions for divergent, autistic people and people with a learning disability. Sessions are designed alongside each individual to support people to develop individual creative skills, knowledge of materials and confidence. We work closely with you to provide opportunities for you to learn, be ambitious and connect with other artists in our wide community. When are sessions? Supported Studio sessions run weekly, over 3 terms throughout the year. What does it cost? The supported Studio has 2 pathways: 1. Supported Studio Membership: £32 half day (10am – 12 or 1pm – 3pm) / £60 full day (10am – 3pm) 2. Supported Studio Bursary Space: £16 half day (10am  12 or 1pm – 3pm) Find out more and book your FREE studio taster session here.

title: SupportLine Email and Phone Support
address: Virtual What we do SupportLine provides a confidential telephone and email support to any individual on any issue. The Helpline is primarily a preventative service and aims to support people before they reach the point of crisis. It is particularly aimed at those who are socially isolated, vulnerable, at risk groups and victims of any form of abuse. SupportLine is open to any individual of any age. Calls are received from children, young adults and adults. SupportLine is able to take calls on a wide range of issues including relationships, child abuse, anger, bullying, eating disorders, exam stress, self harm, domestic violence, rape and sexual assault, disability, mental health, depression, anxiety, panic attacks, addictions, debt, phobias, conflict, sexuality, health concerns, homelessness, racial harassment, loneliness.
price: Free Organisation SupportLine Telephone 01708 765200 Email info@supportline.org.uk Organisation SupportLine

title: Sustain Community Foodbank
address: Tonbridge Baptist Church, Darenth Avenue, Tonbridge, TN10 3HZ What we do Sustain is a community food bank run by Tonbridge Baptist Church, providing emergency food supplies to local people experiencing a short term need. Emergency food parcels are available by referral from any front line care agency. All the food items are donated by individuals or local groups or purchased with donated funds. The individual can then collect the food parcel from Tonbridge Baptist Church between 12-1pm on Monday, Wednesday or Friday. Referrals made before 1pm can be collected on the same day. Please note, Sustain is not open on weekends or bank holidays.

title: Swallows Leisure Centre
address: Swallows Leisure Centre, Central Avenue, Sittingbourne, Kent, ME10 4NT What we do Swallows Leisure Centre is run by Swale Community Leisure . At Swallows Leisure Centre we offer a fantastic range of recently refurbished facilities suitable for all ages and abilities, including fully air-conditioned gyms split across three rooms including a dedicated eGYM area. Plus two swimming pools and flume, a versatile 6-court sports hall and fitness studios where we hold a variety of workout classes. title: Swimming and Exercise Group
address: Maidstone Leisure Centre, Mote Park, Maidstone, ME15 7RN What we do There are no formal lessons. Members are given assistance based on their individual needs. The Boom pool is for less confident/abled persons, the lane pool is for confident swimmers and the Aqua pool is for aerobic exercises. There is a hoist in both pool areas to assist those with physical disabilities to enter and exit the pool. For those who can swim unaided, the fitness pool is available to them to swim at their own leisure in an uncrowded environment. However, we have qualified teachers for those who wish to develop water skills or simply benefit from the freedom of moving in water. The Swimming and Exercise group runs on Tuesday from 12:00 pm to 13:00 pm. After the first FREE session, the classes cost £2 plus a £10 yearly membership.
price: Set charge Organisation Maidstone Swimming Club for the Disabled Telephone 07811298911 Email maidstoneswimdisabled@gmail.com Organisation Maidstone Swimming Club for the Disabled

title: Take Home and Settle Service (Maidstone Hospital)
address: Maidstone Hospital, Hermitage Lane, Maidstone, Kent, ME16 9QQ What we do Our service empowers older individuals who have visited Accident and Emergency at Pembury or Maidstone Hospital to return home instead of enduring prolonged waits for ambulances and potential hospital readmission’s. The service is free for patients being discharged from Maidstone & Pembury Hospital only. Catering to older residents in Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells, particularly those living alone or serving as caregivers, our service prioritises a safe discharge from the hospital. We offer support by accompanying patients home and ensuring they settle in safely. Additionally, we help by transporting clients’ equipment provided by occupational therapists, which may not be feasible in an ambulance. Moreover, we assist in picking up patient’s medications, further contributing to a seamless and stress-free transition from hospital to home. Our comprehensive service extends support to clients recently discharged from the hospital, encompassing a wide array of offerings: Safely transporting client’s home and ensuring their comfort upon arrival. Conducting a thorough risk assessment to enhance safety. Procuring any immediate shopping essentials. Preparing a comforting cup of tea or a snack as needed. Performing light housekeeping tasks, including tidying up and changing bed linen. Setting up and positioning any equipment received from the hospital. Aiding in medication management. Assisting with straightforward form-filling tasks. Supporting self-care and confidence-building endeavours. Providing guidance on accessing additional services through effective signposting. This free service is for residents of Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells who are aged 55 and over, especially those who live alone, are carers or have had a fall. This service is available Monday to Sunday, 7 days a week, from 1pm – 8pm (except Bank Holidays). For referrals, please ask the staff nurse to contact the Take Home and Settle Worker on duty.
price: Free Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Talking Therapies Referral Service
address: Virtual What we do Feeling low, isolated or lonely? You’re not alone. Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge has partnered with NHS Kent and Medway Talking Therapies to offer a dedicated referral service making it easier for you to access the support you need. We’ll guide you step-by-step through the Talking Therapies referral process, ensuring you feel supported throughout. What is Talking Therapies? Talking Therapies is a free NHS service that offers a range of evidence-based psychological treatments to help you better understand and manage your mood. This support may be helpful if you’re feeling: Anxious or overwhelmed Low or depressed Lonely or withdrawn Stuck and unsure where to turn Through therapy, you’ll gain practical tools and coping strategies to support your mental wellbeing, both now and in the future. Everyone has different needs, and therapy can be accessed in a variety of ways, including: Secure video sessions Text-based therapy Webinars Telephone appointments In-person sessions Support Whilst You Wait Waiting for therapy to begin can be difficult. As a part of this dedicated referral service, you’ll receive monthly check-ins from our team to see how you’re feeling and offer support. You can also access other Age UK MST services during this period, to help ensure you feel supported and connected whilst you wait for therapy sessions to begin. Ready to start? We’re here to help you take the first step. Get in touch with our team today, and we can begin your referral.
price: Free Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Taming Anxiety and Depression Course
address: Virtual What we do The course is based on CBT (Cognitive Behavioural Therapy) and ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, a newer – but also evidence-based! – type of CBT). ACT aims to change our relationship to difficult experiences such that they command less attention from us, thus enabling us to dedicate more time and energy to things that are valuable to us. The course explores: – your symptoms of depression and/or anxiety, and how they are maintained – what is important to you – what is standing in the way of you dedicating as much time and energy as you would like to what is important to you We will learn: – to build a balance of valuable activities, step by step – to manage difficult thoughts and emotions in such a way that they have less power over you, so you can engage in valuable activities and live the kind of life you want to live
price: Free Organisation We Are With You Telephone 0300 0120012 Email enquiries@wearewithyou.org.uk Organisation We Are With You

title: Taming Depression and Anxiety in Long-Term Conditions Online Course
address: Virtual What we do The course is based on CBT (Cognitive Behavioural Therapy) and ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, a newer – but also evidence-based! – type of CBT). ACT aims to change our relationship to difficult experiences which we cannot control such that they command less attention from us, thus enabling us to dedicate more time and energy to things that are valuable to us. The course explores: – your symptoms of depression and/or anxiety in the context of your long-term condition, and how they are maintained – what is important to you – what within your control is standing in the way of you dedicating as much time and energy as you would like to what is important to you We will learn: – to build a balance of valuable activities, step by step, considering your condition – to manage difficult thoughts and emotions in such a way that they have less power over you, so you can engage in valuable activities and live more of the kind of life you want to live

title: Teaching Assistant and Child-Minding Courses
address: Various locations What we do Introduction to Working with Children – explore the many different environments and settings available in the children’s sector and identify the qualifications necessary to work in these settings, including the progression routes. Duration: Course is 4 hours. Level 3 Combined Emergency First Aid at Work and Emergency Paediatric First Aid – provides the skills and knowledge needed to provide both emergency adult and paediatric first aid. This is aimed at people who are looking to work or volunteer in settings that require both first aiders and paediatric first aiders such as schools, sports clubs and other childcare settings. There are 6 practical assessments and a multiple-choice test. Duration: The course is a full day. Introduction to Healthy Eating / Healthy Eating on a Budget – Learners are introduced to the different aspects of nutrition and lifestyle that come together to create a healthy diet. Duration: Course is 4 hours. Level 2 Award in Food Allergen Awareness – This accredited qualification raises the awareness of characteristics and consequences of food allergies and intolerances. It looks at the procedures for identifying and controlling contamination from allergenic ingredients and procedures around communicating accurate information. Knowledge is tested by 15 multiple choice questions. Duration: Course is 4 hours.

address: Age UK Tunbridge Wells, Wood St, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2QS What we do Accessing the service is simple; just call and ask to speak to our Befriending Service Co-ordinator. Whether you are calling for yourself or on behalf of someone you know, you will need to complete a referral form and register with the service. Please ensure you have the persons permission before making a referral for somebody else.

title: The Bread and Butter Thing: Blackthorn Trust
phone: Text 0750 723 7311 to become a member // call 01622 602658 for support
address: Blackthorn Trust, St Andrews Road, Barming, Kent, ME16 9AN What we do The bread and butter thing – Blackthorn Trust hub. This hub location is open every Monday from 12.30pm – 1pm. You can get around £35 worth of food for £8.50 – this includes a bag of fresh fruit and veg, a bag of fridge favourites and a bag of cupboard staples. You need to become a member to use the hub but it’s free to sign-up and book a slot – there is no commitment and it’s free to join. To become a member follow these steps: Text 0750 723 7311 with your full name, postcode and the name of the hub you will be collecting from: “BLACKTHORN ” Select the size of order you want to receive: Family £8.50, individual £5 or Large Family £17. (There is a Vegetarian option available too) You’ll get a text every Monday to see if you want an order. Just reply “YES” by 10am the next day. Your order will be delivered to the hub on Wednesday at 2pm. Collect and pay for your goods from the BLACKTHORN hub. (Don’t forget – someone can collect on your behalf if you can’t make it and Healthy Start Vouchers are also accepted). If you have any questions please fill out our webform and we will contact you.

title: The Centrepoint Helpline
address: Virtual What we do Are you feeling overwhelmed about where to start with finding safe housing? If you’re in England and aged 16 to 25 , ​ call , message ​, WhatsApp or ​​chat live ​with our non-judgemental and experienced ​​​Helpline ​advisors to get advice on your housing situation and rights. They will listen, give you guidance and connect you to the right services to help you find a place to stay where you can close the door and feel safe. Our friendly​ advisors can also support you if ​​you’re worried about a young person who is homeless or facing homelessness. Call the Centrepoint Helpline: Call free on ​ 0808 800 0661 , Monday to Friday, 9am–5pm. ​​If you’d prefer to speak in a language other than English, let us know and we can get in touch with a translator for you within a few minutes of your call.​​​ ​​If you need help ​​​​outside these hours, ​​​​send us a message and one of our advisors will call you back within two days.​ Send a message to a Helpline advisor ​​​Send us a message and one of our Helpline advisors will phone or email you back within two working days. We’ll ask you to let us know a bit about what you want to discuss. Send us a message on WhatsApp: Message us via WhatsApp on 07557 255 965 , Monday to Friday, 9am-4.30pm.
price: Free Organisation Centrepoint Telephone 0808 800 0661 Email ask@centrepoint.org Organisation Centrepoint

title: At a Loss Bereavement Services
address: PO Box 824, CHICHESTER, PO19 7WW What we do Helping bereaved people find support & wellbeing . AtaLoss is a national charity providing easy and accurate signposting to services, information and resources for the bereaved. title: Family Matters Therapy
address: Various locations throughout Kent and Medway, remote sessions are also available. What we do Our therapy will help you begin to heal from the physical and mental scars that you have been left with as a result of the trauma. Research shows that individuals who receive therapy following sexual assault have improved recovery. This can lead to a reduction in your anxiety, depression and an increase in your quality of life. When you begin your therapy we will assess your needs to ensure your therapy is tailored to suit you. Throughout, our aim is: To provide a safe and confidential environment in which to explore the thoughts, feelings and behaviour affecting your life. To listen, offer support and help as you find a way through some of the difficulties you are experiencing and how they impact on your wellbeing. To help reduce the mental and physical distress you may be experiencing. To assist those faced with inflicting self-harm or suicidal thoughts. To guide you through a period of change, during which we will help you grow and replace old behaviour with new ways of thinking and self-discovery. How it works: Free of charge Up to 12 weekly sessions Face to face, online or telephone therapy Whether you have reported or not Open to all ages, genders
price: Free Organisation Family Matters Telephone 01474 536661 option 1 Email admin@familymattersuk.org Organisation Family Matters

title: Time for change
address: Medway What we do Our Time for Change group is a 12 week programme offering a 2 hour session based around forming positive relationships, recovery and making changes that benefit our lives. Our 2 hour sessions will then be followed up with a further 2 hours activity. This could be going for a coffee, having a group meal, going bowling or watching a film together. Our peer groups will rely on what the attendees need and their own ideas and suggestions, ensuring our peer support programme is entirely co-produced by ourselves and our attendees.

title: Time Out Group
address: Communigrow, East Malling, Communigrow, Kent, UK What we do Fridays 10am-12pm A free weekly session aimed at veterans, serving personnel and emergency services workers (retired or currently employed). At Communigrow we combine gardening with many practical and garden DIY projects such as constructing and repairing sheds/stores, building growing beds, maintaining our polytunnels, creating a sensory garden. These are projects the group can really get stuck into, working alongside others who have been through similar experiences while at the same time making a difference to the local community. Importantly – there will always be a cup of tea and somewhere tranquil to sit and chat or just sit in a safe and calming natural space. Please visit our website or contact us to find out more.

title: Time to Grow
address: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, West Malling, Kent, ME19 5NA What we do On Tuesday morning 9am until 12am. Please contact Spadework to check these sessions are running, as they are seasonal. Time to Grow is an eight week programme giving you the opportunity to enjoy our peaceful site, make meaningful connections with nature and work alongside other like-minded people. We know the benefits of a sustained and active interest in gardening include the following: Better physical health through exercise and learning how to use or strengthen muscles to improve mobility Improved mental health through a sense of purpose and achievement Reduced feelings of isolation The opportunity to connect with others – reducing feelings of isolation or exclusion Acquiring new skills to improve the chances of finding employment Just feeling better for being outside, in touch with nature and in the ‘great outdoors’

We are encouraging mums (or someone on their behalf) to come and collect Totcycle items from one of our Hubs when they are open. We are pre-booking Totcycle appointments wherever possible, to ensure everyone can be seen. Appointments can be booked by email, phone, text or WhatsApp using the contact details below. We can still arrange delivery of bulky items, or deliver to mums who can’t get to us and have no-one who can collect on their behalf. Price Free

title: Touch Base Project
address: Saphir House, 5, Jubilee Way, Faversham, ME13 8GD What we do The Touch Base Project is a crisis alternatives service for independent adults with a diagnosis of, or awaiting assessment for Autism or Asperger’s, living in Kent and Medway. Self-advocacy support is provided for 6 weeks through 1:1 sessions with an advocate either face-to-face in the community or remotely (via Zoom or telephone). Support is tailored to the needs of the individual and can include signposting, peer support groups, information/access to resources, and support accessing community interest groups and societies. title: Transport Service (Maidstone and Malling)
address: Registered address: Involve Kent, 30, Turkey Court, Maidstone, ME14 5PP What we do Involve Volunteer Transport offers transport to those who are unable to use public transport or have use of their own car due to age, frailty, disability or injury and are based in the Maidstone & Malling area. We use volunteer drivers that use their own vehicles and charge 50 pence per mile which includes the driver’s personal mileage. We also charge a booking fee which ranges from £2.50 – £10, depending on type of journey. We are very excited to be able to offer our new wheelchair accessible vehicle to people from September 2025. You must be able to mobilise the wheelchair or scooter safely up the ramp unaided and safely down the ramp unaided. We can offer drivers who are happy to drive to London Hospitals and out of county appointments. We require a minimum of 2 weeks’ notice due to the demand for this service.

title: TT time – Community Project
address: The forum shopping center, High street, sittingbourne, ME10 3DL What we do TT Time is a 12-week programme based in Swale, meeting once per week, designed to support women and families during key stages of the breastfeeding journey and early childhood nutrition. This holistic initiative provides practical guidance, emotional support, and opportunities for creative and social connection. The programme also offers a dedicated workshop and exercise space, hosting activities such as sound baths, sister circles, and creative expression workshops. With sessions accommodating 10–15 families, participants benefit from an intimate and personalised experience. These sessions take place on a Monday from 11:00am- 14:00pm. Contact Philly to find out more!

title: Tunbridge Wells Repair Café
address: Trinity Theatre, Church Rd, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 1JP What we do The repair cafe aims to provide a place where people can bring items that no longer work or need repairing where they can be repaired. The overall goal is to prolong the life of existing items that might otherwise have been thrown away. The net effect of this is to reduce the need to buy new items, reduce landfill and reduce the subsequent carbon impact on the environment. Secondly the cafe is a lovely social environment that allows those with skills to help others.

title: Tunbridge Wells Safe Haven MHM
address: 69 Calverley Road, Tunbridge Wells, TN1 2UY What we do This is a walk-in service – there’s no need to be referred. The Tunbridge Wells Safe Haven offers a welcoming, safe, comfortable, non-judgmental and non-clinical environment, sometimes known as ‘crisis cafes’. At the haven, we will be able to provide information and emotional support if you are in crisis or feel you are heading towards a crisis situation. You can come just for a chat, or to access one-to-one emotional support from trained mental health professionals. We also offer help in creating staying well and crisis plans, and support visitors to access other services and organisations that may be useful to you. Opening hours: Monday to Sunday, 6pm-11pm.

title: Ukrainian Support Helpline
address: Virtual What we do The ongoing war and violence in Ukraine has caused families to forcibly leave their homes, in search of safety, with many arriving to the UK. Call our free helpline on: 0800 148 8586 During this very distressing and uncertain time, children and adults will need continued support. In response to this, following discussion with the Association of Ukrainians in Great Britain, we have set up the Ukrainian Support Helpline to provide a holistic support service. The Helpline is available to anyone fleeing the war in Ukraine. Get in touch if you need support with: Therapy with a qualified psychotherapist – delivered via the phone or online, with access to interpreters if required. Therapeutic support is available to children and young people up to and including age 21 years. Advice on a range of issues e.g., housing, accessing key health services, education, employment and more via our trained helpline support workers Practical support – access to digital devices to ensure families stay connected to loved ones during this worrying time, as well as stimulating toys for children, vital baby items and more (subject to demand) All services include access to interpreters in Ukrainian and Russian. The Helpline is open: Monday – Friday 10.00am-8.00pm Saturday 10.00am-3.00pm You can freephone us on: 0800 148 8586 You can email us at: ukrainiansupport@barnardos.org.uk You can find more information on our website here.
price: Free Organisation Barnardo's Telephone 0800 148 8586 Email ukrainiansupport@barnardos.org.uk Organisation Barnardo's

The Helpline is open: Monday – Friday 10.00am-8.00pm Saturday 10.00am-3.00pm You can freephone us on: 0800 148 8586 You can email us at: ukrainiansupport@barnardos.org.uk You can find more information on our website here. The ongoing war and violence in Ukraine has caused families to forcibly leave their homes, in search of safety, with many arriving to the UK. Call our free helpline on: 0800 148 8586 During this very distressing and uncertain time, children and adults will need continued support. In response to this, following discussion with the Association of Ukrainians in Great Britain, we have set up the Ukrainian Support Helpline to provide a holistic support service. The Helpline is available to anyone fleeing the war in Ukraine. Get in touch if you need support with: All services include access to interpreters in Ukrainian and Russian. The Helpline is open: You can freephone us on: 0800 148 8586 You can email us at: ukrainiansupport@barnardos.org.uk You can find more information on our website here. Price Free
categories: Advocacy and Information, advice and guidance; Helplines; Support Services and Self-help

title: Urgent Response Service
address: Registered Address: The Oast, 170, Tonbridge Road, Wateringbury, ME18 5NS What we do Crossroads are commissioned by GP’s and Social Services to provide a rapid response service, to help prevent the breakdown of a caring situation that might otherwise lead to a hospital or care home admission. Currently referrals can only come through Health or Social Care professionals, but if you feel you need this service, please ask a health or social care professional to refer you.

title: Virtual Book Club
address: Virtual What we do We’ve set up a virtual book group (via Zoom- held on the 1st Tuesday each month at 7pm) to boost companionship, friendship, and sanity through stories. You don’t need to be well read or clever, and whether you’re curious about the classics, contemporary books or are a reading virgin, everyone is welcome. In a nutshell, we’d love to see you there! Email to express your interest. There is no set reading list for this inaugural meeting but please bring along, if you wish a passage or two that you’ve read that made you chuckle.

title: Volunteer Transport Scheme Dartford
address: HLC Dartford (Healthy Living Centre Dartford), Tree Community Centre, Cedar Road, Dartford, Kent, DA1 2RS What we do A volunteer-led transport service for older people in the Borough of Dartford, to enable them to access health-related services. Eligibility Criteria To access this service, clients must meet the following requirements: 50 years or older. Must reside within the Dartford Borough. Unable to access standard public transport (due to health issues, disability, or location). The scheme is ideally for those booking regular journeys rather than a one-off trips to make it as cost effective as possible. Drivers are not permitted to enter the client’s home, so clients requiring extra assistance (including those with dementia or Alzheimer’s) must arrange for a companion to accompany them. Membership & Fees Annual Membership (2026/27) = £20.00 (Subsidised) Annual Renewal (2027/28 onwards) – £40.00 Each booking will also attract a £2.00 administration charge, which contributes towards the running costs and sustainability of the service. This scheme is supported by Dartford Borough Council, funded through the UK Shared Prosperity Fund and delivered by Healthy Living Centre Dartford.

title: Volunteering Courses
description: Confidence into Volunteering – For those who are not yet ready for their first volunteering role, this course will give you the confidence to take a step towards your goal. It looks at what confidence means to you and how you recognise confidence in others. You will learn how to utilise positive self-talk and attitude to help you change self-limiting beliefs. Course is 3 to 6 hours. Skills for Volunteers – This course looks at the benefits of volunteering transferable skills and overcoming barriers, rights and responsibilities, body language and training. It is ideal for those ready for their first role in volunteering. Course is 3 to 6 hours. Level 1 Introduction to Customer Service – This course provides an introduction to the skills and personal qualities required for careers and job opportunities within the customer service sector. It will look at dealing with the face-to-face and on the phone, as well as dealing with queries and complaints. Course is 6 hours or two sessions of 3 hours and is also available as an accredited course by Laser Awards. Communication and Listening Skills for Volunteers – You will explore communication skills and techniques, understand issues that impact communication in different types of situations and assess strategies for personal development and resilience. Course is 4 hours. Level 3 Emergency First Aid at Work – This qualification is delivered through hands-on learning, practical teaching, demonstrations and a theory test. An Emergency First Aid at Work certificate will be issued upon successful completion of the course which is valid for three years. The course is a full day. title: Walking Group
address: Registered Address Only: 63, Betenson Avenue, Sevenoaks, TN13 3ER What we do Every Step Counts is a gentle, supportive walking group which run 8 walks in the Sevenoaks district. Our walks are aimed at people who would like to walk, chat and meet new people at the same time as improving or maintaining their fitness. We walk at the speed and for the length that suits each individual. Most walks last 20 minutes but there is always an option to do more or less for those who wish to and are able. After the walk there is always an opportunity to have refreshments in a nearby café and to enjoy the company of the group. It doesn’t matter if you can only manage a short distance, are recovering from an operation, are trying to lose weight or need someone to encourage you to exercise. Every Step Counts. (Please note the below address is the registered address only). For more information including the meeting point of the walking group, please contact us using the details below).

title: CAP Weald Life Skills Course
address: St Mary the Virgin, Church, Back Lane, Goudhurst, Cranbrook, UK What we do Learn practical tools to help you live well on a low income every day. Life skills is a friendly group that will give you the confidence and decision-making skills needed to live on a low income. You’ll learn practical money saving techniques, such as cooking on a budget and making money go further. What to expect from the group: Community – We know that when it comes to saving money and making lifestyle changes, there’s so much we can learn from each other. Everyone finds dealing with the pressures of life a stressful and lonely challenge at times. That’s why life skills is designed as a community group, where everyone gets together to share practical skills in the areas of life that are impacted by our income, such as shopping on a budget and looking after our wellbeing and mental health. Course – Life skills usually runs over eight sessions. The group is designed to equip you with skills for every day life, including top tips for dealing with life’s pressures and navigating life on a low income. The sessions happen in a relaxed group environment, with a mixture of practical activities and informal discussions. Coaching – You’ll have the opportunity to benefit from informal one-to-one support from a trained Money Coach, who will help you to think through your individual spending choices, and create a simple budget that works for you. This will help you gain better control over your money, lightening the pressures money can bring and building your confidence and self-esteem in managing your finances. Weald Life skills group is run in partnership with: St Mary’s Church, Goudhurst Contact your local coach for more information: Alison Williams / Life skills coach / alisonwilliams@caplifeskills.org
price: Free Organisation Christians Against Poverty Email alisonwilliams@caplifeskills.org Organisation Christians Against Poverty

title: Weekend Wellness – Forest Bathing in Hothfield Heathlands
address: Hothfield Healthlands What we do Soothe your body and mind on a guided, meditative walk through the woods inspired by the Japanese practice of Forest Bathing, Shinrin Yoku. These forest bathing events encourage you to take your experience in nature to a new level. Each session lasts 2 hours and runs every couple of months, offering a seasonal opportunity to reconnect with the natural world. Inspired by the Japanese practice of Shinrin Yoku, this meditative walk supports your wellbeing by helping you switch off and immerse yourself in nature. Evidence suggests that practices of this style can boost your immune system, reduce stress, and improve sleep. This is an invitation to simply ‘be’ in nature and engage with your surroundings through your five senses. You’ll be offered a series of gentle, meditative prompts none of which are compulsory allowing you to choose what feels right for you in each moment. Find out more on the website.

This is an invitation to simply ‘be’ in nature and engage with your surroundings through your five senses. You’ll be offered a series of gentle, meditative prompts none of which are compulsory allowing you to choose what feels right for you in each moment. Find out more on the website. Price £18
categories: Animals and nature; Groups and Community Activities; Hobbies and interests

title: Wellbeing Courses
address: Various locations What we do Five Ways to Wellbeing – Learners will be introduced to the 5 Ways to Wellbeing steps as practical ways to manage wellbeing, stress & anxiety. You will learn the background knowledge on the five steps, how they were created and how understanding each step in turn can benefit our wellbeing. Course is 2 hours. Positive Thoughts, Feelings and Behaviours – This course introduces learners to the concept of thoughts, feelings and behaviours. Learners will gain an understanding of how TFBs are linked to situational triggers; positive and negative cycles and how they link to Cognitive Behavioural Therapy. Course is 2 days of 3 hours. Confidence and Self-esteem – This course can be tailored for groups and will contain some or all of the following topics: awareness of the ‘inner critic’; replacing blame with praise; strategies for working with negative emotions; body consciousness; self-care; making an impression; finding a voice; speaking out, being heard and being a good listener. Course is 2 days of 3 hours. Mindful Living – Explore the reasons behind Mindfulness and try out some practical activities to explore mindful techniques. How do we use mindful language? How can mindfulness help you in your everyday life? You will be guided through practical exercises to explore relaxation. Course is 2 hours. Introduction to Mental Health Support – This course is designed to help learners understand why anyone can be susceptible to mental health challenges. Learners will gain a wider understanding of positive and negative mental health, related signs and symptoms and how they can help themselves and others. Course is 4 hours. Introduction to Wellbeing by Managing Stress and Anxiety – This introduction will assist learners in understanding more about the physical and psychological symptoms of anxiety and stress and the practical ways to manage them. You will receive practical experience of self-help relaxation techniques and how to boost resilience to cope with challenges. Course is 2 days of 3 hours. Relax and Reflect: Be Creative with Crafts – Arts and crafts take many forms. On these courses you can try a variety of seasonal arts and crafts and have the opportunity for some relaxing ‘me’ time. Courses are 2-4 hours. Introduction to Fitness – Learners will be introduced to a simple exercise routine to aid Wellbeing. +D13You will discuss the benefits of exercise and stretching and preparing for exercise. Routines can be adapted according to learner needs and ability. You can reflect on the effectiveness of each session at the end. Courses 4 x 1.5 hour. Introduction to Seated Exercise – This is a simple exercise programme designed to be undertaken while seated. It can be as energetic as Zumba or as relaxed as Tai Chi. Routines can be adapted for individual ability. Courses 4 x 1.5 hour. Introduction to Healthy Eating – Learners are introduced to the different aspects of nutrition and lifestyle that come together to create a healthy diet. It looks at how we define a healthy diet and why we want to aspire to be healthy. You will look at the eat well plate, vitamins and minerals, meal planning, portion sizes and calories. Where facilities allow, learners can make a light lunch. Course is 4 hours.

title: Wellbeing Services in Dartford & Bexley
address: Evergreen Care UK, 39 Broad Lane, Wilmington, Kent, DA2 7AQ What we do Our Wellbeing Service is here to support your independence and help you stay confident, connected, and comfortable at home. We know that even small things can make a big difference. A regular visit from a trusted Home Support Worker can offer a helpful hand, a friendly face, and someone who understands what matters to you. Whether it’s making sure the house feels just right, checking in on your general health, or simply enjoying a conversation over a cuppa, we’re here for whatever you need. This service is about more than tasks; it’s about your whole wellbeing. We support: Feeling physically well, eating and sleeping comfortably, staying active, and managing health. Staying mentally and emotionally balanced through companionship, conversation, and meaningful moments. Living in a home that supports you, is safe, warm, and just how you like it. Finding meaning and purpose in your day-to-day life, your community, or your spiritual journey. There’s no one-size-fits-all approach to wellbeing. That’s why we take time to get to know you, listen to what’s important, and offer support that fits into your life, not the other way around.
price: Charges apply Organisation Evergreen Care UK Telephone 01322431765 Email julia@evergreencareuk.org Organisation Evergreen Care UK

title: AA Meetings
address: https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/AA-Meetings/Find-a-Meeting What we do To search for a meeting you can use any of the search systems on the Find a Meeting page ( https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/find-a-meeting/ ). Not all groups have provided post codes, if you can’t find a group near you using the post code search you might have more success using one of the other methods. Note that in the town search areas of London are preceded by the word London, e.g. for Hackney type in LONDON HACKNEY. Online meetings may be found by searching for the word: online The meeting start time is always indicated and meetings are usually 90 minutes unless a finish time is stipulated. Some meetings may be ‘open’ (to non-alcoholic visitors) or ‘closed’ (for alcoholics & those who think they might have a drink problem) – some meetings give information on open and closed meetings. Please do not use our national helpline number for meeting or general information – this is solely for those people who need to speak to a member of AA about their drinking. To find a meeting please visit: https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/AA-Meetings/Find-a-Meeting
price: Free Organisation Alcoholics Anonymous Email help@aamail.org Organisation Alcoholics Anonymous

To find a meeting please visit: https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/AA-Meetings/Find-a-Meeting https://www.alcoholics-anonymous.org.uk/AA-Meetings/Find-a-Meeting Price Free

title: West Kingsdown Lunch Club
address: St. Edmund's CE Primary School, Fawkham Road, West Kingsdown, TN15 6JP What we do West Kingsdown Lunch Club is a friendly and welcoming lunch club for older people living in West Kingsdown. The Club meets every month on the third Thursday, from 12pm to 2pm, at St Edmunds Primary School. Residents can enjoy a delicious and nutritious meal free of charge, while spending time socialising in a relaxed and supportive setting. Alongside the lunch, the Club regularly welcomes speakers from local organisations who provide information about services and support available to older people. The programme also offers a variety of enjoyable and interactive activities throughout the year, including indoor bingo, reminiscence and memory sessions, seed planting, chair-based exercise, painting and musical memories. When? Third Tuesday of the month from 12pm to 2pm.
