title: CAP West Malling Life Skills Course
address: West Malling Baptist Church, Swan Street, West Malling, UK What we do Learn practical tools to help you live well on a low income every day. Life skills is a friendly group that will give you the confidence and decision-making skills needed to live on a low income. You’ll learn practical money saving techniques, such as cooking on a budget and making money go further. What to expect from the group: Community – We know that when it comes to saving money and making lifestyle changes, there’s so much we can learn from each other. Everyone finds dealing with the pressures of life a stressful and lonely challenge at times. That’s why life skills is designed as a community group, where everyone gets together to share practical skills in the areas of life that are impacted by our income, such as shopping on a budget and looking after our wellbeing and mental health. Course – Life skills usually runs over eight sessions. The group is designed to equip you with skills for every day life, including top tips for dealing with life’s pressures and navigating life on a low income. The sessions happen in a relaxed group environment, with a mixture of practical activities and informal discussions. Coaching – You’ll have the opportunity to benefit from informal one-to-one support from a trained Money Coach, who will help you to think through your individual spending choices, and create a simple budget that works for you. This will help you gain better control over your money, lightening the pressures money can bring and building your confidence and self-esteem in managing your finances. West Malling group is run in partnership with: West Malling Baptist Church. Contact your local coach for more information: John Gledhill / Life skills coach / johngledhill@caplifeskills.org
price: Free Organisation Christians Against Poverty Email johngledhill@caplifeskills.org Organisation Christians Against Poverty

title: Wheelchair Service (Maidstone)
address: Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge, Dairy Court, Fairview Farm, Linton Road, Maidstone, Kent, ME15 0AL What we do If you have an older friend or family member visiting, or someone who may need a bit of support with mobility, consider stopping by one of our centres—either at Dairy Court Day Centre in Maidstone or Hollybush Day Centre in Sevenoaks. We have wheelchairs available for rent. Whether it’s just for a couple of hours or up to four weeks, our wheelchair rental service provides flexible options to meet your needs. A £50 returnable deposit is required when hiring a wheelchair. This is to cover any loss or damage occurred to the wheelchair whilst it is hired. Please note although we can take a card payment for the hire of the wheelchair we cannot take a card payment for the £50.00 deposit. This must be either cash or cheque.
price: 1 Day - £3 Weekend - £10 1 week - £13 Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge Telephone 01622 753618 Email info@ageukmst.org.uk Organisation Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge

title: Wheeleasy Mobility
address: Wheeleasy Mobility CIC, The Cabins, Hermitage Court, Maidstone, Kent, ME169NT What we do Short term provision of Wheelchairs and Mobility aids Wheeleasy Mobility support people thrown into crisis by accident, operation and devastating diagnosis, by immediately supplying Wheelchairs and Mobility aids to ensure clients and families are able to continue to get out and about. We offer wheelchairs from age 5 13 inch up to Bariatric 25 stone all with elevators if required. These can be loaned on a 4 weekly contract for a small fee (this can be renewed after 4 weeks). For those who need wheelchairs or mobility aids for longer use – we accept donated wheelchairs and other mobility aids from the public which we refurbish and sell for a small fee to people in the community needing one long term. We are a drop off point for crutches and mobility aids for MTW hospital. We also run a Wheelchair clinic for anyone who needs a wheelchair “MOT”. We are open: 10:30am to 1pm on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Fridays 1 .30pm to 4pm on Thursdays 10am to 12.30pm on Saturdays Visit our website or contact us for more information.
price: Donation/small fee Organisation Wheeleasy Mobility CIC Telephone 07365 891 388 Email info@wemobility.co.uk Organisation Wheeleasy Mobility CIC

title: Why Weight – 12 Week Group Weight Management Service
address: Sevenoaks District Council, One You Sevenoaks, Health Hub, Argyle Road, Sevenoaks, TN13 1HG What we do The ONE YOU service provides free weight loss programmes that can support individuals to lose weight and maintain a healthy weight for life. It covers information on portion control, food labelling, physical activity, behaviour change and healthy nutritional advice. We offer informative and interactive weekly group sessions to give you the skills and information to take control of your own weight loss. This service is aimed at overweight adults with a Body Mass index (BMI) of 30-40 with up to one chronic condition (27.5+ for residents from BAME communities). We provide this service in various locations as well as online via group meeting platforms. The scheme offers ongoing weight checks after 12 weeks for up to 12 months, for continued weight loss and weight maintenance. The service accepts self-referrals using the link: https://www.kent.gov.uk/social-care-and-health/health/one-you-kent/healthy-weight GPs and other health care professional can send referrals using the form embedded on GP systems such as DORIS and DXS (and also attached) which can be safely sent to: sdc.healthylivingteam@nhs.net
price: Free Organisation One You Sevenoaks Telephone 01732 227 000 Email sdc.healthylivingteam@nhs.net Organisation One You Sevenoaks

title: Wilder Wellbeing Programme
address: Various What we do Our 6-week programmes are free and designed for individuals who may be experiencing a lowered wellbeing, living with mental or physical health conditions, or are feeling lonely or isolated. Participants can be referred to these sessions by a social prescriber or can self-refer through our website. Programmes are run on an ad-hoc basis in line with funding, but we try to ensure that there are programmes evenly spread across the county. You can find an up to date list of our current wellbeing programmes here: https://www.kentwildlifetrust.org.uk/whats-on/wilder-wellbeing When attending, please bring any refreshments you may want and wear suitable attire for the weather. Facilities and accessibility of the site can be checked on our reserve pages (which will be linked from the programme information), or a member of the Wilder Wellbeing team can answer any questions you may have.
price: Free Organisation Kent Wildlife Trust Telephone 01622 662012 Email wilderwellbeing@kentwildlife.org.uk Organisation Kent Wildlife Trust

title: Wishing Hearts Charity Activities
address: 92 Florence Road, Maidstone, ME16 8EN What we do We organise social activities and events such as bi-weekly coffee mornings, tea parties, trips and afternoon teas to combat loneliness and bring people together. We also organise ‘wish experiences’ in which we grant one of a kind wishes for individuals that are experiencing barriers such as loneliness, mental health problems, health conditions etc. Most of our work is targeted towards people that are aged 65 and over. Contact us for more information about our services.

title: WomenKind
address: Kent wide What we do WomenKind is our proven empowerment and development programme for Kent and Medway women. For the past 15 years our team has been empowering, training and encouraging women to overcome barriers and challenges in order to achieve their goals and ambitions. Whether looking to get into work, wanting to start a new business, wanting to take on a personal challenge or getting into volunteering, our team can help make it happen. We will be applying for funding from the National Lottery Community Fund and hope to be able to run WomenKind again. Please sign up to our newsletter and we will keep you in the loop.

title: Bipolar UK Women’s Support Group online
address: Virtual What we do Where: Online When: 2nd and 4th Monday of the month (except when this falls on a bank holiday) Time: 12.30pm – 1.30pm Register for free here. Our support groups are free and open to anyone identifying as a woman affected by bipolar, including family, friends and loved ones. If you don’t have a confirmed diagnosis and want to find out about bipolar, and related conditions such as cyclothymia, you are also welcome. Anyone over 18 is welcome. If you’re 16 or 17, please bring a parent or guardian. The format of the meetings vary, but you will have the opportunity to introduce yourself, say how you are feeling using the Bipolar UK mood scale and may listen to an invited speaker. The groups are organised and facilitated by volunteers who are affected by bipolar in some way. If you would like to join the next support meeting, please book a free ticket here. You will receive the link to join via email after booking a ticket. If you have any questions please email: supportgroups@bipolaruk.org We look forward to seeing you soon.
price: Free Organisation Bipolar UK Telephone 0333 323 3880 Email supportgroups@bipolaruk.org Organisation Bipolar UK

title: Kent Autistic Trust Women’s Support Group
address: Virtual What we do Our Autistic women (or those who identify as Autistic) online support groups are for women to have the opportunity to meet others who share similar difficulties, share ideas, advice and problems, talk to others about the issues they face on a daily basis with others who understand. Unfortunately we are unable to offer this group to people who have support through the Learning Disability team within Social Services or deemed to have a Learning Disability (please be aware that this isn’t the same as an education need). For more information or to join our support group, please contact Emilymay Miles.

title: Women’s Walking Football
address: St Augustines Academy, Maidstone ME16 8AE What we do Walking football is a growing sport and offers a multitude of health benefits to older people such as reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and stroke while improving blood pressure. Positive changes in postural balance, blood pressure and resting heart rate, lowered cholesterol, improved blood sugar levels, bone density, and improving reactions, whilst slashing the odds of suffering from type 2 diabetes – all indicators of general good health. We run walking football programmes for women (aged 40 upwards) and cater for all levels of football ability – if you are interested in joining one of our programmes you can simply turn up and have a chat. You can register your interest on the website , or call on 07904 992474 or send an email to walkingfootball@stonesinthecommunity.org When? Fridays at 7pm Where? St Augustines Academy, Maidstone ME16 8AE

title: Woodwork for Wellbeing
address: Spadework, Teston Road, Offham, ME19 5NA What we do Woodwork for Wellbeing (affiliated to Kent Sheds) When : Thursday from 9am-12pm Spadework’s five-acre site is a peaceful and joyful space in which adults with any level of woodworking experience can spend time in a small friendly group. At Spadework we know the benefits of meaningful occupation and acquiring new skills and want to make this available to others and recognise there are these additional benefits: Better physical health through exercise and learning how to use or strengthen muscles to improve mobility Improved mental health through a sense of purpose and achievement The opportunity to connect with others – reducing feelings of isolation or exclusion Acquiring new skills to improve the chances of finding employment Just feeling better for being in closer touch with nature. Please contact us if you are interested in joining.

title: Workshops for Parents and Carers
address: Virtual What we do Our free workshops help parents and carers gain the knowledge and skills they need to support their children after child sexual abuse. We provide a programme of training workshops to help you to navigate this difficult time, providing useful information to support you and your family to manage, move forwards, and hopefully go on to thrive. Support for you We know that the period after a disclosure of child sexual abuse can be a difficult time for parents and carers. It can be hard to know what to do or how to respond. Our training workshops can help you to support your child and regain confidence in yourself and your parenting. Through a mixture of 1:1 support, group work and self-learning our programme of training workshops covers the following topics, and more: What is abuse? Understanding the impact of trauma on the brain Having difficult conversations Therapeutic parenting Self care Staying safe online You can find more information about our workshops and how they work in our FAQs document on our website (https://westand.org.uk/training-for-parents) To access our workshops please call our free national helpline on 0800 980 1958
price: Free Organisation We Stand Telephone 0800 980 1958 Email support@westand.org.uk Organisation We Stand

title: Writing for Wellbeing Workshop (Swanscombe)
address: Upstairs at The Old Fire Station, Church Road, Swanscombe, Kent, DA10 0HF What we do Unlock Your Inner Voice: Writing for Wellbeing Workshop Series Join us at the Swanscombe & Greenhithe Community Hub for a series of transformative Writing for Wellbeing workshops – where words become a powerful tool for healing, connection, and self-discovery. Whether you’re looking to process emotions, boost mental health, or find clarity, this safe and inspiring space is open to all 18+. Discover the incredible emotional and mental health benefits of expressive writing in a supportive community setting. No previous experience needed—just bring your voice and willingness to explore. When: We meet once a month on Mondays at 10:00am – 11:30am Where: Stone Pavilion, Hayes Road Stone, Greenhithe, DA9 9DS Contact us or visit our website to find out more.

title: Yoga4Health Programme
address: https://www.yogainhealthcarealliance.com/yoga-for-health-program/ What we do The Yoga4Health programme is a 10-week programme that has been designed by experts at the Yoga in Healthcare to help patients improve their physical and psychological wellbeing, specifically supporting individuals who: suffer from stress or anxiety or mild depression, need to lose weight, need to improve heart health, need to lower risk of Type 2 diabetes or just need help to feel well, healthy and supported. The Yoga4Health programme is endorsed by the NHS, supported by the College of Medicine and is being delivered successfully across the UK. If you would like to be referred to the programme please email contactyoga4health@gmail.com or ask you GP or Social Prescribing Link Worker.

title: Young Onset Peer Support Group
address: Various in Gravesend & Dartford What we do Our Young Onset Activity Group– Kindred Spirits, is for people diagnosed with dementia under the age of 65. Their carers, family and friends are also welcomed. People living with young-onset dementia can experience different challenges to older people living with the diagnosis. They may have different symptoms which can include changes in personality, difficulties with vision and language as well as memory loss. The diagnosis can be harder to accept for younger people as it is rare at their age. They can experience other age-related difficulties due to still being in work, having younger children and having other responsibilities such as caring for their own parents. People benefit from the opportunity to share their experiences, tips and advice. They also benefit from forming new friendships. Please contact our Wellbeing Team on 01474 533990, email info@adss.org.uk or visit www.adss.org.uk for more information. This is a free service however donations are welcomed to sustain the groups.

title: Young Roots
address: Communigrow Centre, Park Farm, East Malling/Ditton, ME20 6PE What we do Young Roots is designed for young adults seeking work experience in a supportive environment. It is aimed at young adults currently 17 to 30 years of age who would have difficulty attending work experience due to neurodiversity, communication skills, anxiety or mental health issues. The program focuses on developing skills in horticulture and construction, with an emphasis on work skills, practical experience, personal development, and British values, while embedding English and maths in a subtle, integrated way. This is a small group within a supported environment. Many participants from Young Roots have gone on to further education or continued their involvement through volunteering either. Please visit our website or contact us to find out more.

title: Your Home Project (Sevenoaks)
address: 5 Bradford Street, Tonbridge  TN9 1DU What we do Welcome to the “Your Home” project, a collaborative initiative between Age UK Maidstone, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge and Sevenoaks District Council. We specialise in enhancing health through housing, offering support with adaptations, aids, and equipment to ensure your living space meets your needs. It might mean grab rails, moving beds, deep cleans and de-cluttering where it might prevent you being admitted to hospital or make it easier for you to be discharged from hospital. Our staff will carry out an “Environmental Home Assessment”. This is similar to the sort of assessment that might be carried out by an Occupational Therapist from Social Services. Once we have identified what adaptations could be made, we will work closely with Social Services Occupational Therapists and the council’s Housing Department (where appropriate) to carry out the adaptations. If you need help paying for them then we will help you to apply for any grants which are available. For further information please contact for the Sevenoaks District – James Keast. Tel: 07864 959141 Email: jameskeast@ageukmst.org.uk
